title.en: Volume 21: Treatise on Rhythm and the Calendar
title.raw: 卷二十一 律曆志 Volume 21: Treatise on Rhythm and the Calendar

sentences.zh: 《虞書》曰「乃同律度量衡」，所以齊遠近立民信也。

translations.literal: The *Book of Yu* says, "Then he unified pitch-pipes, measures of length, capacity, and weight," by which distant and near were made uniform and the people's trust established.
translations.idiomatic: As the *Book of Yu* says, "He standardized pitch, measures, capacity, and weights," thereby creating uniform standards across near and far and securing public trust.

sentences.zh: 自伏戲畫八卦，由數起，至黃帝、堯、舜而大備。

translations.literal: From when Fuxi drew the Eight Trigrams, numbers began; by the time of the Yellow Emperor, Yao, and Shun, they were greatly complete.
translations.idiomatic: Numerical principles began with Fuxi's Eight Trigrams and reached full development by the age of the Yellow Emperor, Yao, and Shun.

sentences.zh: 三代稽古，法度章焉。

translations.literal: The Three Dynasties examined antiquity, and standards and regulations became clear therefrom.
translations.idiomatic: In the Three Dynasties, careful study of antiquity made legal and technical standards explicit.

sentences.zh: 周衰官失，孔子陳後王之法，曰：「謹權量，審法度，修廢官，舉逸民，四方之政行矣。」

translations.literal: When Zhou declined and offices were lost, Confucius set forth the methods of later kings, saying: "Be careful with weights and measures, examine standards and regulations, restore abolished offices, promote the overlooked worthy, and governance in the four directions will be carried out."
translations.idiomatic: As Zhou authority decayed and institutions broke down, Confucius articulated the model of later sage-kings: "Standardize weights and measures with care, scrutinize legal norms, restore defunct offices, and elevate neglected talent; then government will function throughout the realm."

sentences.zh: 漢興，北平侯張蒼首律曆事，孝武帝時樂官考正。

translations.literal: After Han arose, Marquis of Beiping Zhang Cang first headed matters of pitch-pipes and calendar; in Emperor Wu's time the music officials examined and corrected them.
translations.idiomatic: After the Han founding, the Marquis of Beiping, Zhang Cang, first took charge of pitch and calendrical administration; under Emperor Wu, court music officials reviewed and corrected the system.

sentences.zh: 至元始中王莽秉政，欲燿名譽，徵天下通知鐘律者百餘餘人，使羲和劉歆等典領條奏，言之最詳。

translations.literal: By the Yuanshi period, Wang Mang held power and wished to display reputation and fame; he summoned more than one hundred people throughout the realm who understood bells and pitch-laws, and had Xihe Liu Xin and others manage and submit itemized memorials, whose discussions were the most detailed.
translations.idiomatic: By the Yuanshi era, when Wang Mang controlled the government, he sought prestige by convening more than a hundred specialists in bell acoustics and pitch standards from across the empire. He ordered Liu Xin, in his capacity as Xihe, and others to oversee formal memorials, producing the most detailed discussions on the subject.

sentences.zh: 故刪其偽辭，取正義，著于篇。

translations.literal: Therefore I deleted their false wording, selected correct meaning, and recorded it in this chapter.
translations.idiomatic: So I have cut away spurious claims, retained what is sound, and set it down here.

sentences.zh: 一曰備數，二曰和聲，三曰審度，四曰嘉量，五曰權衡。

translations.literal: First is Completing Numbers; second is Harmonizing Sounds; third is Examining Measures; fourth is Standardizing Capacity; fifth is Weights and Balances.
translations.idiomatic: The treatise has five parts: numeration, musical tuning, linear measure, capacity measure, and weight standards.

sentences.zh: 參五以變，錯綜其數，稽之於古今，效之於氣物，和之於心耳，考之於經傳，咸得其實，靡不協同。

translations.literal: Joining three and five to produce transformations, interweaving their numbers, checking them against antiquity and the present, testing them against qi and things, harmonizing them with mind and ear, and examining them against classics and commentaries, all obtain their reality, and none fail to accord together.
translations.idiomatic: By combining triadic and pentadic principles and interrelating their numbers, then testing them against ancient and later practice, physical phenomena, human perception, and the classical tradition, one finds each point borne out in fact and mutually coherent.

sentences.zh: 數者，一、十、百、千、萬也，所以算數事物，順性命之理也。

translations.literal: Numbers are one, ten, hundred, thousand, and ten-thousand; by them one counts and enumerates things, conforming to the principles of nature and allotment.
translations.idiomatic: Number begins with units, tens, hundreds, thousands, and ten-thousands; these are the means by which all things are calculated, in accord with the underlying order of nature.

sentences.zh: 《書》曰：「先其算命。」

translations.literal: The *Documents* says: "First calculate its mandate."
translations.idiomatic: As the *Book of Documents* says, "One must first compute the mandate."

sentences.zh: 本起於黃鐘之數，始於一而三之，三三積之，歷十二辰之數，十有七萬七千一百四十七，而五數備矣。

translations.literal: Its basis begins from the number of Huangzhong: starting from one and tripling it, then multiplying by three repeatedly, passing through the number of the twelve branches, one reaches 177,147, and the five-number sequence is complete.
translations.idiomatic: The system starts from the Huangzhong fundamental: begin with one, multiply by three, and continue tripling through the twelve terrestrial branches. This yields 177,147, completing the full numerical sequence.

sentences.zh: 其算法用竹，徑一分，長六寸，二百七十一枚而成六觚，為一握。

translations.literal: Its calculating method uses bamboo, with diameter one fen and length six cun; 271 pieces are formed into six-edged rods, making one bundle.
translations.idiomatic: The calculations use bamboo rods one fen in diameter and six cun long; 271 such pieces are arranged into six-edged units as a standard handful set.

sentences.zh: 徑象乾律黃鐘之一，而長象坤呂林鐘之長。

translations.literal: The diameter corresponds to one unit of the Qian-pitch Huangzhong, while the length corresponds to the length of the Kun-lü Linzhong.
translations.idiomatic: Its diameter symbolically matches one unit of the Huangzhong pitch under the Qian principle, while its length corresponds to Linzhong under the Kun principle.

sentences.zh: 其數以易大衍之數五十，其用四十九，成陽六爻，得周流六虛之象也。

translations.literal: Its numbers follow the *Changes*' Great Expansion number of fifty, whose operative use is forty-nine, forming six yang lines and obtaining the image of circulation through the six vacua.
translations.idiomatic: Its numerology follows the *Yijing*'s Great Expansion total of fifty, of which forty-nine are put to use, yielding the six yang lines and the image of cyclical movement through the six empty positions.

sentences.zh: 夫推曆生律制器，規圜矩方，權重衡平，準繩嘉量，探賾索隱，鉤深致遠，莫不用焉。

translations.literal: In deriving calendars, generating pitch-laws, and making instruments, in compassing circles and squaring right angles, in weighing heaviness and balancing levelness, in standards, plumb-lines, and proper measures, in probing complexities and seeking what is hidden, in drawing out depth and reaching what is far - none can do without numbers.
translations.idiomatic: Whether in calendrical computation, pitch standards, instrument-making, geometry, weighing, linear and volumetric measurement, or in analyzing subtle principles and far-reaching patterns, all depend on numerical method.

sentences.zh: 度長短者不失豪氂，量多少者不失圭撮，權輕重者不失黍絫。

translations.literal: Those who measure length and shortness do not miss by a hao or li; those who measure volume do not miss by a gui or cuo; those who weigh light and heavy do not miss by a millet-grain or lei.
translations.idiomatic: With proper standards, linear measures are accurate down to hair-fine units, capacities down to tiny scoops, and weights down to millet-grain increments.

sentences.zh: 紀於一，協於十，長於百，大於千，衍於萬，其法在算術。

translations.literal: It is ordered in one, harmonized in ten, grows in hundred, becomes great in thousand, and extends in ten-thousand; its method lies in arithmetic.
translations.idiomatic: The system is organized by units, coordinated by tens, expanded by hundreds and thousands, and extended into myriads; its operative method is arithmetic.

sentences.zh: 宣於天下，小學是則。

translations.literal: Promulgated to all under heaven, elementary learning takes this as its rule.
translations.idiomatic: Once promulgated across the realm, these standards became the norm in elementary education.

sentences.zh: 職在太史，羲和掌之。

translations.literal: This duty belongs to the Grand Scribe, and Xihe controls it.
translations.idiomatic: This responsibility lay in the Grand Scribe's office, under the authority of the Xihe bureau.

sentences.zh: 聲者，宮、商、角、徵、羽也。

translations.literal: The tones are gong, shang, jue, zhi, and yu.
translations.idiomatic: The five musical tones are gong, shang, jue, zhi, and yu.

sentences.zh: 所以作樂者，諧八音，蕩降人之邪意，全其正性，移風易俗也。

translations.literal: The reason for making music is to harmonize the eight sounds, cleanse and subdue people's deviant intentions, preserve their correct nature, and transform airs and change customs.
translations.idiomatic: Music is made to harmonize the eight classes of sound, purge wayward impulses, preserve right human nature, and reshape social mores.

sentences.zh: 八音：土曰塤，匏曰笙，皮曰鼓，竹曰管，絲曰絃，石曰磬，金曰鐘，木曰柷。

translations.literal: Of the eight sounds: earth is called xun; gourd is called sheng; hide is called drum; bamboo is called pipe; silk is called string; stone is called qing; metal is called bell; wood is called zhu.
translations.idiomatic: The eight timbral classes are: clay (`xun`), gourd (`sheng`), hide (`drum`), bamboo (`pipe`), silk (`strings`), stone (`qing chimes`), metal (`bells`), and wood (`zhu` percussion board).

sentences.zh: 五聲和，八音諧，而樂成。

translations.literal: When the five tones are in harmony and the eight sounds are concordant, then music is complete.
translations.idiomatic: Music is fully achieved when the five tones and eight sound classes are properly harmonized.

sentences.zh: 商之為言章也，物成孰可章度也。

translations.literal: As a term, shang means 'manifest'; when things are completed and ripened, they can be made manifest and measured.
translations.idiomatic: The tone *shang* is associated with manifestation: when things mature, they become distinct and measurable.

sentences.zh: 角，觸也，物觸地而出，戴芒角也。

translations.literal: Jue means 'to touch': things touch the earth and emerge, bearing shoots and points like horns.
translations.idiomatic: The tone *jue* signifies emergence: life presses against the earth and sprouts forth with sharp tips.

sentences.zh: 宮，中也，居中央，暢四方，唱始施生，為四聲綱也。

translations.literal: Gong means 'center': it dwells in the middle, extends to the four directions, leads in initiating generation, and serves as the guiding cord of the other four tones.
translations.idiomatic: The tone *gong* is the center: positioned at the middle, it radiates to the four quarters, initiates generative force, and serves as the framework for the other four tones.

sentences.zh: 徵，祉也，物盛大而茇祉也。

translations.literal: Zhi means 'blessing': things flourish and grow great, branching and thriving.
translations.idiomatic: The tone *zhi* signifies thriving prosperity, when things reach vigorous expansion.

sentences.zh: 羽，宇也，物聚臧宇覆之也。

translations.literal: Yu means 'shelter': things gather and are stored, and a covering shelter encloses them.
translations.idiomatic: The tone *yu* corresponds to enclosure: things collect, are stored, and come under a protective covering.

sentences.zh: 夫聲者，中於宮，觸於角，祉於徵，章於商，宇於羽，故四聲為宮紀也。

translations.literal: As for tones: they center in gong, touch in jue, flourish in zhi, become manifest in shang, and are sheltered in yu. Therefore the four tones take gong as their guiding standard.
translations.idiomatic: The tonal process is centered in *gong*, emerges in *jue*, flourishes in *zhi*, clarifies in *shang*, and settles under shelter in *yu*; thus the other four tones are structured by *gong*.

sentences.zh: 協之五行，則角為木，五常為仁，五事為貌。

translations.literal: Correlating them with the Five Phases: jue corresponds to wood; among the five constants it corresponds to benevolence; among the five affairs it corresponds to appearance.
translations.idiomatic: When mapped onto Five-Phase theory, the tone *jue* corresponds to wood, to benevolence among the five virtues, and to bearing among the five human faculties.

sentences.zh: 商為金為義為言，徵為火為禮為視，羽為水為智為聽，宮為土為信為思。

translations.literal: Shang is metal, righteousness, and speech; zhi is fire, ritual, and sight; yu is water, wisdom, and hearing; gong is earth, trustworthiness, and thought.
translations.idiomatic: The tone *shang* aligns with metal, righteousness, and speech; *zhi* with fire, ritual propriety, and vision; *yu* with water, wisdom, and hearing; and *gong* with earth, trustworthiness, and thought.

sentences.zh: 以君臣民事物言之，則宮為君，商為臣，角為民，徵為事，羽為物。

translations.literal: Speaking in terms of ruler, ministers, people, affairs, and things: gong is ruler, shang is minister, jue is people, zhi is affairs, and yu is things.
translations.idiomatic: In political analogy, *gong* stands for the ruler, *shang* for ministers, *jue* for the people, *zhi* for affairs, and *yu* for material things.

sentences.zh: 唱和有象，故言君臣位事之體也。

translations.literal: Leading and responding have their pattern, therefore this speaks of the structure of ruler-minister positions and affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Because musical lead-and-response follows a patterned order, it serves as a model for the proper structure of ruler, minister, office, and state business.

sentences.zh: 五聲之本，生於黃鐘之律。

translations.literal: The root of the five tones is generated from the Huangzhong pitch-pipe.
translations.idiomatic: The five-tone system takes its origin from the Huangzhong standard pitch.

sentences.zh: 九寸為宮，或損或益，以定商、角、徵、羽。

translations.literal: Nine cun make gong; by subtracting or adding, shang, jue, zhi, and yu are fixed.
translations.idiomatic: With nine cun as the *gong* baseline, the other tones - *shang*, *jue*, *zhi*, and *yu* - are determined through calibrated reduction and extension.

sentences.zh: 九六相生，陰陽之應也。

translations.literal: Nine and six generate one another; this is the correspondence of yin and yang.
translations.idiomatic: The reciprocal generation of nine and six expresses the interaction of yin and yang.

sentences.zh: 律十有二，陽六為律，陰六為呂。

translations.literal: There are twelve pitch standards: six yang are called lü, and six yin are called lü (lü-lu).
translations.idiomatic: There are twelve pitch standards in all, divided into six yang pitches (*lü*) and six yin pitches (*lü*).

sentences.zh: 律以統氣類物，一曰黃鐘，二曰太族，三曰姑洗，四曰蕤賓，五曰夷則，六曰亡射。

translations.literal: The yang pitches govern qi and classify things: first Huangzhong, second Taicu, third Guxian, fourth Ruibin, fifth Yize, sixth Wuyi.
translations.idiomatic: The six yang pitches regulate vital energies and classify phenomena: Huangzhong, Taicu, Guxian, Ruibin, Yize, and Wuyi.

sentences.zh: 呂以旅陽宣氣，一曰林鐘，二曰南呂，三曰應鐘，四曰大呂，五曰夾鐘，六曰中呂。

translations.literal: The yin pitches accompany yang and diffuse qi: first Linzhong, second Nanlü, third Yingzhong, fourth Dalü, fifth Jiazhong, sixth Zhonglü.
translations.idiomatic: The six yin pitches travel alongside the yang and help diffuse qi: Linzhong, Nanlü, Yingzhong, Dalü, Jiazhong, and Zhonglü.

sentences.zh: 有三統之義焉。

translations.literal: Herein lies the meaning of the Three Systems.
translations.idiomatic: This arrangement also encodes the principle of the Three Systems.

sentences.zh: 其傳曰，黃帝之所作也。

translations.literal: The tradition says this was made by the Yellow Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Traditional accounts attribute this system to the Yellow Emperor.

sentences.zh: 黃帝使泠綸，自大夏之西，昆侖之陰，取竹之解谷生，其竅厚均者，斷兩節間而吹之，以為黃鐘之宮。

translations.literal: The Yellow Emperor sent Ling Lun to the west of Daxia, on the northern slope of Kunlun, to take bamboo growing in Jiegu; selecting stalks with evenly thick cavities, he cut sections between two nodes and blew them, making this the gong of Huangzhong.
translations.idiomatic: The tale says the Yellow Emperor dispatched Ling Lun to gather bamboo from Jiegu in the Kunlun region west of Daxia. Choosing pieces of even bore and thickness, he cut them between nodes and sounded them, establishing the fundamental *gong* pitch of Huangzhong.

sentences.zh: 制十二筩以聽鳳之鳴，其雄鳴為六，雌鳴亦六，比黃鐘之宮，而皆可以生之，是為律本。

translations.literal: He made twelve pipes to hear the phoenix's cries: its male cries were six, and female cries were also six. Matching them to the gong of Huangzhong, all could be generated from it; this became the root of the pitch system.
translations.idiomatic: He then fashioned twelve pitch pipes and tuned them to phoenix calls - six male and six female. By calibrating them against Huangzhong as the base *gong*, the full set of pitches could be derived. This became the foundation of the tonal system.

sentences.zh: 至治之世，天地之氣合以生風；

translations.literal: In an age of perfect governance, the qi of Heaven and Earth join to generate wind;
translations.idiomatic: In a perfectly ordered age, the qi of Heaven and Earth converge to produce winds;

sentences.zh: 天地之風氣正，十二律定。

translations.literal: When the wind-qi of Heaven and Earth is correct, the twelve pitches are fixed.
translations.idiomatic: When cosmic winds and qi are in proper order, the twelve pitch standards settle into place.

sentences.zh: 黃鐘：黃者，中之色，君之服也；

translations.literal: Huangzhong: 'huang' is the color of the center, the ruler's color of dress;
translations.idiomatic: Huangzhong: "yellow" is the central color and the color associated with the ruler's robes;

sentences.zh: 鐘者，種也。

translations.literal: 'Zhong' means to seed.
translations.idiomatic: "Zhong" here connotes sowing and generation.

sentences.zh: 天之中數五，五為聲，聲上宮，五聲莫大焉。

translations.literal: Heaven's central number is five; five makes tone. Among tones, gong is highest; of the five tones none is greater.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's central number is five, from which tonal order is derived. Within that system, *gong* is supreme, the greatest of the five tones.

sentences.zh: 地之中數六，六為律，律有形有色，色上黃，五色莫盛焉。

translations.literal: Earth's central number is six; six makes the pitch-standards. Pitch-standards have form and color; among colors, yellow is highest, and none of the five colors is more flourishing.
translations.idiomatic: Earth's central number is six, giving rise to the pitch standards. These standards are expressed in both form and color, and among the five colors, yellow holds the premier place.

sentences.zh: 故陽氣施種於黃泉，孳萌萬物，為六氣元也。

translations.literal: Therefore yang qi sows seeds in the yellow springs, causing the ten-thousand things to sprout and grow; this is the origin of the six qi.
translations.idiomatic: Thus yang qi plants its generative force in the yellow depths, from which all things germinate; this is treated as the source of the six qi.

sentences.zh: 以黃色名元氣律者，著宮聲也。

translations.literal: Naming this primal-qi pitch with the color yellow is to make manifest the gong tone.
translations.idiomatic: Calling this primal-qi pitch "yellow" marks its status as the *gong* fundamental.

sentences.zh: 宮以九唱六，變動不居，周流六虛。

translations.literal: Gong uses nine to initiate six; in transformation and movement it does not stay fixed, but circulates through the six void positions.
translations.idiomatic: The *gong* fundamental operates by using nine to activate six, moving through continual transformation and circulating across the six empty positions.

sentences.zh: 始於子，在十一月。

translations.literal: It begins at zi, in the eleventh month.
translations.idiomatic: Its cycle begins at the *zi* branch, corresponding to the eleventh month.

sentences.zh: 大呂：呂，旅也，言陰大，旅助黃鐘宮氣而牙物也。

translations.literal: Dalü: lü means 'to accompany in ranks.' It says yin is great and accompanies in assisting the Huangzhong gong-qi so that things bud forth.
translations.idiomatic: Dalü: *lü* carries the sense of accompanying progression. Here it indicates strong yin assisting the *gong* qi of Huangzhong, enabling things to bud.

sentences.zh: 位於丑，在十二月。

translations.literal: Its position is at chou, in the twelfth month.
translations.idiomatic: It is assigned to the *chou* branch, the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 太族：族，奏也，言陽氣大，奏地而達物也。

translations.literal: Taicu: cu means 'to advance and play forth.' It says yang qi is great, pressing into earth and reaching things.
translations.idiomatic: Taicu: *cu* is glossed as forceful emergence; it denotes abundant yang qi pushing through the earth and bringing things forth.

sentences.zh: 位於寅，在正月。

translations.literal: Its position is at yin, in the first month.
translations.idiomatic: This pitch corresponds to the *yin* branch, the first lunar month.

sentences.zh: 夾鐘，言陰夾助太族宣四方之氣而出種物也。

translations.literal: Jiazhong means yin flanks and assists Taicu in diffusing the qi of the four directions and bringing forth seeded things.
translations.idiomatic: Jiazhong indicates yin supporting Taicu from either side, spreading qi through the four quarters and drawing seeded life into emergence.

sentences.zh: 位於卯，在二月。

translations.literal: Its position is at mao, in the second month.
translations.idiomatic: It is placed at the *mao* branch, corresponding to the second month.

sentences.zh: 姑洗：洗，絜也，言陽氣洗物辜絜之也。

translations.literal: Guxian: 'xian' means cleansing; it says yang qi washes things and makes them pure and clean.
translations.idiomatic: Guxian: the term implies purification, meaning that yang qi rinses and cleanses living things.

sentences.zh: 位於辰，在三月。

translations.literal: Its position is at chen, in the third month.
translations.idiomatic: This pitch corresponds to the chen branch, the third month.

sentences.zh: 中呂，言微陰始起未成，著於其中旅助姑洗宣氣齊物也。

translations.literal: Zhonglü: it means slight yin has just begun and is not yet complete; appearing within, it travels to assist Guxian in diffusing qi and equalizing things.
translations.idiomatic: Zhonglü indicates incipient yin, not yet fully formed; from within, it joins Guxian in spreading qi and bringing things into balance.

sentences.zh: 位於巳，在四月。

translations.literal: Its position is at si, in the fourth month.
translations.idiomatic: It is assigned to the si branch, the fourth month.

sentences.zh: 蕤賓：蕤，繼也，賓，導也，言陽始導陰氣使繼養物也。

translations.literal: Ruibin: rui means continuation, and bin means guidance; it says yang begins to guide yin qi so it may continue nurturing things.
translations.idiomatic: Ruibin: with the senses of continuation and leading, it denotes yang beginning to direct yin qi so that nourishment of life can continue.

sentences.zh: 位於午，在五月。

translations.literal: Its position is at wu, in the fifth month.
translations.idiomatic: This pitch belongs to the wu branch, the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 林鐘：林，君也，言陰氣受任，助蕤賓君主種物使長大楙盛也。

translations.literal: Linzhong: lin means ruler; it says yin qi receives its charge, assisting Ruibin in governing and directing seeded things to grow large and luxuriant.
translations.idiomatic: Linzhong: with lin glossed as rulership, it marks yin qi taking up its mandate and assisting Ruibin so that seeded life grows to full abundance.

sentences.zh: 位於未，在六月。

translations.literal: Its position is at wei, in the sixth month.
translations.idiomatic: It is placed at the wei branch, the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 夷則：則，法也，言陽氣正法度而使陰氣夷當傷之物也。

translations.literal: Yize: ze means rule or standard; it says yang qi rectifies norms and causes yin qi to level and properly check things that should decline.
translations.idiomatic: Yize: since ze means standard, this pitch signifies yang qi setting proper norms while yin qi brings due diminishment to what should wane.

sentences.zh: 位於申，在七月。

translations.literal: Its position is at shen, in the seventh month.
translations.idiomatic: This pitch corresponds to the shen branch, the seventh month.

sentences.zh: 南呂：南，任也，言陰氣旅助夷則任成萬物也。

translations.literal: Nanlü: nan means bearing responsibility; it says yin qi travels to assist Yize in bearing through and completing the ten-thousand things.
translations.idiomatic: Nanlü: with nan interpreted as taking responsibility, it indicates yin qi joining Yize to carry all things through to completion.

sentences.zh: 位於酉，在八月。

translations.literal: Its position is at you, in the eighth month.
translations.idiomatic: It is assigned to the you branch, the eighth month.

sentences.zh: 亡射：射，厭也，言陽氣究物而使陰氣畢剝落之，終而復始，亡厭已也。

translations.literal: Wuyi: yi means satiation; it says yang qi brings things to completion and causes yin qi to strip and shed them to the end, then return again to the beginning, without ever being satiated.
translations.idiomatic: Wuyi: taking yi as 'satiation,' it describes yang qi carrying things to their limit while yin qi fully strips and sheds them, ending only to begin again in an unending cycle.

sentences.zh: 位於戌，在九月。

translations.literal: Its position is at xu, in the ninth month.
translations.idiomatic: This pitch belongs to the xu branch, the ninth month.

sentences.zh: 應鐘，言陰氣應亡射，該臧萬物而雜陽閡種也。

translations.literal: Yingzhong: it says yin qi responds to Wuyi, comprehensively storing the ten-thousand things while mixing with yang and enclosing the seed.
translations.idiomatic: Yingzhong denotes yin qi answering Wuyi: it gathers and stores all things, while in conjunction with yang it seals and preserves the seed.

sentences.zh: 位於亥，在十月。

translations.literal: Its position is at hai, in the tenth month.
translations.idiomatic: This pitch is assigned to the hai branch, the tenth month.

sentences.zh: 三統者，天施，地化，人事之紀也。

translations.literal: The Three Systems are the ordering principles of Heaven's bestowal, Earth's transformation, and human affairs.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Systems mark the sequence of heavenly action, earthly transformation, and human governance.

sentences.zh: 十一月，乾之初九，陽氣伏於地下，始著為一，萬物萌動，鐘於太陰，故黃鐘為天統，律長九寸。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, the first nine of Qian: yang qi is hidden beneath the earth, first appearing as one; the ten-thousand things begin to stir, gathered in great yin. Therefore Huangzhong is the Heavenly System, and its pitch length is nine cun.
translations.idiomatic: In month eleven, corresponding to Qian's initial nine, yang qi lies latent underground and first emerges as unity; all things begin to stir under Great Yin. Accordingly Huangzhong is taken as the heavenly standard, with a pipe length of nine cun.

sentences.zh: 九者，所以究極中和，為萬物元也。

translations.literal: Nine is what reaches the utmost of central harmony and serves as the origin of all things.
translations.idiomatic: The number nine signifies the extreme of balanced harmony, and thus the primal source of all things.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「立天之道，曰陰與陽。」

translations.literal: The *Changes* says: "In establishing the Way of Heaven, one calls it yin and yang."
translations.idiomatic: As the *Yijing* states: "The way of Heaven is established as yin and yang."

sentences.zh: 六月，坤之初六，陰氣受任於太陽，繼養化柔，萬物生長，楙之於未，令種剛彊大，故林鐘為地統，律長六寸。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, the first six of Kun: yin qi receives its charge from great yang, continuing nurture and transforming into softness; the ten-thousand things grow, flourishing in wei and causing the seed to become hard, strong, and great. Therefore Linzhong is the Earthly System, and its pitch length is six cun.
translations.idiomatic: In month six, aligned with Kun's initial six, yin qi takes up its mandate under Great Yang, sustaining and softening growth; all things flourish at wei, and the seed becomes firm and robust. Hence Linzhong serves as the earthly standard, with a pipe length of six cun.

sentences.zh: 六者，所以含陽之施，楙之於六合之內，令剛柔有體也。

translations.literal: Six is that by which Heaven's yang endowment is contained and made to flourish within the six directions, giving fixed form to hard and soft.
translations.idiomatic: The number six contains Heaven's yang impulse and extends it through the six directions, giving stable form to both hardness and softness.

sentences.zh: 「立地之道，曰柔與剛。」

translations.literal: "In establishing the Way of Earth, one calls it soft and hard."
translations.idiomatic: "The way of Earth is defined by yielding and firmness."

sentences.zh: 「乾知太始，坤作成物。」

translations.literal: "Qian knows the great beginning; Kun acts to complete things."
translations.idiomatic: "Qian apprehends the great inception, while Kun brings things to completion."

sentences.zh: 正月，乾之九三，萬物棣通，族出於寅，人奉而成之，仁以養之，義以行之，令事物各得其理。

translations.literal: In the first month, Qian's nine in the third place: all things connect and circulate, clans emerge from yin; humans receive and complete them, nurturing with benevolence and carrying them out with righteousness, so that affairs and things each obtain their proper principle.
translations.idiomatic: In month one, corresponding to Qian's third nine, life circulates freely and lineages emerge at yin. Human beings take up and complete this process, nurturing through benevolence and enacting through righteousness, so each affair and thing attains its proper order.

sentences.zh: 寅，木也，為仁；

translations.literal: Yin is wood, and it corresponds to benevolence;
translations.idiomatic: The yin branch belongs to wood and corresponds to benevolence;

sentences.zh: 其聲，商也，為義。

translations.literal: its associated tone is shang, corresponding to righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: its corresponding tone is *shang*, associated with righteousness.

sentences.zh: 故太族為人統，律長八寸，象八卦，宓戲氏之所以順天地，通神明，類萬物之情也。

translations.literal: Therefore Taicu is the Human System, with pitch length eight cun, imaging the Eight Trigrams, by which Fuxi followed Heaven and Earth, communicated with numinous brightness, and classified the dispositions of the ten-thousand things.
translations.idiomatic: Thus Taicu represents the human standard, with an eight-cun pipe corresponding to the Eight Trigrams - the same framework by which Fuxi aligned with Heaven and Earth, communicated with the numinous, and classified the natures of all things.

sentences.zh: 「立人之道，曰仁與義。」

translations.literal: "In establishing the Way of humanity, one calls it benevolence and righteousness."
translations.idiomatic: "The human way is founded on benevolence and righteousness."

sentences.zh: 「在天成象，在地成形。」

translations.literal: "In Heaven it becomes images; on Earth it becomes forms."
translations.idiomatic: "In Heaven it appears as pattern; on Earth it appears as form."

sentences.zh: 「后以裁成天地之道，輔相天地之宜，以左右民。」

translations.literal: "Thus the sage trims and completes the ways of Heaven and Earth, assists the proprieties of Heaven and Earth, and thereby guides the people."
translations.idiomatic: "Thus one shapes and completes Heaven-and-Earth's pattern, supports what is fitting in Heaven and Earth, and with that steers the people."

sentences.zh: 此三律之謂矣，是為三統。

translations.literal: This is what is meant by these three pitches; this is the Three Systems.
translations.idiomatic: These three pitch standards are precisely what is meant by the Three Systems.

sentences.zh: 其於三正也，黃鐘子為天正，林鐘未之衝丑為地正，太族寅為人正。

translations.literal: As to the Three Correct Beginnings: Huangzhong at zi is the heavenly beginning; Linzhong at wei, opposite chou, is the earthly beginning; Taicu at yin is the human beginning.
translations.idiomatic: In the framework of the Three New-Year standards, Huangzhong at zi marks the heavenly commencement, Linzhong at wei (opposed to chou) marks the earthly commencement, and Taicu at yin marks the human commencement.

sentences.zh: 三正正始，是以地正適其始紐於陽東北丑位。

translations.literal: The Three Correct standards rectify beginnings; therefore the earthly standard takes its initial knotting at chou in the yang northeast.
translations.idiomatic: Because the Three Correct standards define proper beginnings, the earthly standard is set at the initial nodal point of chou in the northeastern yang sector.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰「東北喪朋，乃終有慶」，答應之道也。

translations.literal: The *Changes* says, "In the northeast one loses companions, and in the end there is blessing"; this is the way of response and correspondence.
translations.idiomatic: As the *Yijing* says, "In the northeast one loses one's companions, yet in the end there is good fortune" - this describes the logic of resonance and response.

sentences.zh: 及黃鐘為宮，則太族、姑洗、林鐘、南呂皆以正聲應，無有忽微，不復與它律為役者，同心一統之義也。

translations.literal: When Huangzhong serves as gong, then Taicu, Guxian, Linzhong, and Nanlü all respond with proper tones, with not even the slightest omission; no longer serving other pitches, this is the meaning of one mind and one system.
translations.idiomatic: When Huangzhong is established as the gong fundamental, Taicu, Guxian, Linzhong, and Nanlü all answer with correct tones, without even minute deviation. They no longer function as subordinates to other pitches: this is the principle of unified accord within one system.

sentences.zh: 非黃鐘而它律，雖當其月自宮者，則其和應之律有空積忽微，不得其正。

translations.literal: If not Huangzhong but another pitch, then even when that month's self-gong is appropriate, the harmonizing pitches show empty accumulation and minute discrepancy, and cannot attain correctness.
translations.idiomatic: With any pitch other than Huangzhong, even if a monthly tonic is nominally in place, the corresponding harmonics accumulate subtle deficits and cannot reach true precision.

sentences.zh: 此黃鐘至尊，亡與並也。

translations.literal: This is why Huangzhong is supreme, with nothing that can stand alongside it.
translations.idiomatic: Hence Huangzhong is paramount, unmatched by any other pitch.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「參天兩地而倚數。」

translations.literal: The *Changes* says: "Join Heaven by three and Earth by two, and on this numbers depend."
translations.idiomatic: As the *Yijing* says, "Take three for Heaven and two for Earth, and numbers are established on that basis."

sentences.zh: 天之數始於一，終於二十有五。

translations.literal: Heaven's numbers begin at one and end at twenty-five.
translations.idiomatic: The heavenly numerical sequence starts from one and culminates at twenty-five.

sentences.zh: 其義紀之以三，故置一得三，又二十五分之六，凡二十五置，終天之數，得八十一，以天地五位之合終於十者乘之，為八百一十分，應曆一統千五百三十九歲之章數，黃鐘之實也。

translations.literal: Its principle is recorded by threefolding; therefore setting one gives three, and further six twenty-fifths. Taking twenty-five settings to complete Heaven's number gives eighty-one. Multiplying this by ten, the completion of the union of Heaven-and-Earth's five positions, yields eight hundred ten, corresponding to the chapter-count of one calendrical system, 1,539 years - this is the Huangzhong constant.
translations.idiomatic: Its logic is organized by triplication: one becomes three, with an added ratio of six twenty-fifths. Carrying this through twenty-five placements to complete the heavenly number gives eighty-one; multiplied by ten (the completion of the five positional correspondences of Heaven and Earth), it yields 810. This matches the cycle chapter-count for one calendrical system of 1,539 years, namely the Huangzhong constant.

sentences.zh: 繇此之義，起十二律之周徑。

translations.literal: From this principle arise the circumferences and diameters of the twelve pitches.
translations.idiomatic: On this basis, the dimensional ratios of the twelve pitch-pipes are derived.

sentences.zh: 地之數始於二，終於三十。

translations.literal: Earth's numbers begin at two and end at thirty.
translations.idiomatic: The terrestrial numerical sequence begins at two and ends at thirty.

sentences.zh: 其義紀之以兩，故置一得二，凡三十置，終地之數，得六十，以地中數六乘之，為三百六十分，當期之日，林鐘之實。

translations.literal: Its principle is recorded by pairing; thus setting one gives two. Taking thirty settings to complete Earth's number gives sixty; multiplying by Earth's central number six yields three hundred sixty points, matching the days of a year - this is the Linzhong constant.
translations.idiomatic: Its logic is organized by pairing: one gives two. Carrying this through thirty placements completes the earthly number at sixty; multiplied by Earth's central six, it yields 360 divisions, corresponding to the days of the annual cycle - the Linzhong constant.

sentences.zh: 人者，繼天順地，序氣成物，統八卦，調八風，理八政，正八節，諧八音，舞八佾，監八方，被八荒，以終天地之功，故八八六十四。

translations.literal: Humans continue Heaven and follow Earth, sequence qi and complete things, govern the Eight Trigrams, regulate the eight winds, order the eight policies, correct the eight seasonal nodes, harmonize the eight sounds, dance the eight rows, oversee the eight directions, and extend over the eight wildernesses, thereby completing the work of Heaven and Earth. Therefore eight times eight is sixty-four.
translations.idiomatic: Humanity carries forward Heaven while conforming to Earth: ordering qi to complete things, coordinating the Eight Trigrams, eight winds, eight administrative domains, and eight seasonal divisions, harmonizing eight sounds, performing the eight-row dance, supervising the eight directions, and extending to the far reaches. Thus the human completion of Heaven-and-Earth's work is expressed as eight times eight, or sixty-four.

sentences.zh: 其義極天地之變，以天地五位之合終於十者乘之，為六百四十分，以應六十四卦，大族之實也。

translations.literal: Its principle reaches the transformations of Heaven and Earth; multiplying by ten, the completion of the union of Heaven-and-Earth's five positions, yields 640, corresponding to the sixty-four hexagrams. This is the Taicu constant.
translations.idiomatic: Its logic extends to all Heaven-and-Earth transformations: multiplied by ten (the completion of the five positional correspondences), it gives 640, matching the sixty-four hexagrams. This is the Taicu constant.

sentences.zh: 《書》曰：「天功人其代之。」

translations.literal: The *Documents* says: "Heaven's work is to be carried out by humans in its stead."
translations.idiomatic: As the *Book of Documents* says, "Human beings carry out Heaven's work on its behalf."

sentences.zh: 天兼地，人則天，故以五位之合乘焉，「唯天為大，唯堯則之」之象也。

translations.literal: Heaven encompasses Earth, and humans model Heaven; therefore one multiplies by the union of the five positions. This is the image of "Only Heaven is great, and only Yao modeled himself on it."
translations.idiomatic: Because Heaven embraces Earth and human order takes Heaven as its norm, the calculation uses the five-position union as multiplier. This reflects the saying, "Only Heaven is truly great, and Yao alone took it as his model."

sentences.zh: 地以中數乘者，陰道理內，在中餽之象也。

translations.literal: As for multiplying by Earth's central number, this is because the yin-way orders inwardly; it is the image of nourishing within the center.
translations.idiomatic: Using Earth's central number as multiplier reflects the inward ordering of yin, symbolized as nourishment within the center.

sentences.zh: 三統相通，故黃鐘、林鐘、太族律長皆全寸而亡餘分也。

translations.literal: The Three Systems interconnect, therefore the pitch lengths of Huangzhong, Linzhong, and Taicu are all whole cun with no fractional remainder.
translations.idiomatic: Since the Three Systems are mutually linked, Huangzhong, Linzhong, and Taicu all have whole-cun pipe lengths without fractional excess.

sentences.zh: 天之中數五，地之中數六，而二者為合。

translations.literal: Heaven's central number is five; Earth's central number is six; these two form a union.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's central five and Earth's central six combine as a paired system.

sentences.zh: 六為虛，五為聲，周流於六虛。

translations.literal: Six is the void-positions; five is tone; they circulate through the six voids.
translations.idiomatic: The number six designates the empty positions, while five denotes tone, and tonal operation circulates through those six positions.

sentences.zh: 虛者，爻律夫陰陽，登降運行，列為十二，而律呂和矣。

translations.literal: These voids are where line and pitch govern yin and yang: ascending, descending, and circulating in movement, they are arrayed as twelve, and then lü and lü come into harmony.
translations.idiomatic: Within these six void positions, line-structure and pitch regulate yin and yang; through ascent, descent, and cyclical movement they are arranged as twelve, and the lü-lü system is harmonized.

sentences.zh: 太極元氣，函三為一。

translations.literal: The primordial qi of the Great Ultimate enfolds three into one.
translations.idiomatic: The Great Ultimate's primal qi contains a triad within unity.

sentences.zh: 極，中也。

translations.literal: Ji means center.
translations.idiomatic: "Ultimate" here denotes centrality.

sentences.zh: 元，始也。

translations.literal: Yuan means beginning.
translations.idiomatic: "Primal" means the beginning.

sentences.zh: 行於十二辰，始動於子。

translations.literal: It moves through the twelve branches, beginning its movement at zi.
translations.idiomatic: Its cycle runs through the twelve earthly branches, beginning at zi.

sentences.zh: 參之於丑，得三。

translations.literal: Tripling it at chou gives three.
translations.idiomatic: Applying the tripling operation at chou yields three.

sentences.zh: 又參之於寅，得九。

translations.literal: Tripling again at yin gives nine.
translations.idiomatic: Tripling once more at yin yields nine.

sentences.zh: 又參之於卯，得二十七。

translations.literal: Tripling again at mao gives twenty-seven.
translations.idiomatic: A further tripling at mao produces twenty-seven.

sentences.zh: 又參之於辰，得八十一。

translations.literal: Tripling again at chen gives eighty-one.
translations.idiomatic: A further tripling at chen yields eighty-one.

sentences.zh: 又參之於巳，得二百四十三。

translations.literal: Tripling again at si gives two hundred forty-three.
translations.idiomatic: Tripling at si again produces 243.

sentences.zh: 又參之於午，得七百二十九。

translations.literal: Tripling again at wu gives seven hundred twenty-nine.
translations.idiomatic: Another tripling at wu gives 729.

sentences.zh: 又參之於未，得二千一百八十七。

translations.literal: Tripling again at wei gives two thousand one hundred eighty-seven.
translations.idiomatic: Tripling at wei further yields 2,187.

sentences.zh: 又參之於申，得六千五百六十一。

translations.literal: Tripling again at shen gives six thousand five hundred sixty-one.
translations.idiomatic: At shen, the next tripling gives 6,561.

sentences.zh: 又參之於酉，得萬九千六百八十三。

translations.literal: Tripling again at you gives nineteen thousand six hundred eighty-three.
translations.idiomatic: Tripling again at you yields 19,683.

sentences.zh: 又參之於戌，得五萬九千四十九。

translations.literal: Tripling again at xu gives fifty-nine thousand forty-nine.
translations.idiomatic: At xu, the next tripling produces 59,049.

sentences.zh: 又參之於亥，得十七萬七千一百四十七。

translations.literal: Tripling again at hai gives one hundred seventy-seven thousand one hundred forty-seven.
translations.idiomatic: A further tripling at hai yields 177,147.

sentences.zh: 此陰陽合德，氣鐘於子，化生萬物者也。

translations.literal: This is yin and yang joining in virtue, with qi gathered at zi, transforming and generating the ten-thousand things.
translations.idiomatic: This sequence represents yin and yang united in efficacy, qi concentrating at zi, and from there transforming into the generation of all things.

sentences.zh: 故孳萌於子，紐牙於丑，引達於寅，冒茆於卯，振美於辰，已盛於巳，咢布於午，昧薆於未，申堅於申，留孰於酉，畢入於戌，該閡於亥。

translations.literal: Thus germination begins at zi, budding and rooting at chou, extension and emergence at yin, covering shoots at mao, vigorous flourishing at chen, full abundance at si, broad display at wu, dim concealment at wei, hardening at shen, retained ripening at you, completion and entry at xu, and full enclosure at hai.
translations.idiomatic: So life first germinates at zi, puts down buds and roots at chou, stretches outward at yin, covers itself with shoots at mao, flourishes at chen, reaches fullness at si, spreads at wu, turns obscure at wei, hardens at shen, ripens and lingers at you, withdraws at xu, and is sealed in at hai.

sentences.zh: 出甲於甲，奮軋於乙，明炳於丙，大盛於丁，豐楙於戊，理紀於己，斂更於庚，悉新於辛，懷任於壬，陳揆於癸。

translations.literal: It emerges in jia, strives and presses in yi, shines bright in bing, reaches great flourishing in ding, grows rich and luxuriant in wu, is ordered and regulated in ji, gathers and renews in geng, is wholly renewed in xin, bears and receives in ren, and displays measured ordering in gui.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly it issues forth at jia, surges at yi, shines at bing, flourishes fully at ding, becomes rich at wu, enters ordered regulation at ji, gathers and shifts at geng, renews completely at xin, receives and bears at ren, and presents settled pattern at gui.

sentences.zh: 故陰陽之施化，萬物之終始，既類旅於律呂，又經歷於日辰，而變化之情可見矣。

translations.literal: Therefore, in the transformative workings of yin and yang and in the beginnings and endings of all things, they are both correspondingly ordered in the lü-lü pitches and pass through the day-branches; thus the conditions of transformation can be seen.
translations.idiomatic: Thus the operations of yin-yang and the cycles of beginning and ending in all things are mapped both through the pitch system and through calendrical branches, making the pattern of transformation intelligible.

sentences.zh: 玉衡杓建，天之綱也；

translations.literal: The Jade Balance's handle alignment is Heaven's guiding framework;
translations.idiomatic: The directional setting of the Jade Balance's handle forms Heaven's governing framework;

sentences.zh: 日月初纏，星之紀也。

translations.literal: the initial ecliptic circuits of sun and moon are the ordering record of the stars.
translations.idiomatic: and the initial paths of sun and moon provide the starry calendar's ordering sequence.

sentences.zh: 綱紀之交，以原始造設，合樂用焉。

translations.literal: Where these guiding and ordering lines intersect, one traces origins and establishes institutions, and from this music is employed.
translations.idiomatic: At the intersection of these frameworks, one can trace first principles and found institutions, from which ritual music takes practical form.

sentences.zh: 律呂唱和，以育生成化，歌奏用焉。

translations.literal: The lü and lü sing and respond, thereby nurturing generation and transformation; chant and performance make use of them.
translations.idiomatic: The lü-lü system works by call-and-response, giving rise to generation and transformation; sung and instrumental performance both depend on it.

sentences.zh: 指顧取象，然後陰陽萬物靡不條鬯該成。

translations.literal: By pointing and taking patterns from such correspondences, then yin-yang and the ten-thousand things are all set in order, flourishing and brought to completion.
translations.idiomatic: Once one takes these correspondences as formal models, yin and yang and all things can be ordered, unfolded, and comprehensively completed.

sentences.zh: 故以成之數忖該之積，如法為一寸，則黃鐘之長也。

translations.literal: Therefore, taking the completed number and measuring the accumulated total, if one follows the rule and makes it one cun, that is the length of Huangzhong.
translations.idiomatic: So if the completed numerical total is reduced according to rule to one cun, that value gives the Huangzhong length.

sentences.zh: 參分損一，下生林鐘。

translations.literal: Divide by three and subtract one share: below is generated Linzhong.
translations.idiomatic: Apply the sanfen method and remove one part: the descending generation yields Linzhong.

sentences.zh: 參分林鐘益一，上生太族。

translations.literal: Divide Linzhong by three and add one share: above is generated Taicu.
translations.idiomatic: From Linzhong, apply sanfen and add one part: the ascending generation yields Taicu.

sentences.zh: 參分太族損一，下生南呂。

translations.literal: Divide Taicu by three and subtract one share: below is generated Nanlü.
translations.idiomatic: From Taicu, subtract one third by the sanfen rule to generate Nanlü downward.

sentences.zh: 參分南呂益一，上生姑洗。

translations.literal: Divide Nanlü by three and add one share: above is generated Guxian.
translations.idiomatic: From Nanlü, add one third by sanfen to generate Guxian upward.

sentences.zh: 參分姑洗損一，下生應鐘。

translations.literal: Divide Guxian by three and subtract one share: below is generated Yingzhong.
translations.idiomatic: From Guxian, subtract one third to generate Yingzhong in descending order.

sentences.zh: 參分應鐘益一，上生蕤賓。

translations.literal: Divide Yingzhong by three and add one share: above is generated Ruibin.
translations.idiomatic: From Yingzhong, add one third to generate Ruibin upward.

sentences.zh: 參分蕤賓損一，下生大呂。

translations.literal: Divide Ruibin by three and subtract one share: below is generated Dalü.
translations.idiomatic: From Ruibin, subtract one third to generate Dalü downward.

sentences.zh: 參分大呂益一，上生夷則。

translations.literal: Divide Dalü by three and add one share: above is generated Yize.
translations.idiomatic: From Dalü, add one third and generate Yize upward.

sentences.zh: 參分夷則損一，下生夾鐘。

translations.literal: Divide Yize by three and subtract one share: below is generated Jiazhong.
translations.idiomatic: From Yize, subtract one third to generate Jiazhong downward.

sentences.zh: 參分夾鐘益一，上生亡射。

translations.literal: Divide Jiazhong by three and add one share: above is generated Wuyi.
translations.idiomatic: From Jiazhong, add one third to generate Wuyi upward.

sentences.zh: 參分亡射損一，下生中呂。

translations.literal: Divide Wuyi by three and subtract one share: below is generated Zhonglü.
translations.idiomatic: From Wuyi, subtract one third to generate Zhonglü downward.

sentences.zh: 陰陽相生，自黃鐘始而左旋，八八為伍。

translations.literal: Yin and yang generate each other, beginning from Huangzhong and rotating leftward; eight and eight form their grouping.
translations.idiomatic: Yin and yang mutually generate in sequence, beginning from Huangzhong and turning leftward in cyclical order, organized in an eight-by-eight structure.

sentences.zh: 其法皆用銅。

translations.literal: All these methods use bronze.
translations.idiomatic: All of these instruments are made of bronze.

sentences.zh: 職在大樂，太常掌之。

translations.literal: The office belongs to the Grand Music bureau, under the Grand Minister of Ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: This fell under the Grand Music office, administered by the Minister of Ceremonies.

sentences.zh: 度者，分、寸、尺、丈、引也，所以度長短也。

translations.literal: Measures of length are fen, cun, chi, zhang, and yin; these are used to measure long and short.
translations.idiomatic: Linear measure consists of fen, cun, chi, zhang, and yin, by which length is determined.

sentences.zh: 本起黃鐘之長。

translations.literal: Its basis begins from the length of Huangzhong.
translations.idiomatic: The system is grounded in the Huangzhong standard length.

sentences.zh: 以子穀秬黍中者，一黍之廣，度之九十分，黃鐘之長。

translations.literal: Using medium-sized black millet grains, the width of one grain is taken, and ninety such units make the length of Huangzhong.
translations.idiomatic: Using mid-sized black millet as the reference, the width of one grain is treated as a unit, and ninety such units define the Huangzhong length.

sentences.zh: 一為一分，十分為寸，十寸為尺，十尺為丈，十丈為引，而五度審矣。

translations.literal: One unit is one fen; ten fen make one cun; ten cun make one chi; ten chi make one zhang; ten zhang make one yin. Thus the five measures are fixed.
translations.idiomatic: The scale proceeds as follows: 1 fen, 10 fen = 1 cun, 10 cun = 1 chi, 10 chi = 1 zhang, and 10 zhang = 1 yin; in this way the five length units are established.

sentences.zh: 其法用銅，高一寸，廣二寸，長一丈，而分寸尺丈存焉。

translations.literal: Its method uses bronze: height one cun, width two cun, length one zhang, with fen, cun, chi, and zhang marked on it.
translations.idiomatic: The bronze rule is one cun high, two cun wide, and one zhang long, with fen, cun, chi, and zhang divisions inscribed.

sentences.zh: 用竹為引，高一分，廣六分，長十丈，其方法矩，高廣之數，陰陽之象也。

translations.literal: Bamboo is used to make the yin measure: height one fen, width six fen, length ten zhang. Its square-and-straight form and the numbers of height and width are images of yin and yang.
translations.idiomatic: The yin rod is made of bamboo, one fen high, six fen wide, and ten zhang long; its rectilinear form and dimensions are taken as symbolic expressions of yin and yang.

sentences.zh: 分者，自三微而成著，可分別也。

translations.literal: Fen means that from minute subtleties one reaches manifest distinctions, so things can be differentiated.
translations.idiomatic: Fen signifies differentiation: beginning from tiny subtleties, distinctions become clear.

sentences.zh: 寸者，忖也。

translations.literal: Cun means 'to gauge by hand.'
translations.idiomatic: Cun is glossed as measuring by estimation.

sentences.zh: 尺者，卺也。

translations.literal: Chi means 'to grasp and hold.'
translations.idiomatic: Chi is explained as a unit for taking hold.

sentences.zh: 丈者，張也。

translations.literal: Zhang means 'to stretch out.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhang is glossed as extension.

sentences.zh: 引者，信也。

translations.literal: Yin means 'trust and verification.'
translations.idiomatic: Yin is understood as reliable verification.

sentences.zh: 夫度者，別於分，忖於寸，卺於尺，張於丈，信於引。

translations.literal: Thus in measuring: one distinguishes by fen, gauges by cun, grasps by chi, extends by zhang, and verifies by yin.
translations.idiomatic: So the sequence of measurement is: differentiation in fen, gauging in cun, grasping in chi, extension in zhang, and final verification in yin.

sentences.zh: 引者，信天下也。

translations.literal: Yin is that by which trust is established throughout the realm.
translations.idiomatic: The yin unit is what secures common trust in measurement across the realm.

sentences.zh: 職在內官，廷尉掌之。

translations.literal: This office belongs to the Inner Administration and is overseen by the Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: This responsibility fell under the inner administration, under the Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 量者，龠、合、升、斗、斛也，所以量多少也。

translations.literal: Measures of capacity are yue, he, sheng, dou, and hu; they are used to measure greater and lesser amounts.
translations.idiomatic: Capacity measurement consists of yue, he, sheng, dou, and hu, by which quantity is determined.

sentences.zh: 本起於黃鐘之龠，用度數審其容，以子穀秬黍中者千有二百實其龠，以井水準其概。

translations.literal: It originates from the Huangzhong yue. Using numerical measures, one verifies its volume: 1,200 medium black millet grains fill one yue, and well water is used as the leveling standard.
translations.idiomatic: The standard begins from the Huangzhong yue. Its volume is tested numerically: one yue is filled by 1,200 mid-grade black millet grains, and well water serves as the leveling reference.

sentences.zh: 合龠為合，十合為升，十升為斗，十斗為斛，而五量嘉矣。

translations.literal: Two yue make one he, ten he make one sheng, ten sheng make one dou, ten dou make one hu; thus the five capacity measures are completed.
translations.idiomatic: The conversion is: 2 yue = 1 he, 10 he = 1 sheng, 10 sheng = 1 dou, and 10 dou = 1 hu; in this way the five capacity units are standardized.

sentences.zh: 其法用銅，方尺而圜其外，旁有庣焉。

translations.literal: Its method uses bronze: the interior is a square chi, while the exterior is rounded, with projecting sides.
translations.idiomatic: The vessel is cast in bronze, with a one-chi square interior and a rounded exterior, with side projections.

sentences.zh: 其上為斛，其下為斗。

translations.literal: The upper section is the hu; the lower section is the dou.
translations.idiomatic: Its upper part corresponds to the hu unit, and its lower part to dou.

sentences.zh: 左耳為升，右耳為合龠。

translations.literal: The left ear is the sheng; the right ear is he and yue.
translations.idiomatic: The left handle corresponds to sheng, while the right corresponds to he and yue.

sentences.zh: 其狀似爵，以縻爵祿。

translations.literal: Its form resembles a jue cup, in order to tie it to rank and stipend.
translations.idiomatic: Its shape imitates the ritual jue cup, linking measurement to the order of office and emolument.

sentences.zh: 上三下二，參天兩地，圜而函方，左一右二，陰陽之象也。

translations.literal: Three above and two below: joining three for Heaven and two for Earth; round outside enclosing square inside; one on the left and two on the right - this is the image of yin and yang.
translations.idiomatic: With three above and two below, it follows the Heaven-three Earth-two ratio; round exterior and square interior, with one left and two right, it is designed as an image of yin-yang structure.

sentences.zh: 其圜象規，其重二鈞，備氣物之數，合萬有一千五百二十。

translations.literal: Its circular form images the compass; its weight is two jun, completing the numerical system of qi and things, totaling 11,520.
translations.idiomatic: Its round contour corresponds to the compass model, and at a weight of two jun it completes the qi-material number set, totaling 11,520.

sentences.zh: 聲中黃鐘，始於黃鐘而反覆焉，君制器之象也。

translations.literal: Its sound matches Huangzhong, beginning from Huangzhong and recurring back to it - this is the image of the ruler's standard instrument-making.
translations.idiomatic: Its tone is keyed to Huangzhong, beginning from Huangzhong and returning to it in repetition; this represents the ruler's normative pattern in instrument-making.

sentences.zh: 龠者，黃鐘律之實也，躍微動氣而生物也。

translations.literal: Yue is the material substance of the Huangzhong pitch-law; leaping from subtle movement of qi, it gives rise to things.
translations.idiomatic: The yue unit is the concrete expression of the Huangzhong pitch standard: from subtle stirring of qi, generation begins.

sentences.zh: 合者，合龠之量也。

translations.literal: He is the measure that combines yue.
translations.idiomatic: He is the composite unit formed from yue.

sentences.zh: 升者，登合之量也。

translations.literal: Sheng is the measure that raises from he.
translations.idiomatic: Sheng is the next unit built up from he.

sentences.zh: 斗者，聚升之量也。

translations.literal: Dou is the measure that aggregates sheng.
translations.idiomatic: Dou is the aggregate unit composed from sheng.

sentences.zh: 斛者，角斗平多少之量也。

translations.literal: Hu is the measure by which dou-quantities are compared and leveled for more and less.
translations.idiomatic: Hu is the unit used to calibrate and equalize larger dou-level quantities.

sentences.zh: 夫量者，躍於龠，合於合，登於升，聚於斗，角於斛也。

translations.literal: As for measures: they begin from yue, combine into he, rise to sheng, gather into dou, and are finalized in hu.
translations.idiomatic: The capacity scale progresses from yue to he, rises to sheng, aggregates as dou, and reaches its full form at hu.

sentences.zh: 職在太倉，大司農掌之。

translations.literal: This office belongs to the Grand Granary, and is overseen by the Grand Minister of Agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: Administration of this standard was assigned to the Grand Granary under the Grand Minister of Agriculture.

sentences.zh: 衡權者，衡，平也，權，重也，衡所以任權而均物平輕重也。

translations.literal: As for balance and weight: heng means level, quan means weight; a balance bears weights to equalize things and make light and heavy even.
translations.idiomatic: In weighing, *heng* denotes balancing and *quan* denotes weighted mass; the balance carries weights to equalize objects and determine relative heaviness.

sentences.zh: 其道如底，以見準之正，繩之直，左旋見規，右折見矩。

translations.literal: Its method is like a foundation, showing the levelness of the standard, the straightness of the line; turning left shows the compass, turning right shows the square.
translations.idiomatic: Its principle is foundational: it manifests level by the standard, straightness by the line, circularity by left rotation, and squareness by right-angled turn.

sentences.zh: 其在天也，佐助旋機，斟酌建指，以齊七政，故曰玉衡。

translations.literal: In Heaven, it assists the revolving mechanism, adjusts and sets the pointer, and thereby aligns the seven celestial governors; thus it is called the Jade Balance.
translations.idiomatic: In celestial terms, it supports the turning mechanism and sets the directional indicator so the seven luminary motions are harmonized; hence the name Jade Balance.

sentences.zh: 論語云：「

translations.literal: The *Analects* says: "
translations.idiomatic: The *Analects* says:

sentences.zh: 立則見其參於前也，在車則見其倚於衡也。」

translations.literal: "When standing, one sees it set before him in alignment; when in the carriage, one sees it leaning against the crossbar."
translations.idiomatic: "Standing, one sees it aligned before oneself; riding in a carriage, one sees it resting against the balance beam."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「齊之以禮。」

translations.literal: It also says: "Order them through ritual."
translations.idiomatic: It also says, "Bring them into order through ritual."

sentences.zh: 此衡在前居南方之義也。

translations.literal: This is the meaning of the balance being in front and occupying the southern position.
translations.idiomatic: This is why the balance is placed in front, signifying the southern orientation.

sentences.zh: 權者，銖、兩、斤、鈞、石也，所以稱物平施，知輕重也。

translations.literal: Weights are zhu, liang, jin, jun, and shi; by them one weighs things evenly and knows light and heavy.
translations.idiomatic: The weight system consists of zhu, liang, jin, jun, and shi, by which objects are weighed and their relative heaviness determined.

sentences.zh: 本起於黃鐘之重。

translations.literal: Its basis begins from the weight of Huangzhong.
translations.idiomatic: Its standard is grounded in the Huangzhong weight value.

sentences.zh: 一龠容千二百黍，重十二銖，兩之為兩。

translations.literal: One yue holds 1,200 millet grains and weighs twelve zhu; doubling it gives one liang.
translations.idiomatic: A single yue containing 1,200 millet grains weighs 12 zhu; doubled, that becomes one liang.

sentences.zh: 二十四銖為兩。

translations.literal: Twenty-four zhu make one liang.
translations.idiomatic: 24 zhu are equivalent to one liang.

sentences.zh: 十六兩為斤。

translations.literal: Sixteen liang make one jin.
translations.idiomatic: 16 liang are equivalent to one jin.

sentences.zh: 三十斤為鈞。

translations.literal: Thirty jin make one jun.
translations.idiomatic: 30 jin are equivalent to one jun.

sentences.zh: 四鈞為石。

translations.literal: Four jun make one shi.
translations.idiomatic: 4 jun are equivalent to one shi.

sentences.zh: 忖為十八，易十有八變之象也。

translations.literal: A cun is taken as eighteen, an image of the Changes' eighteen transformations.
translations.idiomatic: The measure is set at eighteen units, corresponding to the *Yijing* image of eighteen transformations.

sentences.zh: 五權之制，以義立之，以物鈞之，其餘小大之差，以輕重為宜。

translations.literal: The system of five weights is established by principle and equalized against things; as for remaining differences of smaller and larger, appropriateness is determined by lightness and heaviness.
translations.idiomatic: The five-grade weight system is founded on normative principle and calibrated against actual objects; residual differences of scale are adjusted according to relative heaviness.

sentences.zh: 圜而環之，令之肉倍好者，周旋無端，終而復始，無窮已也。

translations.literal: It is made circular and ringed, with the thickness twice the aperture, so its turning has no end: ending and then beginning again, endlessly.
translations.idiomatic: Its ring form is circular, with body thickness twice the opening, symbolizing endless rotation: completion returning to beginning without limit.

sentences.zh: 銖者，物繇忽微始，至於成著，可殊異也。

translations.literal: Zhu signifies that things begin from minute subtlety and proceed to clear manifestation, thereby becoming distinguishable.
translations.idiomatic: The zhu unit signifies how things emerge from minute beginnings into manifest form, where distinctions become clear.

sentences.zh: 兩者，兩黃鐘律之重也。

translations.literal: Liang is the doubled weight of the Huangzhong pitch standard.
translations.idiomatic: A liang represents a doubled Huangzhong standard weight.

sentences.zh: 二十四銖而成兩者，二十四氣之象也。

translations.literal: That twenty-four zhu make one liang is an image of the twenty-four qi phases.
translations.idiomatic: The equation 24 zhu = 1 liang is taken as an analogue of the twenty-four seasonal qi divisions.

sentences.zh: 斤者，明也，三百八十四銖，易二篇之爻，陰陽變動之象也。

translations.literal: Jin means illumination: 384 zhu, corresponding to the lines of the two books of the Changes and the image of yin-yang transformation.
translations.idiomatic: Jin is associated with clarification; at 384 zhu, it corresponds to the line count of the two *Yijing* books and symbolizes yin-yang transformation.

sentences.zh: 十六兩成斤者，四時乘四方之象也。

translations.literal: That sixteen liang make one jin is the image of four seasons multiplied by four directions.
translations.idiomatic: The ratio 16 liang per jin represents the four seasons times the four directions.

sentences.zh: 鈞者，均也，陽施其氣，陰化其物，皆得其成就平均也。

translations.literal: Jun means equalization: yang applies its qi, yin transforms its things, and all attain balanced completion.
translations.idiomatic: Jun signifies equilibrium: yang exerts qi, yin transforms things, and both arrive at balanced completion.

sentences.zh: 權與物均，重萬一千五百二十銖，當萬物之象也。

translations.literal: When weight and object are equalized, the total is 11,520 zhu, corresponding to the image of the ten-thousand things.
translations.idiomatic: When weights are brought into full equilibrium with objects, the figure is 11,520 zhu, symbolically matching the myriad things.

sentences.zh: 四百八十兩者，六旬行八節之象也。

translations.literal: That 480 liang make this unit is the image of six xun and the progression through eight seasonal nodes.
translations.idiomatic: The value of 480 liang is interpreted as an analogue of six ten-day cycles moving through eight seasonal nodes.

sentences.zh: 三十斤成鈞者，一月之象也。

translations.literal: That thirty jin make one jun is the image of one month.
translations.idiomatic: The ratio of 30 jin to one jun symbolizes a month's cycle.

sentences.zh: 石者，大也，權之大者也。

translations.literal: Shi means great; it is the greatest of the weights.
translations.idiomatic: Shi denotes largeness and is the largest unit in the weight scale.

sentences.zh: 始於銖，兩於兩，明於斤，均於鈞，終於石，物終石大也。

translations.literal: It begins at zhu, pairs into liang, becomes clear at jin, equalizes at jun, and ends at shi; things end at shi because it is the great unit.
translations.idiomatic: The sequence starts with zhu, doubles into liang, clarifies at jin, equalizes at jun, and culminates at shi, since shi is the largest terminal unit.

sentences.zh: 四鈞為石者，四時之象也。

translations.literal: That four jun make one shi is the image of the four seasons.
translations.idiomatic: The ratio 4 jun = 1 shi symbolizes the four seasons.

sentences.zh: 重百二十斤者，十二月之象也。

translations.literal: Its weight of 120 jin is the image of the twelve months.
translations.idiomatic: A total weight of 120 jin represents the twelve-month cycle.

sentences.zh: 終於十二辰而復於子，黃鐘之象也。

translations.literal: Completing at the twelve branches and returning again to zi is the image of Huangzhong.
translations.idiomatic: The full turn through twelve branches and return to zi is taken as the Huangzhong pattern.

sentences.zh: 千九百二十兩者，陰陽之數也。

translations.literal: One thousand nine hundred twenty liang is the number of yin and yang.
translations.idiomatic: The figure 1,920 liang is interpreted as a yin-yang number.

sentences.zh: 三百八十四爻，五行之象也。

translations.literal: The 384 lines are the image of the Five Phases.
translations.idiomatic: The total of 384 lines is treated as an analogue of Five-Phase operation.

sentences.zh: 四萬六千八十銖者，萬一千五百二十物歷四時之象也。

translations.literal: Forty-six thousand eighty zhu is the image of 11,520 things passing through the four seasons.
translations.idiomatic: The value 46,080 zhu symbolizes 11,520 entities cycling through all four seasons.

sentences.zh: 而歲功成就，五權謹矣。

translations.literal: Thus yearly work is completed, and the five weights are made precise.
translations.idiomatic: In this way annual operations are completed and the five weight standards are made exact.

sentences.zh: 權與物鈞而生衡，衡運生規，規圜生矩，矩方生繩，繩直生準，準正則平衡而鈞權矣。

translations.literal: When weight and object are equalized, balance is produced; movement of balance produces compass; from compass circle produces square; from square form produces line; from line straightness produces level; when level is correct, then balance is level and weights are equalized.
translations.idiomatic: Equalized weights and objects produce balance; operating the balance yields the compass, from compass comes the circle, from circle the square, from square the line, and from straight line the level. When the level is correct, balance and weighing are both correct.

sentences.zh: 是為五則。

translations.literal: These are called the Five Norms.
translations.idiomatic: This is what is meant by the Five Standards.

sentences.zh: 規者，所以規圜器械，令得其類也。

translations.literal: The compass is what regulates round instruments, causing them to obtain their proper kind.
translations.idiomatic: The compass governs circular implements so each takes its proper form.

sentences.zh: 矩者，所以矩方器械，令不失其形也。

translations.literal: The square is what regulates square instruments, causing them not to lose their shape.
translations.idiomatic: The square-rule governs rectilinear implements so their form does not deviate.

sentences.zh: 規矩相須，陰陽位序，圜方乃成。

translations.literal: Compass and square require one another; with yin and yang properly ordered, round and square are then completed.
translations.idiomatic: Compass and square are mutually dependent; once yin and yang are correctly sequenced, circular and square forms can be fully realized.

sentences.zh: 準者，所以揆平取正也。

translations.literal: The level is what gauges flatness and takes correctness.
translations.idiomatic: The level is used to assess flatness and establish true alignment.

sentences.zh: 繩者，上下端直，經緯四通也。

translations.literal: The line is straight at both ends up and down, with warp and weft connecting in all four directions.
translations.idiomatic: The line establishes vertical straightness from top to bottom, while longitudinal and transverse directions connect through all four quarters.

sentences.zh: 準繩連體，衡權合德，百工繇焉，以定法式，輔弼執玉，以翼天子。

translations.literal: Level and line form a connected body; balance and weight share one virtue. The hundred crafts proceed from them to fix standards and models. Ministers upholding the jade insignia use them to assist and support the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Level and line function as one system, and balance and weight embody the same principle. From these, all crafts establish technical standards; with such norms, ministers bearing court insignia can properly support the emperor.

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：「尹氏大師，秉國之鈞，四方是維，天子是毗，俾民不迷。」

translations.literal: The *Odes* says: "The Grand Master of the Yin clan held the state's balancing authority, serving as the support of the four directions and the aid of the Son of Heaven, so that the people were not led astray."
translations.idiomatic: As the *Book of Odes* says: "The Yin clan's Grand Master held the state's balancing power, sustaining the four quarters and assisting the Son of Heaven, so that the people did not lose their way."

sentences.zh: 咸有五象，其義一也。

translations.literal: All of these have five symbolic forms, and their principle is one.
translations.idiomatic: All are expressed through five symbols, but their underlying principle is single.

sentences.zh: 以陰陽言之，大陰者，北方。

translations.literal: In terms of yin and yang, great yin is the northern direction.
translations.idiomatic: Within yin-yang correspondences, Great Yin is assigned to the north.

sentences.zh: 北，伏也，陽氣伏於下，於時為冬。

translations.literal: North means concealment: yang qi lies hidden below; in seasonal terms this is winter.
translations.idiomatic: North signifies latency, where yang qi is hidden beneath; seasonally, this is winter.

sentences.zh: 冬，終也，物終臧，乃可稱。

translations.literal: Winter means ending: things conclude and are stored, and only then can they be weighed.
translations.idiomatic: Winter marks completion: things come to an end and are stored away, making assessment possible.

sentences.zh: 水潤下。

translations.literal: Water moistens and flows downward.
translations.idiomatic: Water's nature is to moisten and descend.

sentences.zh: 知者謀，謀者重，故為權也。

translations.literal: Wisdom plans; planning is weighty; therefore it corresponds to weight.
translations.idiomatic: Wisdom consists in deliberation, and deliberation has weight; hence it corresponds to weighting.

sentences.zh: 大陽者，南方。

translations.literal: Great yang is the southern direction.
translations.idiomatic: Great Yang is assigned to the south.

sentences.zh: 南，任也，陽氣任養物，於時為夏。

translations.literal: South means bearing responsibility: yang qi takes up the nurturing of things; in seasonal terms this is summer.
translations.idiomatic: South signifies taking charge: yang qi assumes the task of nourishing life, corresponding to summer.

sentences.zh: 夏，假也，物假大，乃宣平。

translations.literal: Summer means expansion: things assume great growth, and then diffuse and level out.
translations.idiomatic: Summer signifies expansion, when things reach great growth and unfold toward full expression.

sentences.zh: 火炎上。

translations.literal: Fire blazes upward.
translations.idiomatic: Fire's tendency is to rise.

sentences.zh: 禮者齊，齊者平，故為衡也。

translations.literal: Ritual is ordering; ordering is leveling; therefore it corresponds to the balance.
translations.idiomatic: Ritual establishes ordered alignment, and ordered alignment produces equilibrium; thus it corresponds to balancing.

sentences.zh: 少陰者，西方。

translations.literal: Lesser yin is the western direction.
translations.idiomatic: Lesser Yin corresponds to the west.

sentences.zh: 西，遷也，陰氣遷落物，於時為秋。

translations.literal: West means shifting: yin qi shifts and causes things to fall away; in seasonal terms this is autumn.
translations.idiomatic: West signifies transition, where yin qi moves things toward decline; seasonally, this is autumn.

sentences.zh: 秋，胆也，物呙斂，乃成孰。

translations.literal: Autumn means probing firmness: things contract and gather, and thus reach ripeness.
translations.idiomatic: Autumn signifies contraction and hardening, as things draw inward and come to maturity.

sentences.zh: 金從革，改更也。

translations.literal: Metal follows reform and change.
translations.idiomatic: Metal's nature is transformation and reformation.

sentences.zh: 義者成，成者方，故為矩也。

translations.literal: Righteousness is completion; completion is squareness; therefore it corresponds to the square-rule.
translations.idiomatic: Righteousness brings things to completion, and completion takes square form; thus it corresponds to the square.

sentences.zh: 少陽者，東方。

translations.literal: Lesser yang is the eastern direction.
translations.idiomatic: Lesser Yang corresponds to the east.

sentences.zh: 東，動也，陽氣動物，於時為春。

translations.literal: East means movement: yang qi moves things; in seasonal terms this is spring.
translations.idiomatic: East signifies movement, where yang qi sets life in motion; seasonally, this is spring.

sentences.zh: 春，蠢也，物蠢生，乃動運。

translations.literal: Spring means stirring: things stir to life and then begin to move and circulate.
translations.idiomatic: Spring means awakening, when life stirs and begins active circulation.

sentences.zh: 木曲直。

translations.literal: Wood bends and straightens.
translations.idiomatic: Wood's property is flexible straightness - bending and returning straight.

sentences.zh: 仁者生，生者圜，故為規也。

translations.literal: Benevolence is generation; what generates is round; therefore it corresponds to the compass.
translations.idiomatic: Benevolence is life-generating, and generation takes circular form; hence it corresponds to the compass.

sentences.zh: 中央者，陰陽之內，四方之中，經緯通達，乃能端直，於時為四季。

translations.literal: The center is within yin and yang and in the middle of the four directions; with warp and weft fully connected, only then can proper uprightness be achieved. In seasonal terms this is the four interseasonal periods.
translations.idiomatic: The center lies within the yin-yang field and amid the four directions; only when longitudinal and transverse lines are fully connected can true uprightness be established. Seasonally, this corresponds to the four interseasonal nodes.

sentences.zh: 土稼嗇蕃息。

translations.literal: Earth supports sowing, storing, and flourishing growth.
translations.idiomatic: Earth's function is to receive cultivation, store, and foster proliferation.

sentences.zh: 信者誠，誠者直，故為繩也。

translations.literal: Trustworthiness is sincerity; sincerity is straightness; therefore it corresponds to the line.
translations.idiomatic: Trust is grounded in sincerity, and sincerity in straightness; thus it corresponds to the line.

sentences.zh: 五則揆物，有輕重圜方平直陰陽之義，四方四時之體，五常五行之象。

translations.literal: The Five Norms gauge things and contain the meanings of light/heavy, round/square, level/straight, and yin/yang; they embody the four directions and four seasons, and image the five constants and five phases.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Standards evaluate things through paired principles - weight and lightness, round and square, level and straight, yin and yang - while embodying the structure of four directions, four seasons, and the correspondences of the five virtues and five phases.

sentences.zh: 厥法有品，各順其方而應其行。

translations.literal: Their methods have graded categories, each following its proper direction and corresponding phase.
translations.idiomatic: Their methods are classed by rank, each aligned to its proper directional position and phase-correspondence.

sentences.zh: 職在大行，鴻臚掌之。

translations.literal: The office belongs to the Office of Grand Processions, under the supervision of the Director of Guests.
translations.idiomatic: This function was assigned to the Office of Grand Processions, administered by the Director of Guests.

sentences.zh: 《書》曰：「予欲聞六律、五聲、八音、七始詠，以出內五言，女聽。」

translations.literal: The *Documents* says: "I wish to hear the six pitches, five tones, eight sounds, and chants of the seven beginnings, so as to bring forth and receive the five utterances; you listen."
translations.idiomatic: As the *Book of Documents* says: "I want to hear the six pitch standards, five tones, eight timbral classes, and the chants of the seven beginnings, so that the five forms of utterance may be properly issued and received. Listen well."

sentences.zh: 予者，帝舜也。

translations.literal: 'I' refers to Emperor Shun.
translations.idiomatic: The pronoun "I" here refers to Emperor Shun.

sentences.zh: 言以律呂和五聲，施之八音，合之成樂。

translations.literal: It says to use the lü-lü pitches to harmonize the five tones, apply them to the eight sounds, and combine them to complete music.
translations.idiomatic: The passage means that one should harmonize the five tones through the lü-lü system, apply them across the eight timbral classes, and integrate them into complete music.

sentences.zh: 七者，天地四時人之始也。

translations.literal: The seven are the beginnings of Heaven, Earth, the four seasons, and humanity.
translations.idiomatic: The "seven" refers to the primal origins of Heaven, Earth, the four seasons, and human order.

sentences.zh: 順以歌詠五常之言，聽之則順乎天地，序乎四時，應人倫，本陰陽，原情性，風之以德，感之以樂，莫不同乎一。

translations.literal: Following this, one sings and chants words of the five constants; when heard, they accord with Heaven and Earth, sequence with the four seasons, respond to human relations, root in yin and yang, trace emotional nature, influence through virtue, and move through music, with nothing not brought into one.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, song and chant should express the language of the five constants. Properly heard, they align with Heaven and Earth, follow the seasons, answer to human relations, root in yin-yang principle, and accord with human feeling and nature; through moral influence and musical transformation, all are brought into unity.

sentences.zh: 唯聖人為能同天下之意，故帝舜欲聞之也。

translations.literal: Only sages can unify the intentions of all under Heaven; therefore Emperor Shun wished to hear this.
translations.idiomatic: Only a sage can unify the intentions of the whole realm, which is why Emperor Shun wished to hear such music.

sentences.zh: 今廣延群儒，博謀講道，修明舊典，同律，審度，嘉量，平衡，鈞權，正準，直繩，立于五則，備數和聲，以利兆民，貞天下於一，同海內之歸。
