translations.literal: Now we broadly invite scholars, seek wide counsel and discuss the Way, restore and clarify old canons, standardize pitches, examine measures, perfect capacities, level balances, equalize weights, correct levels, straighten lines, establish the five standards, complete numbers and harmonize tones, to benefit the multitude, rectify the realm into oneness, and unify all under the seas in return.
translations.idiomatic: Now we have broadly convened scholars, sought wide-ranging counsel, and deliberated on principle, restoring and clarifying the old canons: unifying pitch, scrutinizing measures, standardizing capacities, balancing scales, equalizing weights, correcting levels, and straightening lines, all grounded in the Five Standards. By perfecting number and tonal harmony, we aim to benefit the people, secure unity in the realm, and bring all under heaven to a common order.

sentences.zh: 凡律度量衡用銅者，名自名也，所以同天下，齊風俗也。

translations.literal: As for lü, measure, capacity, and balance using bronze, each name corresponds to its name; this is by which the realm is unified and customs made uniform.
translations.idiomatic: For pitch standards, linear measures, capacities, and weights made in bronze, each designation matches a fixed standard; by this, the realm is unified and customs standardized.

sentences.zh: 銅為物之至精，不為燥溼寒暑變其節，不為風雨暴露改其形，介然有常，有似於士君子之行，是以用銅也。

translations.literal: Bronze is the utmost refined among materials: dryness, dampness, cold, and heat do not alter its calibration; wind, rain, and exposure do not change its form. Steadfast and constant, it resembles the conduct of the gentleman, therefore bronze is used.
translations.idiomatic: Bronze is a highly refined material: neither dryness nor moisture, neither cold nor heat alters its measure, and wind, rain, or exposure do not distort its shape. Its stable constancy resembles the conduct of the noble person, and for this reason it is used.

sentences.zh: 用竹為引者，事之宜也。

translations.literal: Using bamboo for the yin measure is what is fitting for practical affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Using bamboo for the yin rod is a matter of practical suitability.

sentences.zh: 曆數之起上矣。

translations.literal: The origins of calendrical numbers are very ancient indeed.
translations.idiomatic: The beginnings of calendrical computation reach back to the highest antiquity.

sentences.zh: 傳述顓頊命南正重司天，火正黎司地，其後三苗亂德，二官咸廢，而閏餘乖次，孟陬殄滅，攝提失方。

translations.literal: Traditions recount that Zhuanxu ordered Zhong, the Southern Corrector, to govern Heaven, and Li, Corrector of Fire, to govern Earth. Later, the Three Miao threw virtue into disorder, both offices were abolished, intercalary surplus fell out of sequence, the first month notation was lost, and Sheti lost its direction.
translations.idiomatic: According to transmitted accounts, Zhuanxu appointed Zhong, the Southern Corrector, to oversee celestial affairs, and Li, the Fire Corrector, to oversee terrestrial affairs. Later, when the Three Miao disrupted moral order, both offices collapsed; intercalary reckoning went out of sequence, first-month alignment disappeared, and the Sheti cycle lost its orientation.

sentences.zh: 堯復育重、黎之後，使纂其業，故《書》曰：「乃命羲、和，欽若昊天，曆象日月星辰，敬授民時。」

translations.literal: Yao then again nurtured the descendants of Zhong and Li and had them continue their work. Therefore the *Documents* says: "He then commanded Xi and He to reverently follow Vast Heaven, calculate and image the sun, moon, stars, and constellations, and respectfully give the people the seasons."
translations.idiomatic: Yao then restored the line of Zhong and Li and ordered their descendants to continue that work. Hence the *Book of Documents* says: "He charged Xi and He to revere High Heaven, calculate the patterns of sun, moon, stars, and constellations, and respectfully promulgate seasonal time for the people."

sentences.zh: 「歲三百有六旬有六日，以閏月定四時成歲，允釐百官，眾功皆美。」

translations.literal: "A year has three hundred and sixty-six days; by an intercalary month the four seasons are fixed and the year completed. Thereby the hundred offices are truly regulated and all achievements are excellent."
translations.idiomatic: "The year contains 366 days; with intercalary months, the four seasons are fixed and the annual cycle is completed. Thus the hundred offices are properly ordered and all undertakings succeed."

sentences.zh: 其後以授舜曰：「咨爾舜，天之曆數在爾躬。」

translations.literal: Afterward he transmitted it to Shun, saying: "Ah, you Shun - Heaven's calendrical numbers rest in your person."
translations.idiomatic: He then transmitted this charge to Shun, saying: "Ah, Shun, Heaven's calendrical mandate now resides in your person."

sentences.zh: 「舜亦以命禹。」

translations.literal: "Shun in turn used it to command Yu."
translations.idiomatic: "Shun, in turn, entrusted the same charge to Yu."

sentences.zh: 至周武王訪箕子，箕子言大法九章，而五紀明曆法。

translations.literal: When King Wu of Zhou consulted Jizi, Jizi spoke of the Nine Sections of the Great Law, in which the fifth category clarifies calendrical methods.
translations.idiomatic: When King Wu of Zhou sought counsel from Jizi, Jizi presented the Nine Articles of the Great Plan, whose fifth section explicates calendrical governance.

sentences.zh: 故自殷周，皆創業改制，咸正曆紀，服色從之，順其時氣，以應天道。

translations.literal: Therefore from Shang through Zhou, all founders who created dynasties and altered institutions corrected the calendar sequence, and official colors followed it, conforming to seasonal qi to correspond with Heaven's Way.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, from Shang to Zhou, each founding regime that restructured institutions also corrected the calendrical cycle, with official color codes adjusted to match seasonal qi and align with Heaven's order.

sentences.zh: 三代既沒，五伯之末史官喪紀，疇人子弟分散，或在夷狄，故其所記，有黃帝、顓頊、夏、殷、周及魯曆。

translations.literal: After the Three Dynasties were gone, by the end of the Five Hegemons the historiographic offices had lost their records, and descendants of calendrical specialists scattered, some among non-Chinese peoples. Thus in what was recorded there were calendars of Yellow Emperor, Zhuanxu, Xia, Yin, Zhou, and Lu.
translations.idiomatic: After the Three Dynasties fell, and by the late age of the Five Hegemons, historical offices had lost their archival continuity; calendrical families dispersed, some even beyond the central states. What remained were records of the calendars of the Yellow Emperor, Zhuanxu, Xia, Yin, Zhou, and Lu.

sentences.zh: 戰國擾攘，秦兼天下，未皇暇也，亦頗推五勝，而自以為獲水德，乃以十月為正，色上黑。

translations.literal: In the turmoil of the Warring States, Qin unified the realm and had no imperial leisure; it nevertheless somewhat promoted the Five Victory theory, taking itself to have obtained Water Virtue, therefore making the tenth month the first month and exalting black as color.
translations.idiomatic: Amid Warring States chaos, Qin unified all-under-heaven and had little leisure for full reform. Still, it adopted the Five-Phase conquest doctrine, claiming Water Virtue, and therefore set month ten as the year's beginning and elevated black as the dynastic color.

sentences.zh: 漢興，方綱紀大基，庶事草創，襲秦正朔。

translations.literal: When Han arose, while establishing the great foundations of order and creating institutions from scratch, it inherited Qin's calendrical beginning.
translations.idiomatic: At the Han founding, while state structures were still being built from the ground up, the regime initially retained Qin's calendar basis.

sentences.zh: 以北平侯張蒼言，用顓頊曆，比於六曆，疏闊中最為微近。

translations.literal: Following the advice of the Marquis of Beiping, Zhang Cang, it used the Zhuanxu calendar; compared among the six calendars, though broadly rough, it was the nearest in subtle accuracy.
translations.idiomatic: On Zhang Cang's recommendation, Han adopted the Zhuanxu calendar, which - among the six transmitted systems - was judged rough in scope but closest in fine approximation.

sentences.zh: 然正朔服色，未睹其真，而朔晦月見，弦望滿虧，多非是。

translations.literal: Yet as to the true correctness of first month and color code, it had not been fully seen; and in new moon, dark moon, moonrise, quarter, full, and waning, many calculations were not correct.
translations.idiomatic: Even so, true correctness in New-Year reckoning and dynastic color had not been achieved, and many predictions of lunar phases - new, dark, rising, quarter, full, and waning - were inaccurate.

sentences.zh: 至武帝元封七年，漢興百二歲矣，大中大夫公孫卿、壺遂、太史令司馬遷等言「曆紀壞廢，宜改正朔」。

translations.literal: By Emperor Wu's seventh year of Yuanfeng, 102 years had passed since Han's rise. Grandee for Palace Service Gongsun Qing, Hu Sui, the Grand Historian Sima Qian, and others said, "The calendar system has broken down and lapsed; the first month should be corrected."
translations.idiomatic: By the seventh year of Yuanfeng under Emperor Wu, 102 years had elapsed since Han's founding. Gongsun Qing, Hu Sui, Sima Qian, and others memorialized that the calendrical system had decayed and should be corrected, beginning with the proper first month.

sentences.zh: 是時御史大夫兒寬明經術，上乃詔寬曰：「與博士共議，今宜何以為正朔？

translations.literal: At that time the Censor-in-Chief Ni Kuan was learned in the classics. The emperor then issued an edict to Kuan: "Discuss with the Erudites - by what should the first month now be fixed?
translations.idiomatic: At the time, the Censor-in-Chief Ni Kuan was renowned for classical learning, so the emperor ordered him: "Consult the court Erudites: by what standard should we now set the year's first month?

sentences.zh: 服色何上？」

translations.literal: Which color should be given highest place?"
translations.idiomatic: And which dynastic color should hold primacy?"

sentences.zh: 寬與博士賜等議，皆曰：「帝王必改正朔，易服色，所以明受命於天也。

translations.literal: Kuan and Erudites such as Ci discussed and all said: "Emperor-kings must change the first month and alter official colors, in order to make clear that they have received Heaven's mandate.
translations.idiomatic: Ni Kuan and the Erudites, including Ci, deliberated and replied: "Sovereign founders must revise calendrical commencement and change state colors to show clearly that they have received Heaven's mandate.

sentences.zh: 創業變改，制不相復，推傳序文，則今夏時也。

translations.literal: When founding and transforming institutions, systems are not repeated from one another; if one infers from transmitted sequence texts, then now it is Xia time.
translations.idiomatic: At dynastic founding, institutional systems are transformed rather than simply repeated; judged from transmitted succession principles, the present should follow the Xia standard.

sentences.zh: 臣等聞學褊陋，不能明。

translations.literal: We subjects have heard narrowly and are shallow in learning, and cannot make it fully clear.
translations.idiomatic: Our learning is limited and inadequate, and we cannot claim full clarity on this point.

sentences.zh: 陛下躬聖發憤，昭配天地，臣愚以為三統之制，後聖復前聖者，二代在前也。

translations.literal: Your Majesty, personally sage and ardently intent, illuminates in matching Heaven and Earth. In this foolish subject's view, in the system of the Three Orders, where a later sage succeeds an earlier sage, two dynasties stand in between.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty's personal sageliness and determined effort are worthy of Heaven and Earth. In my humble view, under the Three-Order model, when one sage age succeeds another, two dynasties intervene between them.

sentences.zh: 今二代之統絕而不序矣，唯陛下發聖德，宣考天地四時之極，則順陰陽以定大明之制，為萬世則。」

translations.literal: Now the lines of those two dynasties are broken and no longer in sequence. If only Your Majesty unfolds sagely virtue and publicly examines the utmost principles of Heaven, Earth, and the four seasons, then by following yin and yang you can establish the great and clear system as a model for ten thousand generations."
translations.idiomatic: Now those two dynastic lines are interrupted and no longer properly ordered. If Your Majesty manifests sagely virtue and fully examines the ultimate principles of Heaven, Earth, and the four seasons, then by following yin and yang you can establish a great luminous system to serve as a norm for all later ages."

sentences.zh: 於是乃詔御史曰：「乃者有司言曆未定，廣延宣問，以考星度，未能讎也。

translations.literal: Thereupon he issued an edict to the Censorate: "Recently the offices reported that the calendar is not yet fixed. We have broadly extended inquiry and consultation and examined stellar degrees, but have not yet been able to verify and reconcile them.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then ordered the Censorate: "The ministries have reported that the calendar remains unsettled. We have widely solicited inquiry and consultation, examining stellar positions, but we still have not been able to reconcile the calculations.

sentences.zh: 蓋聞古者黃帝合而不死，名察發斂，定清濁，起五部，建氣物分數。

translations.literal: I have heard that in antiquity the Yellow Emperor integrated these matters without decline, distinguished names and observed growth and withdrawal, fixed clear and turbid, initiated the five divisions, and established numerical allotments for qi and things.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that in high antiquity the Yellow Emperor brought these domains into coherent order: he distinguished categories, observed cycles of emergence and withdrawal, defined clear and turbid, set out the five divisions, and established numerical ratios for qi and material forms.

sentences.zh: 然則上矣。

translations.literal: That indeed is the highest model.
translations.idiomatic: That is truly the highest precedent.

sentences.zh: 書缺樂弛，朕甚難之。

translations.literal: With records lacking and ritual music slackened, I find this extremely difficult.
translations.idiomatic: Now that records are incomplete and ritual music has lapsed, I find the problem exceedingly difficult.

sentences.zh: 依違以惟，未能修明。

translations.literal: Hesitating and deliberating, we have still been unable to restore clarity.
translations.idiomatic: Despite repeated deliberation and caution, we still have not been able to restore full clarity.

sentences.zh: 其以七年為元年。」

translations.literal: Let the seventh year be taken as the first year."
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, let year seven be reset as year one."

sentences.zh: 遂詔卿、遂、遷與侍郎尊、大典星射姓等議造漢曆。

translations.literal: He then ordered Qing, Sui, and Qian, together with Attendant Gentleman Zun and the Grand Astrologer Shexing and others, to discuss and construct the Han calendar.
translations.idiomatic: He then ordered Gongsun Qing, Hu Sui, Sima Qian, Attendant Gentleman Zun, Grand Astrologer Shexing, and others to deliberate and draft a new Han calendar.

sentences.zh: 乃定東西，立晷儀，下漏刻，以追二十八宿相距於四方，舉終以定朔晦分至，躔離弦望。

translations.literal: They then fixed east and west, set up gnomons and instruments, and installed clepsydra timing, in order to track the spacing of the twenty-eight lodges in the four directions, and in sum to determine new and dark moons, solstices and equinoxes, orbital positions, and quarter and full phases.
translations.idiomatic: They established east-west orientation, erected gnomons and observing instruments, and set water clocks, so they could track the spacing of the twenty-eight lunar lodges across the four quarters and thereby determine new and dark moons, solstices and equinoxes, orbital position, and quarter/full phases.

sentences.zh: 乃以前曆上元泰初四千六百一十七歲，至於元封七年，復得閼逢攝提格之歲，中冬十一月甲子朔旦冬至，日月在建星，太歲在子，已得太初本星度新正。

translations.literal: They then took the former calendar's high origin as 4,617 years before Taichu, and by Yuanfeng year seven again obtained the year of Yanfeng Shetige, with midwinter eleventh month jiazi new moon dawn coinciding with the winter solstice; sun and moon were at the Jian stars, Jupiter was at zi, and thus they obtained the Taichu base stellar degrees and new calendrical commencement.
translations.idiomatic: Using the old calendar, they back-calculated 4,617 years to the Taichu high epoch, and by Yuanfeng 7 they recovered a benchmark year in the sexagenary cycle: in midwinter, month eleven, a jiazi new-moon dawn coincided with the winter solstice, with sun and moon at the Jian stars and Jupiter at zi. On that basis they established the Taichu system's base stellar degrees and new opening date.

sentences.zh: 姓等奏不能為算，願募治曆者，更造密度，各自增減，以造漢太初曆。

translations.literal: Shexing and others memorialized that they could not complete the computations, requesting that specialists in calendrical calculation be recruited to construct finer measurements, each making additions and subtractions, in order to produce the Han Taichu calendar.
translations.idiomatic: Shexing and his colleagues reported that they could not complete the calculations and requested recruitment of calendrical specialists to produce tighter numerical measures, adjusting where needed, so as to compile the Han Taichu calendar.

sentences.zh: 乃選治曆鄧平及長樂司馬可、酒泉候宜君、侍郎尊及與民間治曆者，凡二十餘人，方士唐都、巴郡落下閎與焉。

translations.literal: Thus they selected calendrical specialist Deng Ping, and Simake of Changle, Hou Yijun of Jiuquan, Attendant Gentleman Zun, and civilian calendar specialists - more than twenty in all; the fangshi Tang Du and Luo Xiahong of Bajun also took part.
translations.idiomatic: They therefore selected the calendrical expert Deng Ping, Sima Ke of Changle, Hou Yijun of Jiuquan, Attendant Gentleman Zun, and over twenty private calendar specialists; the technical masters Tang Du and Luo Xiahong of Ba commandery also joined.

sentences.zh: 都分天部，而閎運算轉曆。

translations.literal: Du handled the division of celestial sectors, while Hong carried out calculations and calendar transformations.
translations.idiomatic: Tang Du was responsible for dividing the celestial sectors, while Luo Xiahong ran the core computations and cyclical calendrical conversions.

sentences.zh: 其法以律起曆，曰：「律容一龠，積八十一寸，則一日之分也。

translations.literal: Their method began the calendar from the pitch-laws, saying: "A pitch vessel holds one yue; accumulating to eighty-one cun gives the divisions of one day.
translations.idiomatic: Their method derived calendrical numbers from pitch standards: "A pitch vessel contains one yue; accumulated to 81 cun, this yields the day's fractional divisions.

sentences.zh: 與長相終。

translations.literal: It corresponds to the terminal value of length.
translations.idiomatic: This is paired with the terminal value in the length sequence.

sentences.zh: 律長九寸，百七十一分而終復。

translations.literal: A pitch length of nine cun completes its return in 171 parts.
translations.idiomatic: With a pitch-pipe length of nine cun, the cycle returns to its starting point after 171 divisions.

sentences.zh: 三復而得甲子。

translations.literal: After three returns, one reaches jiazi.
translations.idiomatic: After three full recurrences, the cycle arrives at jiazi.

sentences.zh: 夫律陰陽九六，爻象所從出也。

translations.literal: Now, the lü standards of yin and yang, nine and six, are what line-images derive from.
translations.idiomatic: The yin-yang pitch standards, structured by nine and six, are the source from which line-symbols are derived.

sentences.zh: 故黃鐘紀元氣之謂律。

translations.literal: Therefore Huangzhong, which records primordial qi, is what is called a lü standard.
translations.idiomatic: Hence Huangzhong, as the register of primal qi, is what is meant by a lü standard.

sentences.zh: 律，法也，莫不取法焉。」

translations.literal: Lü means rule or norm; there is nothing that does not take its model from it."
translations.idiomatic: "Lü means normative law: all things take it as their model."

sentences.zh: 與鄧平所治同。

translations.literal: This matched what Deng Ping had computed.
translations.idiomatic: This agreed with Deng Ping's calculations.

sentences.zh: 於是皆觀新星度、日月行，更以算推，如閎、平法。

translations.literal: Thereupon all examined new stellar degrees and the motions of sun and moon, and again used calculation to derive them, as in the methods of Hong and Ping.
translations.idiomatic: They then reexamined new stellar-degree data and solar-lunar motion, recalculating according to the methods associated with Luo Xiahong and Deng Ping.

sentences.zh: 法，一月之日二十九日八十一分日之四十三。

translations.literal: By that method, one month has 29 days and forty-three parts out of eighty-one parts of a day.
translations.idiomatic: In that method, a synodic month is 29 days plus 43/81 of a day.

sentences.zh: 先藉半日，名曰陽曆；

translations.literal: If one first borrows half a day, it is called the yang calendar;
translations.idiomatic: When a half-day offset is applied in advance, it is called the yang calendar;

sentences.zh: 不藉，名曰陰曆。

translations.literal: if one does not borrow it, it is called the yin calendar.
translations.idiomatic: without that offset, it is called the yin calendar.

sentences.zh: 所謂陽曆者，先朔月生；

translations.literal: What is called the yang calendar is that before new moon, the moon is already born;
translations.idiomatic: In the so-called yang calendar, the moon is treated as appearing before the calendrical new-moon point;

sentences.zh: 陰曆者，朔而後月乃生。

translations.literal: as for the yin calendar, only after new moon does the moon then arise.
translations.idiomatic: in the yin calendar, lunar appearance is reckoned only after the new-moon point.

sentences.zh: 平曰：「陽曆朔皆先旦月生，以朝諸侯王群臣便。」

translations.literal: Ping said: "In the yang calendar, on all new moons the moon is born before dawn, making morning audience convenient for feudal kings and assembled officials."
translations.idiomatic: Deng Ping said: "Under the yang calendar, at each new moon the moon is reckoned to have appeared before dawn, which makes court morning audiences more convenient for kings and ministers."

sentences.zh: 乃詔遷用鄧平所造八十一分律曆，罷廢尤疏遠者十七家，復使校曆律昏明。

translations.literal: Thereupon an edict ordered Qian to adopt Deng Ping's eighty-one-part pitch-calendar, abolishing seventeen schools judged especially coarse and distant, and again directing verification of calendrical and pitch standards by dusk and dawn observations.
translations.idiomatic: The court then ordered Sima Qian to implement Deng Ping's 81-part pitch-calendar method, discarding seventeen systems deemed especially imprecise, and to continue verification of calendar and pitch standards through dawn-and-dusk observations.

sentences.zh: 宦者淳于陵渠復覆太初曆晦朔弦望，皆最密，日月如合璧，五星如連珠。

translations.literal: The eunuch Chunyu Lingqu again reviewed new moon, dark moon, quarter, and full phases in the Taichu calendar; all were the most precise - sun and moon like joined jade discs, five planets like strung pearls.
translations.idiomatic: The eunuch Chunyu Lingqu rechecked the Taichu calendar's new-moon, dark-moon, quarter, and full-phase calculations and found them the most exact: sun and moon aligned like paired jade discs, and the five planets like a string of pearls.

sentences.zh: 陵渠奏狀，遂用鄧平曆，以平為太史丞。

translations.literal: Lingqu submitted his memorial, and Deng Ping's calendar was then adopted, with Ping appointed Assistant Grand Historian.
translations.idiomatic: After Lingqu submitted his report, the court adopted Deng Ping's calendar and appointed Deng Ping as Assistant Grand Historian.

sentences.zh: 後二十七年，元鳳三年，太史令張壽王上書言：「曆者天地之大紀，上帝所為。

translations.literal: Twenty-seven years later, in the third year of Yuanfeng, the Grand Astrologer Zhang Shouwang submitted a memorial saying: "The calendar is the great guiding principle of Heaven and Earth, made by the Lord on High."
translations.idiomatic: Twenty-seven years later, in the third year of Yuanfeng, the Grand Astrologer Zhang Shouwang submitted a memorial: "The calendar is the fundamental order of Heaven and Earth, established by the Supreme Deity."

sentences.zh: 傳黃帝調律曆，漢元年以來用之。

translations.literal: Tradition says that the Yellow Emperor harmonized pitch-pipes and the calendar. In the first year of Han, among the six calendars, the Zhuanxu calendar was broad and imprecise, yet among them it came closest; still, the first month and court colors had not been fixed.
translations.idiomatic: It was said that the Yellow Emperor set the pitch standards and calendar. In Han year one, the court compared the Zhuanxu calendar with the six systems: it was loose and imperfect, though still the closest fit. Even so, the official first month and ritual colors were not yet settled.

sentences.zh: 今陰陽不調，宜更曆之過也。」

translations.literal: Now yin and yang are not in harmony; this is because the calendar has errors and should be changed." The edict sent this down to Xianyu Wangren, the envoy in charge of calendars, for questioning. His response on affairs was the best in clepsydra calculations.
translations.idiomatic: "Now yin and yang are out of balance; the calendar is at fault and should be revised." The throne ordered Xianyu Wangren, the official responsible for calendrical matters, to examine the issue. His reply was judged best, including in water-clock calculations.

sentences.zh: 詔下主曆使者鮮于妄人詰問，壽王不服。

translations.literal: Wangren requested to participate in regulating the calendar. He was then summoned and questioned; his statements were sent down together with Shouwang’s.
translations.idiomatic: Wangren asked to help reform the calendar. He was summoned for questioning, and his proposals were circulated alongside Zhang Shouwang’s.

sentences.zh: 妄人請與治曆大司農中丞麻光等二十餘人雜候日月晦朔弦望、八節二十四氣，鈞校諸曆用狀。

translations.literal: Wangren considered: "Those who made calendars in high antiquity took the qi of Heaven as their basis and followed the Pole of the Dipper."
translations.idiomatic: Wangren argued: "The sages who created calendars in antiquity based them on heavenly qi and aligned them with the Dipper’s pole."

sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The throne approved the proposal.

sentences.zh: 詔與丞相、御史、大將軍、右將軍史各一人雜候上林清臺，課諸曆疏密，凡十一家。

translations.literal: An edict ordered [him] together with the Chancellor, Censor-in-Chief, General-in-Chief, General of the Right, court historians, and erudites to jointly observe stars and calendar.
translations.idiomatic: An edict ordered joint observations of stars and calendrical data by the chancellor, censor-in-chief, commander-in-chief, general of the right, court historians, and erudites.

sentences.zh: 以元鳳三年十一月朔旦冬至，盡五年十二月，各有第。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month of the third year of Yuanfeng, on the first day, at dawn, was the winter solstice; the sun and moon were in Jian constellation.
translations.idiomatic: They determined that in Yuanfeng 3, on the first day of the eleventh month, the winter solstice occurred at dawn, with sun and moon in the Jian asterism.

sentences.zh: 壽王課疏遠。

translations.literal: Shouwang’s computations were sparse and far off.
translations.idiomatic: Shouwang’s calculations proved rough and inaccurate.

sentences.zh: 案漢元年不用黃帝調曆，壽王非漢曆，逆天道，非所宜言，大不敬。

translations.literal: On investigation, in the first year of Han they did not use the Yellow Emperor harmonized calendar.
translations.idiomatic: An inquiry showed that the Han court had not used the Yellow Emperor-derived tuned calendar in its first year.

sentences.zh: 有詔勿劾。

translations.literal: There was an edict not to impeach [him].
translations.idiomatic: An edict ordered that no impeachment be brought.

sentences.zh: 復候，盡六年。

translations.literal: They resumed observing, completing six full years.
translations.idiomatic: Observations were resumed and continued for a full six years.

sentences.zh: 太初曆第一，即墨徐萬且、長安徐禹治太初曆亦第一。

translations.literal: At the first year of the Taichu calendar, sun and moon joined like paired jade discs, and the five planets were like linked pearls.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year under the Taichu calendar, sun and moon were in conjunction, and the five planets aligned like a string of pearls.

sentences.zh: 壽王及待詔李信治黃帝調曆，課皆疏闊，又言黃帝至元鳳三年六千餘歲。

translations.literal: Shouwang and Expectant Appointee Li Xin worked on the Yellow Emperor harmonized calendar.
translations.idiomatic: Shouwang and Li Xin, an expectant appointee, worked on the Yellow Emperor-style tuned calendar.

sentences.zh: 丞相屬寶、長安單安國、安陵桮育治終始，言黃帝以來三千六百二十九歲，不與壽王合。

translations.literal: The Chancellor’s subordinate Bao, Shan Anguo of Chang’an, and Bei Yu of Anling worked on beginning-and-end [calculations].
translations.idiomatic: Bao from the chancellor’s office, Shan Anguo of Chang’an, and Bei Yu of Anling handled calculations of cyclical beginnings and endings.

sentences.zh: 壽王又移帝王錄，舜、禹年歲不合人年。

translations.literal: Shouwang further altered the records of sovereigns, and the years for Shun and Yu did not match human years.
translations.idiomatic: Shouwang also revised the imperial chronologies, assigning reign lengths to Shun and Yu that did not accord with normal human lifespans.

sentences.zh: 壽王言化益為天子代禹，驪山女亦為天子，在殷周間，皆不合經術。

translations.literal: Shouwang said that Hua Yi became Son of Heaven succeeding Yu, and that a woman of Lishan also became Son of Heaven, in the interval between Yin and Zhou; none of this accords with the classical arts.
translations.idiomatic: Shouwang claimed that Hua Yi succeeded Yu as Son of Heaven, and even that a woman from Mount Li ruled as Son of Heaven between Shang and Zhou; such claims matched no recognized classical learning.

sentences.zh: 壽王曆乃太史官殷曆也。

translations.literal: Shouwang’s calendar was in fact the Yin calendar of the Grand Historian office.
translations.idiomatic: Shouwang’s system was simply the old Yin calendar kept by the Office of the Grand Astrologer.

sentences.zh: 壽王猥曰安得五家曆，又妄言太初曆虧四分日之三，去小餘七百五分，以故陰陽不調，謂之亂世。

translations.literal: Shouwang rashly said, "How could one obtain the calendars of the five schools?" and further falsely claimed that the Taichu calendar was deficient by three quarters of a quarter-day, subtracting 705 small fractions; therefore yin and yang were not in harmony, calling it an age in disorder.
translations.idiomatic: Shouwang recklessly spoke of “recovering the five-family calendars,” and falsely argued that the Taichu system was short by calendrical fractions, which supposedly threw yin and yang out of balance and marked the age as chaotic.

sentences.zh: 劾壽王吏八百石，古之大夫，服儒衣，誦不詳之辭，作祅言欲亂制度，不道。

translations.literal: They impeached Shouwang: an official of 800-shi rank, equivalent to an ancient grandee, wearing Confucian robes, reciting poorly attested words, making ominous statements to disrupt institutions, contrary to the Way.
translations.idiomatic: The impeachment charged that Shouwang, an 800-shi official of grandee standing, donned Confucian dress while reciting dubious claims and spreading ominous talk to unsettle state institutions, conduct contrary to proper principle.

sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The throne approved the impeachment memorial.

sentences.zh: 壽王候課，比三年下，終不服。

translations.literal: In observation and assessment, Shouwang ranked below over three years, yet in the end he would not submit.
translations.idiomatic: After three years of comparative observations, Shouwang’s results consistently ranked lower, but he still refused to concede.

sentences.zh: 再劾死，更赦勿劾，遂不更言，誹謗益甚，竟以下吏。

translations.literal: He was impeached a second time with a death penalty; then again pardoned with an order not to impeach. He then no longer spoke, but his slander grew even worse, and in the end he was handed to judicial officials.
translations.idiomatic: He was impeached again with capital punishment recommended, then pardoned once more under orders to stop further impeachment. He ceased open argument, but his slander intensified, and he was eventually turned over to the courts.

sentences.zh: 故曆本之驗在於天，自漢曆初起，盡元鳳六年，三十六歲，而是非堅定。

translations.literal: Therefore, verification of calendar fundamentals lies in Heaven. From the beginning of the Han calendar through the sixth year of Yuanfeng was thirty-six years, and right and wrong became firmly settled.
translations.idiomatic: Thus calendrical principles are ultimately tested against the heavens. From the start of Han calendrical reform to Yuanfeng 6, thirty-six years passed, and the dispute over right and wrong was decisively settled.

sentences.zh: 至孝成世，劉向總六曆，列是非，作五紀論。

translations.literal: By the reign of Emperor Cheng, Liu Xiang gathered the six calendars, listed right and wrong, and composed the Discussion of the Five Epochs.
translations.idiomatic: By Emperor Cheng’s time, Liu Xiang had collated the six calendrical systems, sorted out their merits and faults, and wrote the Treatise on the Five Epochs.

sentences.zh: 向子歆究其微眇，作三統曆及譜以說春秋，推法密要，故述焉。

translations.literal: Xiang’s son Xin probed their subtle minutiae and made the Santong Calendar and tables to explain the Spring and Autumn; his computational methods were dense and essential, therefore they are recorded here.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang’s son Liu Xin examined the finest technical details, produced the Santong Calendar and accompanying tables to interpret the *Spring and Autumn Annals*, and developed compact, crucial computational methods; therefore they are set forth here.

sentences.zh: 夫曆春秋者，天時也，列人事而目以天時。

translations.literal: As for calendaring the Spring and Autumn, it is heavenly timing: it arrays human affairs and indexes them by heavenly times.
translations.idiomatic: In the *Spring and Autumn* system, chronology is grounded in celestial time: human events are listed and marked according to heavenly timing.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「民受天地之中以生，所謂命也。

translations.literal: The Commentary says: "The people receive life from the central harmony of Heaven and Earth; this is what is called mandate.
translations.idiomatic: The *Commentary* says: "Human beings receive life from the balanced center of Heaven and Earth; this is what is called one’s mandate.

sentences.zh: 是故有禮誼動作威儀之則以定命也，能者養以之福，不能者敗以取禍。」

translations.literal: Therefore there are standards of rites, rightness, movement, and dignified bearing by which to settle that mandate. Those who are able nurture it and thereby gain blessing; those who are not fail and thereby incur misfortune."
translations.idiomatic: Therefore norms of ritual, propriety, conduct, and bearing are established to stabilize that mandate. The capable cultivate it and gain blessing; the incapable ruin it and bring misfortune on themselves."

sentences.zh: 故列十二公二百四十二年之事，以陰陽之中制其禮。

translations.literal: Therefore it lists the affairs of the twelve dukes over 242 years, using the centrality of yin and yang to regulate their rites.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, it records the affairs of the twelve dukes across 242 years, using the balance of yin and yang as the standard for ordering ritual.

sentences.zh: 故春為陽中，萬物以生；

translations.literal: Therefore spring is within yang, and the myriad things are thereby born;
translations.idiomatic: Spring is the season centered in yang, when all things come into being;

sentences.zh: 秋為陰中，萬物以成。

translations.literal: autumn is within yin, and the myriad things are thereby completed.
translations.idiomatic: autumn is centered in yin, when all things come to completion.

sentences.zh: 是以事舉其中，禮取其和，曆數以閏正天地之中，以作事厚生，皆所以定命也。

translations.literal: Therefore in affairs one raises the center, in rites one takes harmony, and calendrical numbers use intercalation to rectify the center of Heaven and Earth, so as to carry out affairs and enrich life; all are means by which mandate is settled.
translations.idiomatic: Hence policy must hold to central balance and ritual must seek harmony. Intercalary adjustments in calendrical calculation correct the median of Heaven and Earth, enabling governance and the nurturing of life; all this serves to stabilize the Mandate.

sentences.zh: 易金火相革之卦曰「湯武革命，順乎天而應乎人」，又曰「治曆明時」，所以和人道也。

translations.literal: The *Changes*, in the hexagram of revolution where metal and fire transform each other, says: "Tang and Wu revolutionized the mandate, complying with Heaven and responding to men," and also says, "regulate the calendar and make the seasons clear"; this is how to harmonize the human Way.
translations.idiomatic: The *Book of Changes* says in the Revolution hexagram: "Tang and Wu transformed the Mandate, following Heaven and answering the people," and also, "set the calendar in order and clarify the seasons." This is how the human order is brought into harmony.

sentences.zh: 周道既衰，幽王既喪，天子不能班朔，魯曆不正，以閏餘一之歲為蔀首。

translations.literal: After the Zhou Way had already declined and King You had fallen, the Son of Heaven could no longer promulgate the first day of the month. The Lu calendar was incorrect, taking the year with an intercalary remainder of one as the epoch-head.
translations.idiomatic: Once the Zhou order decayed and King You was lost, the Son of Heaven could no longer issue the monthly calendar. Lu’s calendar went astray, using a flawed intercalary-remainder year as its epoch point.

sentences.zh: 故春秋刺「十一月乙亥朔，日有食之」。

translations.literal: Therefore the *Spring and Autumn* censures: "On the first day of the eleventh month, yihai, there was a solar eclipse."
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, the *Spring and Autumn Annals* marks this as a fault: "On yihai, first day of the eleventh month, there was a solar eclipse."

sentences.zh: 於是辰在申，而司曆以為在建戌，史書建亥。

translations.literal: At that time the celestial marker was at shen, yet the calendar officials took it to be in jianxu, and the scribes recorded jianhai.
translations.idiomatic: The asterism should then have been in shen, but calendrical officials treated it as jian-xu, while the historical record entered it as jian-hai.

sentences.zh: 哀十二年，亦以建申流火之月為建亥，而怪蟄蟲之不伏也。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year of Ai, they also took the month of flowing fire with jianshen as jianhai, and then found it strange that hibernating insects did not go into concealment.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ai’s twelfth year, they likewise misidentified the jian-shen “flowing fire” month as jian-hai, then wondered why the hibernating insects had not yet gone dormant.

sentences.zh: 自文公閏月不告朔，至此百有餘年，莫能正曆數。

translations.literal: From the time Duke Wen did not announce the first day in an intercalary month until this point, more than a hundred years passed, and none could correct the calendar calculations.
translations.idiomatic: From Duke Wen’s failure to proclaim the new moon in an intercalary month to this point, over a century elapsed with no one able to rectify the calendrical reckoning.

sentences.zh: 故子貢欲去其餼羊，孔子愛其禮，而著其法於春秋。

translations.literal: Therefore Zigong wished to remove the sacrificial sheep; Confucius treasured that rite and recorded its method in the *Spring and Autumn*.
translations.idiomatic: This is why Zigong wanted to abolish the offering sheep, while Confucius valued the rite and preserved its rule in the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.

sentences.zh: 經曰：「冬十月朔，日有食之。」

translations.literal: The Classic says: "In winter, tenth month, first day, there was a solar eclipse."
translations.idiomatic: The Annals state: "In winter, first day of the tenth month, there was a solar eclipse."

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「不書日，官失之也。

translations.literal: The Commentary says: "That the day is not written means the officials failed in it.
translations.idiomatic: The Commentary says: "When the exact day is not recorded, it means the officials failed in their duty.

sentences.zh: 天子有日官，諸侯有日御，日官居卿以厎日，禮也。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven has sun-officials; the feudal lords have sun-attendants. Sun-officials hold the rank of minister to determine days; this is ritual.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven employs day officials, and the feudal lords have day attendants. Day officials hold ministerial rank to determine dates; that is the proper rite.

sentences.zh: 日御不失日以授百官於朝。」

translations.literal: Day attendants do not miss the day and use it to convey [the date] to the hundred officials at court."
translations.idiomatic: Day attendants must not miss the date; they report it to the assembled officials at court."

sentences.zh: 言告朔也。

translations.literal: This speaks of announcing the new moon.
translations.idiomatic: This refers to the proclamation of the first day of the lunar month.

sentences.zh: 元典曆始曰元。

translations.literal: In the canonical calendar system, the beginning is called yuan (origin).
translations.idiomatic: In the foundational calendrical canon, the starting point is called *yuan*, “origin.”

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「元，善之長也。」

translations.literal: The Commentary says: "Yuan is the chief of what is good."
translations.idiomatic: The Commentary says, "Origin is the foremost principle of goodness."

sentences.zh: 共養三德為善。

translations.literal: Nurturing the three virtues together constitutes goodness.
translations.idiomatic: Goodness consists in jointly cultivating the three virtues.

sentences.zh: 又曰：「元，體之長也。」

translations.literal: It also says: "Yuan is the chief of embodied form."
translations.idiomatic: It also says, "Origin is the leading principle of embodied existence."

sentences.zh: 合三體而為之原，故曰元。

translations.literal: Combining the three bodies to make their source, therefore it is called yuan.
translations.idiomatic: Because it unites the three cosmic bodies into a single source, it is called *yuan*.

sentences.zh: 於春三月，每月書王，元之三統也。

translations.literal: In the three spring months, each month writes "king"; these are the three unifications of yuan.
translations.idiomatic: Across spring’s three months, each month is marked with “king”; this reflects origin’s threefold system.

sentences.zh: 三統合於一元，故因元一而九三之以為法，十一三之以為實。

translations.literal: The three systems unite in one yuan; therefore taking yuan as one, multiply nine by three to make the divisor, and eleven by three to make the dividend.
translations.idiomatic: The three systems converge into one origin. So, from one *yuan*, one sets 9 x 3 as the divisor and 11 x 3 as the dividend.

sentences.zh: 實如法得一。

translations.literal: When the dividend is as the method, one is obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Dividing the dividend by that rule yields one.

sentences.zh: 黃鐘初九，律之首，陽之變也。

translations.literal: Huangzhong is initial nine, the head of the lü-pitches, and the transformation of yang.
translations.idiomatic: Huangzhong is “initial nine,” the first of the regular pitch-pipes and a transformation of yang.

sentences.zh: 因而六之，以九為法，得林鐘初六，呂之首，陰之變也。

translations.literal: From this, multiply by six, with nine as the divisor, obtaining Linzhong initial six, the head of the lü-tones, and the transformation of yin.
translations.idiomatic: From there, multiplying by six and dividing by nine gives Linzhong as “initial six,” first of the complementary tones and a transformation of yin.

sentences.zh: 皆參天兩地之法也。

translations.literal: All are methods of Heaven-three and Earth-two.
translations.idiomatic: All this follows the ratio principle of “three for Heaven, two for Earth.”

sentences.zh: 上生六而倍之，下生六而損之，皆以九為法。

translations.literal: For upward generation, multiply by six and double it; for downward generation, multiply by six and diminish it; all use nine as the divisor.
translations.idiomatic: In upward generation one multiplies by six and doubles; in downward generation one multiplies by six and reduces. In all cases, nine is the divisor.

sentences.zh: 九六，陰陽夫婦子母之道也。

translations.literal: Nine and six are the way of yin and yang as husband and wife, child and mother.
translations.idiomatic: Nine and six encode the yin-yang pattern of husband and wife, mother and child.

sentences.zh: 律娶妻而呂生子，天地之情也。

translations.literal: The lü takes a wife and the lü produces a child; this is the nature of Heaven and Earth.
translations.idiomatic: The regular pitch “takes a wife,” and the complementary pitch “bears a child”; this mirrors the generative relation of Heaven and Earth.

sentences.zh: 六律六呂，而十二辰立矣。

translations.literal: With six lü and six lü, the twelve chen are established.
translations.idiomatic: With six regular pitches and six complementary pitches, the twelve earthly branches are set in place.

sentences.zh: 五聲清濁，而十日行矣。

translations.literal: With the five tones distinguished as clear and turbid, the ten-day sequence proceeds.
translations.idiomatic: Once the five tones are ordered into clear and turbid registers, the ten-day cycle can operate.

sentences.zh: 傳曰「天六地五」，數之常也。

translations.literal: The Commentary says, "Heaven six, Earth five"; this is the constant of number.
translations.idiomatic: The Commentary says, "Heaven is six, Earth is five"; this is the fixed numerical pattern.

sentences.zh: 天有六氣，降生五味。

translations.literal: Heaven has six qi, descending to generate the five flavors.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven possesses six vital influences, which descend and produce the five flavors.

sentences.zh: 夫五六者，天地之中合，而民所受以生也。

translations.literal: As for five and six: they are the central union of Heaven and Earth, and what the people receive in order to live.
translations.idiomatic: Five and six form Heaven and Earth in balanced union, and that is what people receive as the basis of life.

sentences.zh: 故日有六甲，辰有五子，十一而天地之道畢，言終而復始。

translations.literal: Therefore days have six jia, and time-markers have five zi; with eleven, the Way of Heaven and Earth is complete, meaning it ends and returns to begin again.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the day cycle has six jia and the branch cycle has five zi: together they make eleven, completing the pattern of Heaven and Earth, which ends only to begin again.

sentences.zh: 太極中央元氣，故為黃鐘，其實一龠，以其長自乘，故八十一為日法，所以生權衡度量，禮樂之所繇出也。

translations.literal: The Great Ultimate is central primal qi, therefore it is Huangzhong. Its capacity is one yue; with its length multiplied by itself, therefore eighty-one is the day-divisor. From this are generated weights, balances, measures of length and volume; rites and music all issue from this.
translations.idiomatic: At the center of the Great Ultimate is primal qi, represented by Huangzhong. Its standard capacity is one yue, and its length squared gives eighty-one as the calendrical day rule. From this derive weights and measures, and from these in turn come ritual and music.

sentences.zh: 經元一以統始，易太極之首也。

translations.literal: The Classic takes origin as one to unify beginnings; this is the head of the Great Ultimate in the Changes.
translations.idiomatic: The Classic sets origin at one to govern all beginnings; this corresponds to the Great Ultimate at the start of the *Changes*.

sentences.zh: 春秋二以目歲，易兩儀之中也。

translations.literal: The Spring and Autumn uses two to mark the year; this is the middle of the Two Modes in the Changes.
translations.idiomatic: The *Spring and Autumn* uses a dual structure to mark the year, matching the Two Modes in the *Changes*.

sentences.zh: 於春每月書王，易三極之統也。

translations.literal: In spring, each month writes "king"; this is the unity of the three ultimates in the Changes.
translations.idiomatic: Writing “king” in each spring month reflects the *Changes* principle of the three ultimate powers brought under one governance.

sentences.zh: 於四時雖亡事必書時月，易四象之節也。

translations.literal: In the four seasons, even when there is no event, the season and month must be recorded; this is the seasonal articulation of the four images in the Changes.
translations.idiomatic: Across all four seasons, even uneventful periods still record season and month; that follows the *Changes* pattern of the Four Images and their divisions.

sentences.zh: 時月以建分至啟閉之分，易八卦之位也。

translations.literal: Season and month use the established solstices, equinoxes, opening and closing divisions; this is the placement of the eight trigrams in the Changes.
translations.idiomatic: Seasonal and monthly records mark the divisions of solstices, equinoxes, opening, and closing; this mirrors the positional scheme of the Eight Trigrams.

sentences.zh: 象事成敗，易吉凶之效也。

translations.literal: Images events as success or failure; this is the effect of auspicious and inauspicious in the Changes.
translations.idiomatic: By representing events as success or failure, it enacts the *Changes* logic of auspicious and inauspicious outcomes.

sentences.zh: 朝聘會盟，易大業之本也。

translations.literal: Court visits, diplomatic calls, meetings, and covenants are the root of great undertakings in the Changes.
translations.idiomatic: Court audiences, embassies, assemblies, and alliances form the basis of major political action, as in the *Changes*.

sentences.zh: 故易與春秋，天人之道也。

translations.literal: Therefore the Changes and the Spring and Autumn are the Way of Heaven and humankind.
translations.idiomatic: Thus the *Changes* and the *Spring and Autumn* together articulate the Way linking Heaven and humanity.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「龜，象也。

translations.literal: The Commentary says: "The turtle is image.
translations.idiomatic: The Commentary says: "The turtle shell signifies image.

sentences.zh: 筮，數也。

translations.literal: Divination by milfoil is number.
translations.idiomatic: Milfoil divination is fundamentally a matter of number.

sentences.zh: 物生而後有象，象而後有滋，滋而後有數。」

translations.literal: Only after things are born are there images; after images, there is growth; after growth, there are numbers."
translations.idiomatic: Things come into being, then take form; form develops, and only then do numbers arise."

sentences.zh: 是故元始有象一也，春秋二也，三統三也，四時四也，合而為十，成五體。

translations.literal: Therefore at the beginning of origin there is image as one; Spring and Autumn is two; three unifications are three; four seasons are four. Combined they make ten and complete the five bodies.
translations.idiomatic: Thus: primal origin gives one as image, the *Spring and Autumn* gives two, the three systems give three, and the four seasons give four. Together they make ten and complete the five structural elements.

sentences.zh: 以五乘十，大衍之數也，而道據其一，其餘四十九，所當用也，故蓍以為數。

translations.literal: Using five times ten gives the number of Great Expansion. Yet the Way takes one as its basis; the remaining forty-nine are what should be used, therefore milfoil is taken as number.
translations.idiomatic: Five times ten yields the Great Expansion number. But the Way rests on one, leaving forty-nine for operative use; hence milfoil stalks serve as the numerical tool.

sentences.zh: 以象兩兩之，又以象三三之，又以象四四之，又歸奇象閏十九及所據一加之，因以再扐兩之，是為月法之實。

translations.literal: Use images to divide by twos, then by threes, then by fours; then return the odd to image the leap month as nineteen, and add the one on which it is based; then because of this, perform the second tallying and divide by two. This is the dividend of the month-rule.
translations.idiomatic: One models the system through successive pairings, triplings, and quadruplings, then returns the odd remainder to represent intercalation as nineteen, adding the single base unit. A second tallying and halving then yields the monthly-rule dividend.

sentences.zh: 如日法得一，則一月之日數也，而三辰之會交矣，是以能生吉凶。

translations.literal: If, as with the day-rule, one is obtained, then this is the number of days in one month, and the conjunction of the three celestial markers intersects; therefore it can generate auspicious and inauspicious outcomes.
translations.idiomatic: When this produces one under the same procedure as the day-rule, it gives the days per month. At that point the three celestial indicators meet in conjunction, and prognostications of fortune or misfortune become possible.

sentences.zh: 故《易》曰：「天一地二，天三地四，天五地六，天七地八，天九地十。

translations.literal: Therefore the *Changes* says: "Heaven one, Earth two; Heaven three, Earth four; Heaven five, Earth six; Heaven seven, Earth eight; Heaven nine, Earth ten.
translations.idiomatic: So the *Changes* says: "Heaven is one and Earth is two; Heaven three and Earth four; Heaven five and Earth six; Heaven seven and Earth eight; Heaven nine and Earth ten.

sentences.zh: 天數五，地數五，五位相得而各有合。

translations.literal: Heaven-numbers are five, Earth-numbers are five; the five positions mutually obtain and each has its pairing.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven has five numbers and Earth has five; the five positions correspond and each finds its proper match.

sentences.zh: 天數二十有五，地數三十，凡天地之數五十有五，此所以成變化而行鬼神也。」

translations.literal: Heaven’s numbers are twenty-five, Earth’s numbers are thirty. In total the numbers of Heaven and Earth are fifty-five. This is whereby transformations are completed and spirits are set in motion."
translations.idiomatic: Heaven’s numbers total twenty-five and Earth’s thirty; together they are fifty-five. By this, transformations are accomplished and the numinous powers operate."

sentences.zh: 并終數為十九，易窮則變，故為閏法。

translations.literal: Combining final numbers gives nineteen. In the Changes, when exhaustively reached it transforms; therefore this is made the intercalation-rule.
translations.idiomatic: The terminal sum is nineteen. Since the *Changes* teaches that exhaustion leads to transformation, nineteen is used as the intercalation rule.

sentences.zh: 參天九，兩地十，是為會數。

translations.literal: Triple Heaven gives nine, double Earth gives ten; this is the conjunction number.
translations.idiomatic: Threefold Heaven gives nine and twofold Earth gives ten; together they form the conjunction number.

sentences.zh: 參天數二十五，兩地數三十，是為朔望之會。

translations.literal: Triple Heaven-number twenty-five, double Earth-number thirty; this is the conjunction of new moon and full moon.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven’s threefold number is twenty-five and Earth’s twofold number is thirty; this defines the conjunction cycle of new and full moon.

sentences.zh: 以會數乘之，則周於朔旦冬至，是為會月。

translations.literal: Multiply by the conjunction number, then it cycles to new-moon dawn winter-solstice; this is the conjunction month.
translations.idiomatic: Multiply by this conjunction number and the cycle returns to a winter-solstice new moon at dawn; this is called the conjunction month.

sentences.zh: 九會而復元，黃鐘初九之數也。

translations.literal: After nine conjunctions it returns to origin; this is the number of Huangzhong initial nine.
translations.idiomatic: After nine conjunction cycles it returns to origin, matching Huangzhong’s “initial nine” number.

sentences.zh: 經於四時，雖亡事必書時月。

translations.literal: In the Classic, through the four seasons, even with no event one must record season and month.
translations.idiomatic: In the Annals, season and month must always be recorded through all four seasons, even when no event occurs.

sentences.zh: 時所以記啟閉也，月所以紀分至也。

translations.literal: Seasons are used to record opening and closing; months are used to mark equinoxes and solstices.
translations.idiomatic: Seasonal markers record opening and closing phases, while monthly markers track equinoxes and solstices.

sentences.zh: 啟閉者，節也。

translations.literal: Opening and closing are the nodes.
translations.idiomatic: Opening and closing define the seasonal nodes.

sentences.zh: 分至者，中也。

translations.literal: Equinoxes and solstices are the center.
translations.idiomatic: Equinoxes and solstices are the central points.

sentences.zh: 節不必在其月，故時中必在正數之月。

translations.literal: A node need not fall within its month; therefore the central point in season must fall in the month of correct count.
translations.idiomatic: A seasonal node does not always land within its nominal month, so the true midpoint must be fixed in the properly numbered month.

sentences.zh: 故傳曰：「先王之正時也，履端於始，舉正於中，歸餘於終。

translations.literal: Therefore the Commentary says: "When the former kings corrected time, they stepped on the beginning at the start, raised correctness at the middle, and returned surplus at the end.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the Commentary says: "When the sage kings regulated time, they established the beginning at the outset, maintained correctness at the center, and assigned the remainder at the end.

sentences.zh: 履端於始，序則不愆；

translations.literal: Stepping on the beginning at the start, sequence then does not err;
translations.idiomatic: If the outset is correctly established, sequence does not go wrong;

sentences.zh: 舉正於中，民則不惑；

translations.literal: raising correctness at the middle, the people then are not confused;
translations.idiomatic: if correctness is upheld at mid-course, the people are not confused;

sentences.zh: 歸餘於終，事則不誖。」

translations.literal: returning surplus at the end, affairs then are not perverse."
translations.idiomatic: if surplus is assigned at the end, affairs do not become disordered."

sentences.zh: 此聖王之重閏也。

translations.literal: This is why sage kings valued intercalation.
translations.idiomatic: This is why the sage kings treated intercalation as so important.

sentences.zh: 以五位乘會數，而朔旦冬至，是為章月。

translations.literal: Using the five positions multiplied by the conjunction number, one reaches winter-solstice new-moon dawn; this is called the zhang-month.
translations.idiomatic: Multiplying the conjunction number by the five positions returns to a dawn new moon at winter solstice; this defines the zhang month-cycle.

sentences.zh: 四分月法，以其一乘章月，是為中法。

translations.literal: Quartering the month-rule, and multiplying zhang-month by that one part, this is called the middle-rule.
translations.idiomatic: Taking one quarter of the month rule and multiplying it by the zhang cycle yields what is called the middle rule.

sentences.zh: 參閏法為周至，以乘月法，以減中法而約之，則六扐之數，為一月之閏法，其餘七分，此中朔相求之術也。

translations.literal: Triple the intercalation-rule as the complete-solstice cycle; multiply by the month-rule; subtract the middle-rule and reduce it. Then the number of six tallyings is the intercalation-rule for one month, with seven parts remaining. This is the method for seeking correspondence between central point and new moon.
translations.idiomatic: Set three intercalation rules as a full-solstice cycle, multiply by the month rule, subtract the middle rule, and reduce. The result gives six tally-units as the monthly intercalation rule, with seven residual parts. This is the technique for reconciling midpoints and new moons.

sentences.zh: 朔不得中，是謂閏月，言陰陽雖交，不得中不生。

translations.literal: When new moon does not obtain the center, this is called an intercalary month: although yin and yang intersect, without obtaining the center there is no generation.
translations.idiomatic: If the new moon misses the central point, that month is intercalary: yin and yang may meet, but without central alignment no proper generation occurs.

sentences.zh: 故日法乘閏法，是為統歲。

translations.literal: Therefore the day-rule multiplied by the intercalation-rule gives the unified year.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, multiplying the day rule by the intercalation rule yields the integrated year count.

sentences.zh: 三統，是為元歲。

translations.literal: Three unifications: this is called origin-year.
translations.idiomatic: Across the three systems, this is called the origin year.

sentences.zh: 元歲之閏，陰陽災，三統閏法。

translations.literal: The intercalations of the origin-year are yin-yang calamities, according to the intercalation method of the three systems.
translations.idiomatic: Intercalary years within the origin cycle are treated as yin-yang calamity points under the three-system intercalation method.

sentences.zh: 易九厄曰：初入元，百六，陽九；

translations.literal: The *Changes* on the nine calamities says: first entering the origin, after 106, yang-nine;
translations.idiomatic: The *Changes* doctrine of the nine calamities says: at initial entry into the origin cycle, after 106 years comes a “yang-nine” crisis;

sentences.zh: 次三百七十四，陰九；

translations.literal: next 374, yin-nine;
translations.idiomatic: then after 374 years, a “yin-nine” crisis;

sentences.zh: 次四百八十，陽九；

translations.literal: next 480, yang-nine;
translations.idiomatic: then after 480 years, another “yang-nine”;

sentences.zh: 次七百二十，陰七；

translations.literal: next 720, yin-seven;
translations.idiomatic: then after 720 years, a “yin-seven”;

sentences.zh: 次七百二十，陽七；

translations.literal: next 720, yang-seven;
translations.idiomatic: after another 720 years, a “yang-seven”;

sentences.zh: 次六百，陰五；

translations.literal: next 600, yin-five;
translations.idiomatic: then after 600 years, a “yin-five”;

sentences.zh: 次六百，陽五；

translations.literal: next 600, yang-five;
translations.idiomatic: after another 600 years, a “yang-five”;

sentences.zh: 次四百八十，陰三；

translations.literal: next 480, yin-three;
translations.idiomatic: then after 480 years, a “yin-three”;

sentences.zh: 次四百八十，陽三。

translations.literal: next 480, yang-three.
translations.idiomatic: then after 480 years, a “yang-three.”

sentences.zh: 凡四千六百一十七歲，與一元終。

translations.literal: In all, 4,617 years end together with one origin-cycle.
translations.idiomatic: Altogether, one full origin cycle ends at 4,617 years.

sentences.zh: 經歲四千五百六十，災歲五十七。

translations.literal: Regular years are 4,560; calamity years are 57.
translations.idiomatic: Of these, 4,560 are regular years and 57 are calamity years.

sentences.zh: 是以春秋曰：「舉正於中。」

translations.literal: Therefore the Spring and Autumn says: "Raise correctness in the middle."
translations.idiomatic: This is why the *Spring and Autumn* says, "Maintain correctness at the center."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「閏月不告朔，非禮也。

translations.literal: It also says: "If in an intercalary month the new moon is not announced, it is contrary to ritual.
translations.idiomatic: It also says, "If the new moon is not proclaimed in an intercalary month, that violates ritual.

sentences.zh: 閏以正時，時以作事，事以厚生，生民之道於是乎在矣。

translations.literal: Intercalation is used to rectify time; time is used to carry out affairs; affairs are used to enrich life. Herein lies the Way for generating the people.
translations.idiomatic: Intercalation corrects time, time enables governance, and governance nourishes life; in this lies the proper Way of sustaining the people.

sentences.zh: 不告閏朔，棄時正也，何以為民？」

translations.literal: If one does not announce the intercalary new moon, one abandons temporal correctness; how can one govern the people?"
translations.idiomatic: "If intercalary new moons are not proclaimed, proper timekeeping is discarded; how then can the people be governed?"

sentences.zh: 故善僖「五年春王正月辛亥朔，日南至，公既視朔，遂登觀臺以望，而書，禮也。

translations.literal: Therefore [the Commentary] praises Xi: "In the fifth year, spring, royal first month, on xinhai new moon, the sun reached the south. After the duke had observed the new moon, he then ascended the watchtower to observe and had it recorded; this was ritual propriety.
translations.idiomatic: Thus it commends Duke Xi: "In the fifth year, spring, on xinhai, first day of the royal first month, the sun reached its southern limit. After observing the new moon, the duke ascended the observatory to make observations and had them entered in the record; this accorded with ritual.

sentences.zh: 凡分至啟閉，必書雲物，為備故也。」

translations.literal: Whenever there are equinoxes, solstices, openings, and closings, one must record cloud and atmospheric phenomena, to provide against contingencies."
translations.idiomatic: Whenever equinoxes, solstices, and seasonal opening-closing points occur, cloud and atmospheric signs must be recorded as preparedness against anomalies."

sentences.zh: 至昭二十年二月己丑，日南至，失閏，至在非其月。

translations.literal: By Duke Zhao year twenty, second month, jichou day, the sun reached the south; intercalation was missed, and the solstice occurred in a month that was not its own.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhao’s twentieth year, second month, jichou day, the sun reached its southern extreme; because intercalation had been missed, the solstice fell in the wrong month.

sentences.zh: 梓慎望氛氣而弗正，不履端於始也。

translations.literal: Zishen observed vapors and qi but did not correct [the calendar]; he did not tread the beginning at the start.
translations.idiomatic: Zishen observed atmospheric qi but failed to correct the reckoning; he had not set the beginning right from the outset.

sentences.zh: 故傳不曰冬至，而曰日南至。

translations.literal: Therefore the Commentary does not say winter solstice, but says the sun reached the south.
translations.idiomatic: That is why the Commentary does not call it “winter solstice” but instead says “the sun reached the south.”

sentences.zh: 極於牽牛之初，日中之時景最長，以此知其南至也。

translations.literal: At the extreme, at the beginning of Ox-Herd star, at noon the shadow is longest; by this one knows it has reached the south.
translations.idiomatic: At the beginning of the Ox asterism, when the noon shadow is at its greatest length, one knows the sun has reached its southern limit.

sentences.zh: 斗綱之端連貫營室，織女之紀指牽牛之初，以紀日月，故曰星紀。

translations.literal: The tip of the Dipper handle links through Encampment Chamber; the marker of Weaving Maiden points to the beginning of Ox-Herd, to mark sun and moon; therefore it is called Star Marker.
translations.idiomatic: The end of the Dipper’s handle runs through the Encampment and Chamber lodges, and the Weaving Maiden marker points to the start of Ox. This is used to track sun and moon, hence the name “Star Marker.”

sentences.zh: 五星起其初，日月起其中，凡十二次。

translations.literal: The five planets begin from its beginning; sun and moon begin from its middle; in all there are twelve stations.
translations.idiomatic: The five planets are reckoned from its initial point, while sun and moon are reckoned from its midpoint; altogether there are twelve stations.

sentences.zh: 日至其初為節，至其中斗建下為十二辰。

translations.literal: When the sun reaches its beginning, that is a node; when it reaches its middle and the Dipper’s handle points downward, these are the twelve chen.
translations.idiomatic: When the sun reaches a station’s beginning, it marks a seasonal node; when it reaches the middle and the Dipper’s handle establishes below, this yields the twelve branch positions.

sentences.zh: 視其建而知其次。

translations.literal: Observe its establishment and know its station.
translations.idiomatic: By observing where it establishes, one knows which station it is in.

sentences.zh: 故曰「制禮上物，不過十二，天之大數也」。

translations.literal: Therefore it says: "In establishing rites and ranking things above, do not exceed twelve; this is Heaven’s great number."
translations.idiomatic: Thus it is said: "In structuring rites and classifying categories, one does not go beyond twelve; twelve is Heaven’s great number."

sentences.zh: 經曰春王正月，傳曰周正月「火出，於夏為三月，商為四月，周為五月。

translations.literal: The Classic says "spring, royal first month." The Commentary says of Zhou’s first month: "When Fire emerges, in Xia it is third month, in Shang fourth month, in Zhou fifth month.
translations.idiomatic: The Annals say, "Spring, royal first month." The Commentary explains Zhou’s first month: "When the Fire asterism appears, this is the third month in Xia reckoning, the fourth in Shang, and the fifth in Zhou.

sentences.zh: 夏數得天」，得四時之正也。

translations.literal: Xia numbering obtains Heaven," obtaining the correctness of the four seasons.
translations.idiomatic: "Xia’s numbering accords with Heaven," meaning it captures the proper alignment of the four seasons.

sentences.zh: 三代各據一統，明三統常合，而迭為首，登降三統之首，周還五行之道也。

translations.literal: The three dynasties each based themselves on one unification, making clear that the three unifications always combine, yet take turns as first. The ascents and descents of the heads of the three unifications are the cyclical way of the five phases.
translations.idiomatic: Each of the Three Dynasties adopted one governing system, showing that the three systems are always coherent while alternating in precedence. This rising and falling of their leading positions follows the cyclical logic of the Five Phases.

sentences.zh: 故三五相包而生。

translations.literal: Therefore three and five enfold one another and generate.
translations.idiomatic: Thus the triadic and pentadic principles intercontain each other and generate life.

sentences.zh: 天統之正，始施於子半，日萌色赤。

translations.literal: The correctness of Heaven’s system begins to be applied at the midpoint of zi; the sun sprouts and its color is red.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven’s system begins at mid-zi: solar vitality first stirs, marked as red.

sentences.zh: 地統受之於丑初，日肇化而黃，至丑半，日牙化而白。

translations.literal: Earth’s system receives it at the beginning of chou; the sun begins transformation and is yellow. By mid-chou, the sun buds in transformation and is white.
translations.idiomatic: Earth’s system takes over at early chou, when solar transformation first begins and appears yellow; by mid-chou it has budded further and appears white.

sentences.zh: 人統受之於寅初，日孽成而黑，至寅半，日生成而青。

translations.literal: Human system receives it at the beginning of yin; the sun’s growth matures and is black. By mid-yin, the sun generates completion and is blue-green.
translations.idiomatic: The human system receives it at early yin: development ripens and is marked as black; by mid-yin, generative completion appears as blue-green.

sentences.zh: 天施復於子，地化自丑畢於辰，人生自寅成於申。

translations.literal: Heaven’s operation returns at zi; Earth’s transformation starts from chou and completes at chen; human life starts from yin and completes at shen.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven’s action cycles back at zi, Earth’s transformation runs from chou to chen, and human generation runs from yin to shen.

sentences.zh: 故曆數三統，天以甲子，地以甲辰，人以甲申。

translations.literal: Therefore in calendrical numbers of the three systems: Heaven uses jiazi, Earth uses jiachen, Humanity uses jiashen.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, the three calendrical systems take jiazi for Heaven, jiachen for Earth, and jiashen for Humanity.

sentences.zh: 孟仲季迭用事為統首。

translations.literal: Meng, zhong, and ji alternately govern affairs as the head of a system.
translations.idiomatic: First, middle, and last months alternate in administrative primacy as each system’s lead.

sentences.zh: 三微之統既著，而五行自青始，其序亦如之。

translations.literal: Once the system of the three subtle beginnings is manifest, the five phases begin from blue-green, and their sequence is likewise so.
translations.idiomatic: Once the three subtle origins are established, the Five Phases begin with blue-green, and their order follows the same pattern.

sentences.zh: 五行與三統相錯。

translations.literal: The five phases interlace with the three systems.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Phases are interwoven with the three systems.

sentences.zh: 傳曰「天有三辰，地有五行」，然則三統五星可知也。

translations.literal: The Commentary says, "Heaven has three celestial markers; Earth has five phases." Then the three systems and five planets can be known.
translations.idiomatic: As the Commentary says, "Heaven has three celestial markers; Earth has five phases." From this, the relation of three systems and five planets becomes intelligible.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「參五以變，錯綜其數。

translations.literal: The *Changes* says: "Join three and five to transform, and interweave their numbers.
translations.idiomatic: The *Changes* says, "By combining three and five, transformation occurs, and their numbers are cross-woven.

sentences.zh: 通其變，遂成天下之文；

translations.literal: Comprehend its transformations, and one thereby completes the patterns of all under Heaven;
translations.idiomatic: Understand these transformations, and the patterned order of the world is completed;

sentences.zh: 極其數，遂定天下之象。」

translations.literal: Exhaust its numbers, and one thereby fixes the images of all under Heaven."
translations.idiomatic: carry its numbers to the limit, and the world’s configurations are determined."

sentences.zh: 太極運三辰五星於上。

translations.literal: The Great Ultimate moves the three celestial markers and five planets above.
translations.idiomatic: Above, the Great Ultimate sets the three celestial markers and five planets in motion.

sentences.zh: 而元氣轉三統五行於下。

translations.literal: And primal qi turns the three systems and five phases below.
translations.idiomatic: Below, primal qi rotates the three systems and the Five Phases.

sentences.zh: 其於人，皇極統三德五事。

translations.literal: As for humanity, the imperial ultimate governs the three virtues and five affairs.
translations.idiomatic: In human governance, the august ultimate integrates the three virtues and five administrative concerns.

sentences.zh: 故三辰之合於三統也，日合於天統，月合於地統，斗合於人統。

translations.literal: Thus the three celestial markers correspond to the three systems: the sun corresponds to the Heaven system, the moon to the Earth system, and the Dipper to the Human system.
translations.idiomatic: So the three celestial markers match the three systems: sun with Heaven, moon with Earth, and the Dipper with Humanity.

sentences.zh: 五星之合於五行，水合於辰星，火合於熒惑，金合於太白，木合於歲星，土合於填星。

translations.literal: The five planets correspond to the five phases: water corresponds to Chenxing, fire to Yinghuo, metal to Taibai, wood to Suixing, and earth to Tianxing.
translations.idiomatic: The five planets align with the Five Phases: Water with Chenxing, Fire with Yinghuo, Metal with Taibai, Wood with Suixing, and Earth with Tianxing.

sentences.zh: 三辰五星而相經緯也。

translations.literal: The three celestial markers and five planets are warp and woof to one another.
translations.idiomatic: The three celestial markers and five planets interweave as warp and weft.

sentences.zh: 天以一生水，地以二生火，天以三生木，地以四生金，天以五生土。

translations.literal: Heaven by one generates water; Earth by two generates fire; Heaven by three generates wood; Earth by four generates metal; Heaven by five generates earth.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven generates Water through one, Earth generates Fire through two, Heaven generates Wood through three, Earth generates Metal through four, and Heaven generates Earth through five.

sentences.zh: 五勝相乘，以生小周，以乘乾坤之策，而成大周。

translations.literal: The cycles of fivefold conquest multiply one another to generate the minor cycle; multiplying by the tallies of Qian and Kun completes the great cycle.
translations.idiomatic: Mutual multiplication through the Five-Phase overcoming sequence yields the small cycle; multiplied by Qian and Kun tallies, it forms the great cycle.

sentences.zh: 陰陽比類，交錯相成，故九六之變登降於六體。

translations.literal: Yin and yang are grouped by kind; crossing and interlacing, they complete one another. Therefore the nine-six transformations rise and fall through the six bodies.
translations.idiomatic: Yin and yang correlate by category and, through intercrossing, complete each other; thus the transformations of nine and six ascend and descend across the six structural forms.

sentences.zh: 三微而成著，三著而成象，二象十有八變而成卦，四營而成易，為七十二，參三統兩四時相乘之數也。

translations.literal: Three subtleties become manifest; three manifestations become images; two images, through eighteen transformations, become hexagrams. Four operations form the Changes, making seventy-two, the number from three systems times two and four seasons.
translations.idiomatic: Three subtle stages become manifest, three manifest stages become images, and two images become hexagrams through eighteen transformations. Four operations complete the *Changes*, yielding seventy-two, the product of three systems, two principles, and four seasons.

sentences.zh: 參之則得乾之策，兩之則得坤之策。

translations.literal: Tripling it yields the tally of Qian; doubling it yields the tally of Kun.
translations.idiomatic: Tripled, it gives Qian’s tally; doubled, Kun’s.

sentences.zh: 以陽九九之，為六百四十八，以陰六六之，為四百三十二，凡一千八十，陰陽各一卦之微算策也。

translations.literal: Using yang, nine by nine gives 648; using yin, six by six gives 432. In all 1,080: the subtle counting-tallies of one hexagram each for yin and yang.
translations.idiomatic: For yang, 9 x 9 gives 648; for yin, 6 x 6 gives 432. Together they total 1,080, the subtle tally-count for one yin and one yang hexagram.

sentences.zh: 八之，為八千六百四十，而八卦小成。

translations.literal: Multiply by eight to get 8,640, and the eight trigrams are minimally completed.
translations.idiomatic: Multiplying by eight gives 8,640, completing the basic structure of the eight trigrams.

sentences.zh: 引而信之，又八之，為六萬九千一百二十，天地再之，為十三萬八千二百四十，然後大成。

translations.literal: Extending and verifying it, multiply again by eight to get 69,120; doubling for Heaven and Earth gives 138,240; only then is it fully completed.
translations.idiomatic: Extend and confirm the sequence, then multiply by eight again to reach 69,120; doubling for Heaven and Earth gives 138,240, and only then is the system complete.

sentences.zh: 五星會終，觸類而長之，以乘章歲，為二百六十二萬六千五百六十，而與日月會。

translations.literal: At the end of a five-planet conjunction, extending by analogy and multiplying by the zhang-year gives 2,626,560, and this meets with the sun and moon.
translations.idiomatic: At the terminal conjunction of the five planets, extension by category and multiplication by the zhang-year yields 2,626,560, where it coincides with sun and moon cycles.

sentences.zh: 三會為七百八十七萬九千六百八十，而與三統會。

translations.literal: Three such conjunctions make 7,879,680, and this meets with the three systems.
translations.idiomatic: Three conjunction cycles produce 7,879,680, coinciding with the three-system cycle.

sentences.zh: 三統二千三百六十三萬九千四十，而復於太極上元。

translations.literal: With three systems, 23,639,040, and it returns to the Great Ultimate upper origin.
translations.idiomatic: At 23,639,040 across the three systems, the cycle returns to the upper-origin point of the Great Ultimate.

sentences.zh: 九章歲而六之為法，太極上元為實，實如法得一，陰一陽各萬一千五百二十，當萬物氣體之數，天下之能事畢矣。

translations.literal: Take nine zhang-years and multiply by six as divisor; take the Great Ultimate upper-origin as dividend. Dividend as divisor yields one. Yin one and yang one are each 11,520, matching the number of qi-forms of the myriad things; the capacities of all under Heaven are thereby complete.
translations.idiomatic: With nine zhang-years multiplied by six as the rule and the Great Ultimate upper-origin as the dividend, division yields one. Yin and yang each give 11,520, corresponding to the qi-forms of all things; this completes the operational numbers of the world.

sentences.zh: 《律曆志下》

translations.literal: Treatise on Pitch and Calendar, Lower.
translations.idiomatic: Treatise on Pitch Standards and Calendar, Part Two.

sentences.zh: 統母日法八十一。

translations.idiomatic: Primary system day-rule: 81.

translations.literal: At origin beginning, Huangzhong initial nine multiplied by itself, the number of one yue, gives the day-rule.
translations.idiomatic: At the primal beginning, Huangzhong’s initial-nine value is squared; this one-yue number yields the day rule.

sentences.zh: 閏法十九，因為章歲。

translations.literal: Intercalation rule: 19, thereby becoming zhang-years.
translations.idiomatic: Intercalation rule: 19, which defines the zhang-year cycle.

sentences.zh: 合天地終數，得閏法。

translations.literal: Combining Heaven-and-Earth terminal numbers gives the intercalation rule.
translations.idiomatic: The intercalation rule comes from combining Heaven’s and Earth’s terminal numbers.

sentences.zh: 統法千五百三十九。

translations.idiomatic: System divisor: 1,539.

translations.idiomatic: Conjunction number: 47.

translations.idiomatic: Zhang-month count: 235.

sentences.zh: 周天五十六萬二千一百二十。

translations.idiomatic: Full heavenly cycle value: 562,120.

translations.idiomatic: Yearly midpoint count: 12.

translations.idiomatic: Monthly circuit value: 254.

sentences.zh: 以章月加閏法，得月周。

sentences.zh: 朔望之會百三十五。

translations.idiomatic: New-moon/full-moon conjunction value: 135.

sentences.zh: 參天數二十五，兩地數三十，得朔望之會。

translations.idiomatic: Using Heaven’s threefold number 25 and Earth’s twofold number 30 gives the new/full conjunction value.

sentences.zh: 會月六千三百四十五。

translations.literal: Conjunction months: 6,345.
translations.idiomatic: Conjunction-month value: 6,345.

sentences.zh: 以會數乘朔望之會，得會月。

translations.literal: Multiply conjunction number by the conjunction of new and full moon to get conjunction months.
translations.idiomatic: Conjunction number multiplied by the new/full conjunction gives the conjunction-month total.

sentences.zh: 統月萬九千三十五。

translations.idiomatic: System-month total: 19,035.

translations.idiomatic: Three conjunction-month units produce the system-month total.

translations.idiomatic: Origin-month total: 57,105.

translations.idiomatic: Zhang-midpoint value: 228.

translations.idiomatic: System-midpoint total: 18,468.

translations.idiomatic: Ten times the origin-midpoint value minus the full heavenly cycle gives the tally remainder.

sentences.zh: 周至五十七。
