translations.idiomatic: Complete-solstice value: 57.

sentences.zh: 統母。

translations.literal: System matrix.
translations.idiomatic: System matrix base.

translations.literal: Wood and metal multiplied give twelve; this is Jupiter’s small cycle.
translations.idiomatic: Wood and Metal multiplied give 12; this is the small cycle for Jupiter (Suixing).

sentences.zh: 小周乘巛策，為千七百二十八，是為歲星歲數。

translations.literal: Small cycle multiplied by chuan tally gives 1,728; this is Jupiter’s year count.
translations.idiomatic: The small cycle multiplied by the cyclical tally gives 1,728, the year count for Jupiter.

translations.idiomatic: Metal and Fire multiply to 8; multiplying again by Fire gives 16, defining the small return cycle.

sentences.zh: 小復乘乾策，為三千四百五十六，是為太白歲數。

translations.idiomatic: Small return multiplied by the Qian tally gives 3,456, the year count for Venus (Taibai).

sentences.zh: 火經特成，故二歲而過初，三十二過初為六十四歲而小周。

translations.literal: Fire, by warp pattern, is uniquely complete; thus in two years it passes the initial point, and thirty-two such passes are sixty-four years as a small cycle.
translations.idiomatic: Fire has a distinctive complete pattern: it passes the initial point every two years, and 32 such passes make 64 years, one small cycle.

sentences.zh: 小周乘乾策，則太陽大周，為萬三千八百二十四歲，是為熒惑歲數。

translations.literal: Small cycle multiplied by Qian tally gives the Great Yang great cycle: 13,824 years; this is Mars year count.
translations.idiomatic: Multiplying the small cycle by the Qian tally gives the Great Yang major cycle of 13,824 years, the year count for Mars (Yinghuo).

sentences.zh: 見中分十六萬五千八百八十八。

translations.literal: Observed midpoint divisions: 165,888.
translations.idiomatic: Observed midpoint-division value: 165,888.

sentences.zh: 積中二十五，中餘四千一百六十三。

translations.literal: Accumulated midpoints: 25; midpoint remainder: 4,163.
translations.idiomatic: Accumulated midpoint count is 25, with midpoint remainder 4,163.

sentences.zh: 見中法六千四百六十九。

translations.literal: Observed midpoint rule: 6,469.
translations.idiomatic: Observed midpoint-rule value: 6,469.

sentences.zh: 見閏分九萬六千七百六十八。

translations.literal: Observed intercalary divisions: 96,768.
translations.idiomatic: Observed intercalary-division value: 96,768.

sentences.zh: 積月二十六，月餘五萬二千九百五十四。

translations.literal: Accumulated months: 26; month remainder: 52,954.
translations.idiomatic: Accumulated month count is 26, with month remainder 52,954.

sentences.zh: 見月法十二萬二千九百一十一。

translations.literal: Observed month-rule: 122,911.
translations.idiomatic: Observed month-rule value: 122,911.

sentences.zh: 「二千」一作「一千」

translations.literal: "two thousand"; one version reads "one thousand".
translations.idiomatic: Textual variant: "two thousand"; alternate reading "one thousand."

sentences.zh: 見中日法二千九百八十六萬七千三百七十三。

translations.literal: Observed midpoint day-rule: 29,867,373.
translations.idiomatic: Observed midpoint day-rule value: 29,867,373.

sentences.zh: 見月日法九百九十五萬五千七百九十一。

translations.literal: Observed month day-rule: 9,955,791.
translations.idiomatic: Observed month day-rule value: 9,955,791.

sentences.zh: 水經特成，故一歲而及初，六十四及初而小復。

translations.literal: Water by warp pattern is uniquely complete; therefore in one year it reaches the initial point, and after 64 reaches of the initial point there is a small return.
translations.idiomatic: Water has a distinctive complete pattern: it reaches the initial point each year, and after 64 such returns comes a small return cycle.

sentences.zh: 小復乘巛策，則太陰大周，為九千二百一十六歲，是為辰星歲數。

translations.literal: Small return multiplied by chuan tally gives Great Yin great cycle: 9,216 years; this is Mercury year count.
translations.idiomatic: Small return multiplied by the cyclical tally gives the Great Yin major cycle of 9,216 years, the year count for Mercury (Chenxing).

sentences.zh: 見中分十一萬五百九十二。

translations.literal: Observed midpoint divisions: 110,592.
translations.idiomatic: Observed midpoint-division value: 110,592.

sentences.zh: 積中三，中餘三萬二千四百六十九。

translations.literal: Accumulated midpoints: 3; midpoint remainder: 32,469.
translations.idiomatic: Accumulated midpoint count is 3, with midpoint remainder 32,469.

sentences.zh: 見中法二萬九千四十一。

translations.literal: Observed midpoint rule: 29,041.
translations.idiomatic: Observed midpoint-rule value: 29,041.

sentences.zh: 見閏分六萬四千五百一十二。

translations.literal: Observed intercalary divisions: 64,512.
translations.idiomatic: Observed intercalary-division value: 64,512.

sentences.zh: 積月三，月餘五十一萬四百二十三。

translations.literal: Accumulated months: 3; month remainder: 510,423.
translations.idiomatic: Accumulated month count is 3, with month remainder 510,423.

sentences.zh: 見月法五十五萬一千七百七十九。

translations.literal: Observed month-rule: 551,779.
translations.idiomatic: Observed month-rule value: 551,779.

sentences.zh: 晨中分六萬二千二百八。

translations.literal: Morning midpoint divisions: 62,208.
translations.idiomatic: Morning midpoint-division value: 62,208.

sentences.zh: 積中二，中餘四千一百二十六。

translations.literal: Accumulated midpoints: 2; midpoint remainder: 4,126.
translations.idiomatic: Accumulated midpoint count is 2, with midpoint remainder 4,126.

sentences.zh: 夕中分四萬八千三百八十四。

translations.literal: Evening midpoint divisions: 48,384.
translations.idiomatic: Evening midpoint-division value: 48,384.

sentences.zh: 積中一，中餘萬九千三百四十三。

translations.literal: Accumulated midpoints: 1; midpoint remainder: 19,343.
translations.idiomatic: Accumulated midpoint count is 1, with midpoint remainder 19,343.

sentences.zh: 晨閏分三萬六千二百八十八。

translations.literal: Morning intercalary divisions: 36,288.
translations.idiomatic: Morning intercalary-division value: 36,288.

sentences.zh: 積月二，月餘十一萬四千六百八十二。

translations.literal: Accumulated months: 2; month remainder: 114,682.
translations.idiomatic: Accumulated month count is 2, with month remainder 114,682.

sentences.zh: 夕閏分二萬八千二百二十四。

translations.literal: Evening intercalary divisions: 28,224.
translations.idiomatic: Evening intercalary-division value: 28,224.

sentences.zh: 積月一，月餘三十九萬五千七百四十一。

translations.literal: Accumulated months: 1; month remainder: 395,741.
translations.idiomatic: Accumulated month count is 1, with month remainder 395,741.

sentences.zh: 見中日法一億三千四百八萬二千二百九十七。

translations.literal: Observed midpoint day-rule: 134,082,297.
translations.idiomatic: Observed midpoint day-rule value: 134,082,297.

sentences.zh: 見月日法四千四百六十九萬四千九十九。

translations.literal: Observed month day-rule: 44,694,099.
translations.idiomatic: Observed month day-rule value: 44,694,099.

sentences.zh: 合太陰太陽之歲數而中分之，各萬一千五百二十。

translations.literal: Combine the year counts of Great Yin and Great Yang and split at midpoint; each is 11,520.
translations.idiomatic: Combine the year counts of Great Yin and Great Yang, then divide at the midpoint: each side is 11,520.

sentences.zh: 陽施其氣，陰成其物。

translations.literal: Yang applies its qi; yin completes its forms.
translations.idiomatic: Yang emits its qi, and yin brings things to completion.

sentences.zh: 以星行率減歲數，餘則見數也。

translations.literal: Subtract year count by stellar motion rate; the remainder is the visibility count.
translations.idiomatic: Subtract the annual value by the star’s rate of motion; the remainder is the visibility count.

sentences.zh: 東九西七乘歲數，并九七為法，得一，金、水晨夕歲數。

translations.literal: East nine and west seven multiplied by year count, with nine and seven combined as divisor, gives one: morning-evening year counts for metal and water.
translations.idiomatic: Using east-9 and west-7 against the year count, with 9+7 as the divisor, yields one; this gives the morning/evening year counts for Metal and Water.

sentences.zh: 以歲中乘歲數，是為星見中分。

translations.literal: Multiply annual midpoint by year count; this is stellar midpoint visibility division.
translations.idiomatic: Annual midpoint multiplied by year count gives the star’s midpoint-visibility division.

sentences.zh: 星見數，是為見中法。

translations.literal: Stellar visibility count: this is the midpoint visibility rule.
translations.idiomatic: The star-visibility count constitutes the midpoint-visibility rule.

sentences.zh: 以歲閏乘歲數，是為星見閏分。

translations.literal: Multiply annual intercalation by year count; this is stellar intercalary visibility division.
translations.idiomatic: Annual intercalation multiplied by year count gives the star’s intercalary-visibility division.

sentences.zh: 以章歲乘見數，是為見月法。

translations.literal: Multiply zhang-year by visibility count; this is observed month-rule.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang-year value multiplied by the visibility count gives the observed month-rule.

sentences.zh: 以元法乘見數，是為見中日法。

translations.literal: Multiply origin-rule by visibility count; this is observed midpoint day-rule.
translations.idiomatic: Origin-rule multiplied by the visibility count gives the observed midpoint day-rule.

sentences.zh: 以統法乘見數，是為見月日法。

translations.literal: Multiply system-rule by visibility count; this is observed month day-rule.
translations.idiomatic: System-rule multiplied by the visibility count gives the observed month day-rule.

sentences.zh: 五步木，晨始見，去日半次。

translations.literal: For the five-step wood [planet], first seen in the morning, it is half a station from the sun.
translations.idiomatic: For Jupiter in its five-step cycle, first morning appearance occurs at half a lodge from the sun.

sentences.zh: 順，日行十一分度二，百二十一日。

translations.literal: Direct motion: the sun moves 2/11 degree per day, for 121 days.
translations.idiomatic: In direct motion, it advances at 2/11 degree per day for 121 days.

sentences.zh: 始留，二十五日而旋。

translations.literal: First stationing: after 25 days it turns.
translations.idiomatic: It then stations; after 25 days it turns.

sentences.zh: 逆，日行七分度一，八十四日。

translations.literal: Retrograde: the sun moves 1/7 degree per day, for 84 days.
translations.idiomatic: In retrograde phase, motion is 1/7 degree per day for 84 days.

sentences.zh: 復留，二十四日三分而旋。

translations.literal: Second stationing: after 24 and 1/3 days it turns.
translations.idiomatic: It stations again and turns after 24 1/3 days.

sentences.zh: 復順，日行十一分度二，百一十一日有百八十二萬八千三百六十二分而伏。

translations.literal: Returning to direct motion: 2/11 degree per day, for 111 days and 1,828,362 parts, then it becomes hidden.
translations.idiomatic: After resuming direct motion at 2/11 degree per day, it continues for 111 days plus 1,828,362 fractional parts, then disappears from view.

sentences.zh: 凡見三百六十五日有百八十二萬八千三百六十五分，除逆，定行星三十度百六十六萬一千二百八十六分。

translations.literal: In total visible: 365 days and 1,828,365 parts. Subtracting retrograde, the fixed-star motion is 30 degrees and 1,661,286 parts.
translations.idiomatic: Total visibility is 365 days plus 1,828,365 parts; removing retrograde gives a net stellar advance of 30 degrees and 1,661,286 parts.

sentences.zh: 凡見一歲，行一次而後伏。

translations.literal: In all, visible for one year, moving one circuit and then hidden.
translations.idiomatic: Overall, it is visible for one year, completes one circuit, then vanishes.

sentences.zh: 日行不盈十一分度一。

translations.literal: Daily motion is less than 1/11 degree.
translations.idiomatic: Its daily progress is slightly under 1/11 degree.

sentences.zh: 伏三十三日三百三十三萬四千七百三十七分，行星三度百六十七萬三千四百五十一一作「三」分。

translations.literal: Hidden for 33 days and 3,333,737 parts, moving 3 degrees and 1,673,451 parts; one version has "three" for the last part.
translations.idiomatic: The hidden phase lasts 33 days plus 3,333,737 parts, during which it moves 3 degrees and 1,673,451 parts; one textual variant reads the last figure as 3.

sentences.zh: 一見，三百九十八日五百一十六萬三千一百二分，行星三十三度三百三十三萬四千七百三十七分。

translations.literal: One full appearance: 398 days and 5,163,102 parts, moving 33 degrees and 3,333,737 parts.
translations.idiomatic: One full visibility cycle is 398 days plus 5,163,102 parts, with total motion of 33 degrees and 3,333,737 parts.

sentences.zh: 通其率，故曰日行千七百二十八分度之百四十五。

translations.literal: Generalizing its rate, therefore it says: daily motion is 1,728 parts per 145 of a degree.
translations.idiomatic: Reducing to a standard rate, this is stated as 1,728 parts per 145 of a degree per day.

sentences.zh: 金，晨始見，去日半次。

translations.literal: Metal [planet], first seen in morning, half a station from the sun.
translations.idiomatic: Venus first appears in the morning at half a lodge from the sun.

sentences.zh: 逆，日行二分度一，六日。

translations.literal: Retrograde: 1/2 degree per day, for 6 days.
translations.idiomatic: In retrograde, it moves 1/2 degree per day for 6 days.

sentences.zh: 始留，八日而旋。

translations.literal: First station: after 8 days it turns.
translations.idiomatic: It then stations, turning after 8 days.

sentences.zh: 始順，日行四十六分度三十三，四十六日。

translations.literal: First direct: 33/46 degree per day, for 46 days.
translations.idiomatic: In initial direct motion, it moves 33/46 degree per day for 46 days.

sentences.zh: 順，疾，日行一度九十二分度十五，百八十四日而伏。

translations.literal: Direct, fast: 15/92 degree per day, for 184 days, then hidden.
translations.idiomatic: In fast direct motion, it advances 15/92 degree per day for 184 days, then disappears.

sentences.zh: 凡見二百四十四日，除逆，定行星二百四十四度。

translations.literal: Total visible: 244 days. Removing retrograde, net stellar motion is 244 degrees.
translations.idiomatic: Total visibility is 244 days; excluding retrograde, net stellar advance is 244 degrees.

sentences.zh: 伏，日行一度九十二分度三十三有奇。

translations.literal: Hidden phase: 33/92 degree per day and a remainder.
translations.idiomatic: During concealment, motion is about 33/92 degree per day with a fractional remainder.

sentences.zh: 伏八十三日，行星百一十三度四百三十六萬五千二百二十分。

translations.literal: Hidden for 83 days, moving 113 degrees and 4,365,220 parts.
translations.idiomatic: The hidden interval lasts 83 days, with 113 degrees and 4,365,220 parts of motion.

sentences.zh: 凡晨見、伏三百二十七日，行星三百五十七度四百三十六萬五千二百二十分。

translations.literal: Total morning visibility plus hidden: 327 days, with 357 degrees and 4,365,220 parts of motion.
translations.idiomatic: Morning visibility plus hidden phase totals 327 days, with 357 degrees and 4,365,220 parts traversed.

sentences.zh: 夕始見，去日半次。

translations.literal: First evening appearance, half a station from the sun.
translations.idiomatic: It first appears in the evening at half a lodge from the sun.

sentences.zh: 順，日行一度九十二分度十五，百八十一日百七分日四十五。

translations.literal: Direct: 15/92 degree per day, for 181 days and 45/107 day.
translations.idiomatic: In direct motion, it advances 15/92 degree per day for 181 and 45/107 days.

sentences.zh: 順，遲，日行四十六分度三一作「四」十三，四十六日。

translations.literal: Direct, slow: 33 (one version 43) /46 degree per day, for 46 days.
translations.idiomatic: In slow direct motion, it moves 33/46 degree per day (variant: 43/46) for 46 days.

sentences.zh: 始留，七日百七分日六十二分而旋。

translations.literal: First station: after 7 days and 62/107 day, it turns.
translations.idiomatic: It then stations, turning after 7 and 62/107 days.

sentences.zh: 逆，日行二一作「三」分度一，六日而伏。

translations.literal: Retrograde: 1/2 (one version 1/3) degree per day, for 6 days, then hidden.
translations.idiomatic: In retrograde, motion is 1/2 degree per day (variant: 1/3) for 6 days, then it disappears.

sentences.zh: 凡見二百四十一日，除逆，定行星二百四十一度。

translations.literal: Total visible: 241 days. Excluding retrograde, net stellar motion is 241 degrees.
translations.idiomatic: Total visibility is 241 days; excluding retrograde, net stellar advance is 241 degrees.

sentences.zh: 伏，逆，日行八分度七有奇。

translations.literal: When hidden, retrograde, daily motion is 7/8 degree plus a remainder.
translations.idiomatic: In the hidden retrograde phase, motion is 7/8 degree per day with a small remainder.

sentences.zh: 伏十六「一作六十」日百二十九萬五千三百五十二分，行星十四度三百六萬九千八百六十八分。

translations.literal: Hidden for 16 days (one version: 60), with 1,295,352 parts, moving 14 degrees and 3,069,868 parts.
translations.idiomatic: The hidden phase lasts 16 days (variant: 60), plus 1,295,352 parts, with 14 degrees and 3,069,868 parts of motion.

sentences.zh: 一凡夕見伏，二百五十七日百二十九萬五千三百五十二一作「一」分，行星二百二十六度六百九十萬七千四百六十九分。

translations.literal: In all, evening visibility plus hidden: 257 days and 1,295,352 parts (variant reads final part as 1), moving 226 degrees and 6,907,469 parts.
translations.idiomatic: Total evening visibility and concealment is 257 days plus 1,295,352 parts (variant: final figure 1), with 226 degrees and 6,907,469 parts traversed.

sentences.zh: 一復，五百八十四日百二十九萬五千三百五十二分。

translations.literal: One full return: 584 days and 1,295,352 parts.
translations.idiomatic: One full cycle is 584 days plus 1,295,352 parts.

sentences.zh: 行星亦如之，故曰日行一度。

translations.literal: Stellar motion is likewise; therefore it is said daily motion is one degree.
translations.idiomatic: Stellar motion matches this, hence the statement that mean daily motion is one degree.

sentences.zh: 土，晨始見，去日半次。

translations.literal: Earth [planet], first seen in morning, half a station from the sun.
translations.idiomatic: Saturn first appears in the morning at half a lodge from the sun.

sentences.zh: 順，日行十五分度一，八十七日，始留，三十四日而旋。

translations.literal: Direct: 1/15 degree per day for 87 days; first station, turning after 34 days.
translations.idiomatic: In direct motion it advances 1/15 degree per day for 87 days, then stations and turns after 34 days.

sentences.zh: 逆，日行八十一分度五，百一日。

translations.literal: Retrograde: 5/81 degree per day for 101 days.
translations.idiomatic: In retrograde it moves 5/81 degree per day for 101 days.

sentences.zh: 復留，三十三日八十六萬二千四百五十五分而旋。

translations.literal: Second station: turning after 33 days and 862,455 parts.
translations.idiomatic: It stations again, turning after 33 days plus 862,455 parts.

sentences.zh: 復順，日行十五分度一，八十五日而伏。

translations.literal: Returning direct: 1/15 degree per day for 85 days, then hidden.
translations.idiomatic: After resuming direct motion at 1/15 degree per day for 85 days, it disappears.

sentences.zh: 凡見三百四十日八十六萬二千四百五十五分，除逆，定一多「餘」字行星五度四百四十七萬三千九百三十分。

translations.literal: Total visible: 340 days and 862,455 parts; excluding retrograde, net stellar motion is 5 degrees and 4,473,930 parts (one extra "yu" character in a variant).
translations.idiomatic: Total visibility is 340 days plus 862,455 parts; subtracting retrograde gives a net stellar advance of 5 degrees and 4,473,930 parts (with a variant adding one extra character).

sentences.zh: 伏，日行不盈十五分度三。

translations.literal: Hidden phase: daily motion does not reach 3/15 degree.
translations.idiomatic: During concealment, daily motion is slightly under 3/15 degree.

sentences.zh: 百三十七日千七百一十七萬一百七十分，行星七度八百七十三萬六千五百七十分。

translations.literal: 137 days and 17,170,170 parts, moving 7 degrees and 8,736,570 parts.
translations.idiomatic: Over 137 days plus 17,170,170 parts, it moves 7 degrees and 8,736,570 parts.

sentences.zh: 一見，三百七十七日千八百三萬二千六百二十五分，行星十二度千三百二十一萬五百分。

translations.literal: One appearance: 377 days and 18,032,625 parts, moving 12 degrees and 13,210,500 parts.
translations.idiomatic: One visibility cycle totals 377 days plus 18,032,625 parts, with 12 degrees and 13,210,500 parts of motion.

sentences.zh: 通其率，故曰日行四千三百二十分度之百四十五。

translations.literal: Generalizing its rate, therefore it says: daily motion is 4,320 parts per 145 of a degree.
translations.idiomatic: Normalized by rate, this is expressed as 4,320 parts per 145 of a degree per day.

sentences.zh: 火，晨始見，去日半次。

translations.literal: Fire [planet], first seen in morning, half a station from the sun.
translations.idiomatic: Mars first appears in the morning at half a lodge from the sun.

sentences.zh: 順，日行九十二分度五十三，二百七十六日，始留，十日而旋。

translations.literal: Direct: 53/92 degree per day for 276 days; first station, turning after 10 days.
translations.idiomatic: In direct motion it advances 53/92 degree per day for 276 days, then stations and turns after 10 days.

sentences.zh: 逆，日行六十二分度十七，六十二日。

translations.literal: Retrograde: 17/62 degree per day for 62 days.
translations.idiomatic: In retrograde, it moves 17/62 degree per day for 62 days.

sentences.zh: 復留，十日而旋。

translations.literal: Second station: turning after 10 days.
translations.idiomatic: It stations again and turns after 10 days.

sentences.zh: 復順，日行九十二分度五十三，二百七十六日而伏。

translations.literal: Returning direct: 53/92 degree per day for 276 days, then hidden.
translations.idiomatic: After resuming direct motion at 53/92 degree per day for 276 days, it disappears.

sentences.zh: 凡見六百三十四日，除逆，定行星三百一度。

translations.literal: Total visible: 634 days. Excluding retrograde, net stellar motion is 301 degrees.
translations.idiomatic: Total visibility is 634 days; excluding retrograde, net stellar advance is 301 degrees.

sentences.zh: 伏，日行不盈九十二分度七十三分，伏百四十六日千五百六十八萬九千七百分，行星百一十四度八百二十一萬八千五分。

translations.literal: Hidden phase: daily motion is slightly less than 73/92 degree. Hidden for 146 days and 15,689,700 parts, with stellar motion 114 degrees and 8,218,005 parts.
translations.idiomatic: During concealment, daily motion is just under 73/92 degree. The hidden interval lasts 146 days plus 15,689,700 parts, with 114 degrees and 8,218,005 parts of motion.

sentences.zh: 一見，七百八十日千五百六十八萬九千七百分，凡行星四百一十五度八百二十一萬八千五分。

translations.literal: One appearance: 780 days and 15,689,700 parts; total stellar motion 415 degrees and 8,218,005 parts.
translations.idiomatic: One full visibility cycle is 780 days plus 15,689,700 parts, with total stellar motion of 415 degrees and 8,218,005 parts.

sentences.zh: 通其率，故曰日行萬三千八百二十四分度之七千三百五十五。

translations.literal: Generalizing its rate, therefore it says: daily motion is 7,355 of a degree in 13,824 parts.
translations.idiomatic: Normalized by rate, this is expressed as 7,355 degree-parts per 13,824 units per day.

sentences.zh: 水，晨始見，去日半次。

translations.literal: Water [planet], first seen in morning, half a station from the sun.
translations.idiomatic: Mercury first appears in the morning at half a lodge from the sun.

sentences.zh: 逆，日行二度，一日。

translations.literal: Retrograde: 2 degrees per day, for 1 day.
translations.idiomatic: In retrograde, it moves 2 degrees per day for 1 day.

sentences.zh: 始留，二日而旋。

translations.literal: First station: turning after 2 days.
translations.idiomatic: It then stations and turns after 2 days.

sentences.zh: 順，日行七分度六，一多「十」字七日。

translations.literal: Direct: 6/7 degree per day, 17 days (with an extra "ten" character in one witness).
translations.idiomatic: In direct motion, it advances 6/7 degree per day for 17 days (one textual witness has an extra “ten”).

sentences.zh: 順，疾，日行一度三分度一，一多「一」字十八日而伏。

translations.literal: Direct, fast: 1 and 1/3 degree per day (one extra "one" character in one witness), for 18 days then hidden.
translations.idiomatic: In fast direct motion it advances 1 1/3 degrees per day (with one witness adding an extra “one”) for 18 days, then disappears.

sentences.zh: 凡見二十八日，除逆，定行星二十八度。

translations.literal: Total visible: 28 days. Excluding retrograde, net stellar motion is 28 degrees.
translations.idiomatic: Total visibility is 28 days; excluding retrograde, net stellar advance is 28 degrees.

sentences.zh: 伏，日行一度九分度七有奇，三十七日一億二千二百二萬九千六百五分，行星六十八度四千六百六十一萬一百二十八分。

translations.literal: Hidden phase: daily motion is 7/9 degree plus a remainder; 37 days and 122,229,605 parts, with stellar motion 68 degrees and 46,610,128 parts.
translations.idiomatic: In concealment, daily motion is roughly 7/9 degree. Over 37 days plus 122,229,605 parts, stellar motion totals 68 degrees and 46,610,128 parts.

sentences.zh: 凡晨見、伏，六十五日一億二千二百二萬九千六百五分，行星九十六度四千六百六十一萬一百二十八分。

translations.literal: Total morning visibility plus hidden: 65 days and 122,229,605 parts, with stellar motion 96 degrees and 46,610,128 parts.
translations.idiomatic: Morning visibility plus concealment totals 65 days and 122,229,605 parts, with 96 degrees and 46,610,128 parts of motion.

sentences.zh: 夕始見，去日半次。

translations.literal: First evening appearance, half a station from the sun.
translations.idiomatic: It first appears in the evening at half a lodge from the sun.

sentences.zh: 順，疾，日行一度三分度一，十六日二分日一。

translations.literal: Direct, fast: 1 and 1/3 degree per day, 16 days and 1/2 day.
translations.idiomatic: In fast direct motion it advances 1 1/3 degrees per day for 16 1/2 days.

sentences.zh: 順，遲，日行七分度六，七一作「十」日。

translations.literal: Direct, slow: 6/7 degree per day, 7 days (variant: 10 days).
translations.idiomatic: In slow direct motion, it moves 6/7 degree per day for 7 days (variant: 10).

sentences.zh: 留，一日二分日一而旋。

translations.literal: Stationary: 1 and 1/2 days, then turns.
translations.idiomatic: It remains stationary for 1 1/2 days, then turns.

sentences.zh: 逆，日行二度，一日而伏。

translations.literal: Retrograde: 2 degrees per day for 1 day, then hidden.
translations.idiomatic: In retrograde, it moves 2 degrees per day for 1 day, then disappears.

sentences.zh: 凡見二十六日，除逆，定行星二十六度。

translations.literal: Total visible: 26 days. Excluding retrograde, net stellar motion is 26 degrees.
translations.idiomatic: Total visibility is 26 days; excluding retrograde, net stellar advance is 26 degrees.

sentences.zh: 伏，逆，日行十五分度四有奇，二十四日，行星六度五千八百六十六萬二千八百二十分。

translations.literal: Hidden, retrograde: daily motion is 4/15 degree plus a remainder, for 24 days, with stellar motion 6 degrees and 58,662,820 parts.
translations.idiomatic: In hidden retrograde phase, motion is about 4/15 degree per day for 24 days, totaling 6 degrees and 58,662,820 parts.

sentences.zh: 凡夕見伏，五十日，行星十九度七千五百四十一萬九千四百七十七分。

translations.literal: Total evening visibility plus hidden: 50 days, with stellar motion 19 degrees and 75,419,477 parts.
translations.idiomatic: Evening visibility plus concealment totals 50 days, with 19 degrees and 75,419,477 parts of motion.

sentences.zh: 一復，百一十五日一億二千二百二萬九千六百五分。

translations.literal: One full return: 115 days and 122,229,605 parts.
translations.idiomatic: One full cycle is 115 days plus 122,229,605 parts.

sentences.zh: 行星亦如之，故曰日行一度。

translations.literal: Stellar motion is likewise; therefore it is said daily motion is one degree.
translations.idiomatic: Stellar motion follows the same pattern, hence the statement of one degree per day in mean rate.

sentences.zh: 統術推日月元統，置太極上元以來，外所求年，盈元法除之，餘不盈統者，則天統甲子以來年數也。

translations.literal: In system method for deriving solar-lunar origin cycle: set the years sought beyond the Great Ultimate upper-origin, divide any full origin-rule units out; if the remainder does not fill one system-rule, then it is the year count since Heaven system jiazi.
translations.idiomatic: To compute the sun-moon origin cycle, start from Great Ultimate upper-origin and add the target years. Remove complete origin-rule units; if the remainder is less than one system cycle, it gives the elapsed years since Heaven-system jiazi.

sentences.zh: 盈統，除之，餘則地統甲辰以來年數也。

translations.literal: If it fills one system, divide it out; the remainder is the year count since Earth-system jiachen.
translations.idiomatic: If one full system cycle is reached, divide it out; the remainder gives years since Earth-system jiachen.

sentences.zh: 又盈統，除之，餘則人統甲申以來年數也。

translations.literal: If it again fills one system, divide it out; the remainder is the year count since Human-system jiashen.
translations.idiomatic: If another full system cycle is reached, divide again; the remainder gives years since Human-system jiashen.

sentences.zh: 各以其統首日為紀。

translations.literal: Each uses the first day of its system-head as epoch marker.
translations.idiomatic: Each system takes its lead day as the epoch marker.

sentences.zh: 推天正，以章月乘人統歲數，盈章歲得一，名曰積月，不盈者名曰閏餘。

translations.literal: To calculate Heaven-correction: multiply zhang-months by Human-system year count. Each full zhang-year gives one, called accumulated months; what does not fill is called intercalary remainder.
translations.idiomatic: To compute Heaven alignment, multiply the zhang-month value by the Human-system year count. Each complete zhang-year counts as one accumulated month; the remainder is the intercalary remainder.

sentences.zh: 閏餘十二以上，歲有閏。

translations.literal: If intercalary remainder is 12 or more, that year has an intercalary month.
translations.idiomatic: If the intercalary remainder reaches 12 or above, that year contains a leap month.

sentences.zh: 求地正，加積月一；

translations.literal: To seek Earth-correction, add one to accumulated months;
translations.idiomatic: For Earth alignment, add 1 to the accumulated-month count;

sentences.zh: 求人正，加二。

translations.literal: to seek Human-correction, add two.
translations.idiomatic: for Human alignment, add 2.

sentences.zh: 推正月朔，以月法乘積月，盈日法得一，名曰積日，不盈者名曰小餘。

translations.literal: To derive first-month new moon: multiply month-rule by accumulated months. Each full day-rule gives one, called accumulated days; what does not fill is called small remainder.
translations.idiomatic: To compute the first-month new moon, multiply the monthly rule by accumulated months. Each complete day-rule unit gives one accumulated day, and the leftover is the small remainder.

sentences.zh: 小餘三十八以上，其月大。

translations.literal: If small remainder is 38 or above, that month is long.
translations.idiomatic: If the small remainder is 38 or greater, the month is a long month.

sentences.zh: 積日盈六十，除之，不盈者名曰大餘。

translations.literal: If accumulated days fill 60, divide out; what does not fill is called great remainder.
translations.idiomatic: When accumulated days reach 60, reduce by division; the remainder is called the great remainder.

sentences.zh: 數從統首日起，算外，則朔日也。

translations.literal: Count from the system-head day, excluding the starting count; this is the new-moon day.
translations.idiomatic: Count forward from the system-head day, excluding the starting point; the result is the new-moon day.

sentences.zh: 求其次月，加大餘二十九，小餘四十三。

translations.literal: To seek the next month, add 29 to great remainder and 43 to small remainder.
translations.idiomatic: For the following month, add 29 to the great remainder and 43 to the small remainder.

sentences.zh: 小餘盈日法得一，從大餘，數除如法。

translations.literal: If small remainder fills day-rule, carry one to great remainder; divide counts as prescribed.
translations.idiomatic: If the small remainder reaches a full day-rule, carry one to the great remainder, then reduce according to rule.

sentences.zh: 求弦，加大餘七，小餘三十一。

translations.literal: To seek quarter moon, add 7 to great remainder and 31 to small remainder.
translations.idiomatic: To compute the quarter phase, add 7 to the great remainder and 31 to the small remainder.

sentences.zh: 求望，倍弦。

translations.literal: To seek full moon, double the quarter moon value.
translations.idiomatic: To compute full moon, double the quarter-phase value.

sentences.zh: 推閏餘所在，以十二乘閏餘，加十得一。

translations.literal: To locate where intercalary remainder falls: multiply intercalary remainder by 12, then add 10 to get one.
translations.idiomatic: To determine leap-month placement, multiply the intercalary remainder by 12 and apply the +10-to-1 rule.

sentences.zh: 盈章中，數所得，起冬至，算外，則中至終閏盈。

translations.literal: If it fills the zhang-midpoint count, the resulting number, starting from winter solstice and counting outside, marks where midpoint reaches terminal intercalary fullness.
translations.idiomatic: When this reaches the zhang-midpoint value, the resulting count from winter solstice (excluding the starting point) indicates where midpoints run to the terminal leap completion.

sentences.zh: 中氣在朔若二日，則前月閏也。

translations.literal: If the mid-qi falls on the new moon or the second day, then the preceding month is intercalary.
translations.idiomatic: If the principal solar term falls on the new moon day or day two, the previous month is treated as intercalary.

sentences.zh: 推冬至，以算餘乘人統歲數，盈統法得一，名曰大餘，不盈者名曰小餘。

translations.literal: To compute winter solstice: multiply calculation remainder by Human-system year count. Each full system-rule gives one, called great remainder; what does not fill is called small remainder.
translations.idiomatic: To calculate winter solstice, multiply the computation remainder by the Human-system year count. Each complete system-rule unit gives a great remainder; the leftover is the small remainder.

sentences.zh: 除數如法，則所求冬至日也。

translations.literal: Reduce numbers according to rule; the result is the sought winter-solstice day.
translations.idiomatic: Reduce by the prescribed rule, and the result is the target winter-solstice date.

sentences.zh: 求八節，加大餘四十五，小餘千一百。

translations.literal: To get the eight nodes, add 45 to great remainder and 1,100 to small remainder.
translations.idiomatic: For the eight seasonal nodes, add 45 to the great remainder and 1,100 to the small remainder.

sentences.zh: 求二十四氣，三其小餘，加大餘十五，小餘千一十。

translations.literal: To get the twenty-four qi, triple the small remainder, then add 15 to great remainder and 1,010 to small remainder.
translations.idiomatic: For the twenty-four solar terms, triple the small remainder, then add 15 to the great remainder and 1,010 to the small remainder.

sentences.zh: 推中部二十四氣，皆以元為法。

translations.literal: To compute the middle section twenty-four qi, always use origin as the divisor.
translations.idiomatic: For the middle-section twenty-four terms, always use the origin value as divisor.

sentences.zh: 推五行，其四行各七十三日，統歲分之七十七。

translations.literal: To compute five phases: each of the four phases is 73 days and 77/ system-year division.
translations.idiomatic: In the Five-Phase calculation, each of four phases spans 73 days plus 77 system-year parts.

sentences.zh: 中央各十八日，統法分之四百四。

translations.literal: The central phase is each 18 days and 404 system-rule parts.
translations.idiomatic: Each central segment is 18 days plus 404 system-rule parts.

sentences.zh: 冬至後，中央二十七日六百六分。

translations.literal: After winter solstice, the center is 27 days and 606 parts.
translations.idiomatic: After winter solstice, the central interval is 27 days and 606 parts.

sentences.zh: 推合晨所在星，置積日，以統法乘之，以十九乘小餘而并之。

translations.literal: To compute the star of heliacal conjunction at dawn: set accumulated days, multiply by system-rule, multiply small remainder by 19 and combine.
translations.idiomatic: To find the star position at dawn conjunction, start with accumulated days, multiply by the system rule, then add 19 times the small remainder.

sentences.zh: 盈周天，除去之；

translations.literal: If it fills full heaven-cycle, remove it;
translations.idiomatic: If it exceeds the full heavenly cycle, subtract that cycle;

sentences.zh: 不盈者，令盈統法得一度。

translations.literal: if not full, let one full system-rule equal one degree.
translations.idiomatic: if it does not fill, convert by taking one full system-rule as one degree.

sentences.zh: 數起牽牛，算外，則合晨所入星度也。

translations.literal: Count starting from Altair, excluding the start; this gives the entered star-degree at dawn conjunction.
translations.idiomatic: Count from Ox-Herder (Niuniu), excluding the starting point; this yields the star degree entered at dawn conjunction.

sentences.zh: 推其日夜半所在星，以章歲乘月小餘，以減合晨度。

translations.literal: To compute star position at midnight of that day: multiply zhang-year by monthly small remainder and subtract from dawn-conjunction degrees.
translations.idiomatic: To find the star at midnight that day, multiply zhang-year by the month’s small remainder and subtract from the dawn-conjunction degree.

sentences.zh: 小餘不足者，破全度。

translations.literal: If small remainder is insufficient, break one full degree.
translations.idiomatic: If the small remainder is insufficient, borrow from one full degree.

sentences.zh: 推其月夜半所在星，以月周乘月小餘，盈統法得一度，以減合晨度。

translations.literal: To compute star position at midnight of that month: multiply monthly circuit by monthly small remainder; each full system-rule gives one degree, then subtract from dawn-conjunction degree.
translations.idiomatic: For star position at that month’s midnight, multiply monthly circuit by the month’s small remainder; each full system-rule converts to one degree, then subtract from the dawn-conjunction degree.

sentences.zh: 推諸加時，以十二乘小餘為實，各盈分母為法，數起於子，算外，則所加辰也。

translations.literal: To compute all added time divisions: multiply small remainder by twelve as dividend; for each case use full denominator as divisor. Count from zi, excluding the start; this is the added branch-time.
translations.idiomatic: To compute added time offsets, multiply the small remainder by 12 as the dividend, and use each full denominator as divisor. Count from zi, excluding the starting point; the result is the added time branch.

sentences.zh: 推月食，置會餘歲積月，以二十三乘之，盈百三十五，除之。

translations.literal: To compute lunar eclipse: set conjunction-remainder year accumulated months, multiply by 23; if it reaches 135, divide out.
translations.idiomatic: For lunar eclipse calculation, take the conjunction-remainder accumulated months and multiply by 23; whenever it reaches 135, reduce by division.

sentences.zh: 不盈者，加二十三得一月，盈百三十五，數所得，起其正，算外，則食月也。

translations.literal: If it does not fill, add 23 to get one month; when full at 135, the obtained count, starting from the proper month and excluding the start, gives the eclipse month.
translations.idiomatic: If it does not reach full value, add 23 per month; when 135 is reached, count from the proper month (excluding the start) and that gives the eclipse month.

sentences.zh: 加時，在望日衝辰。

translations.literal: Added time is at the opposing branch on full-moon day.
translations.idiomatic: The time offset falls on the branch opposite the full-moon day.

sentences.zh: 紀術推五星見復，置太極上元以來，盡所求年，乘大統見復數，盈歲數得一，則定見復數也。

translations.literal: In chronometric method for deriving five-planet visibility returns: set years from Great Ultimate upper-origin through the target year, multiply by Great System visibility-return number; each full year count gives one, thus fixed visibility-return number.
translations.idiomatic: To compute five-planet visibility returns, count from Great Ultimate upper-origin to the target year and multiply by the Great System return number. Each complete year-unit gives one, yielding the fixed visibility-return count.

sentences.zh: 不盈者名曰見復餘。

translations.literal: What does not fill is called visibility-return remainder.
translations.idiomatic: Any remainder is called the visibility-return remainder.

sentences.zh: 見復餘盈其見復數，一以上見在往年，倍一以上，又在前往年，不盈者在今年也。

translations.literal: If visibility-return remainder fills one visibility-return number or more, the visibility is in a past year; if more than twice one, then in an earlier past year; if not full, then in current year.
translations.idiomatic: If the visibility-return remainder reaches one full return unit, the appearance belongs to a prior year; if it exceeds two units, to an even earlier year; if below one unit, it belongs to the current year.

sentences.zh: 推星所一多「在」字見中次，以見中分乘定見復數，盈見中法得一，則積中法也。

translations.literal: To compute the star’s visibility midpoint station: multiply midpoint divisions by fixed visibility-return number; each full midpoint-rule gives one, yielding accumulated midpoint-rule.
translations.idiomatic: To locate the star’s midpoint visibility station, multiply midpoint divisions by the fixed return count; each full midpoint-rule unit gives one, producing the accumulated midpoint value.

sentences.zh: 不盈者名曰中餘。

translations.literal: What does not fill is called midpoint remainder.
translations.idiomatic: Any remainder is called the midpoint remainder.

sentences.zh: 以元中除積中，餘則中元餘也。

translations.literal: Divide accumulated midpoints by origin midpoint; remainder is origin-midpoint remainder.
translations.idiomatic: Divide accumulated midpoint value by origin-midpoint value; the remainder is the origin-midpoint remainder.

sentences.zh: 以章中除之，餘則入章中數也。

translations.literal: Divide by zhang-midpoint; remainder is the entered zhang-midpoint count.
translations.idiomatic: Divide that by zhang-midpoint value; the remainder is the in-zhang midpoint count.

sentences.zh: 以十二除之，餘則星見中次也。

translations.literal: Divide by twelve; remainder is the star midpoint visibility station.
translations.idiomatic: Divide by 12; the remainder gives the star’s midpoint visibility station.

sentences.zh: 中數從冬至起，次數從星紀起，算外，則星所見中次也。

translations.literal: Midpoint count starts from winter solstice; station count starts from Star Marker. Excluding start in counting gives the star’s midpoint visibility station.
translations.idiomatic: Count midpoint index from winter solstice and station index from Star Marker; excluding the starting point yields the star’s midpoint visibility station.

sentences.zh: 推星見月，以閏分乘定見，以章歲乘中餘從之，盈見月法得一，并積中，則積月也。

translations.literal: To compute star visibility month: multiply intercalary divisions by fixed visibility count; add zhang-year times midpoint remainder; each full visibility-month rule gives one and combined with accumulated midpoints gives accumulated months.
translations.idiomatic: For star-visibility month, multiply intercalary divisions by the fixed visibility count, then add zhang-year times midpoint remainder. Each full visibility-month-rule unit gives one; combining this with accumulated midpoints yields accumulated months.

sentences.zh: 不盈者名曰月中餘。

translations.literal: What does not fill is called month-midpoint remainder.
translations.idiomatic: Any leftover is called the month-midpoint remainder.

sentences.zh: 以元月除積月餘，名曰月元餘。

translations.literal: Divide accumulated-month remainder by origin-month; this is called month-origin remainder.
translations.idiomatic: Divide the accumulated-month remainder by the origin-month value; that gives the month-origin remainder.

sentences.zh: 以章月除月元餘，則入章月數也。

translations.literal: Divide month-origin remainder by zhang-month; this yields the in-zhang month count.
translations.idiomatic: Then divide month-origin remainder by zhang-month to obtain the in-zhang month count.

sentences.zh: 以十二除之，至有閏之歲，除十三入章。

translations.literal: Divide by twelve; in years with intercalation, divide by thirteen into zhang units.
translations.idiomatic: Normally divide by 12; in leap years, divide by 13 for the zhang entry.

sentences.zh: 三歲一閏，六歲二閏，九歲三閏，十一歲四閏，十四歲五閏，十七歲六閏，十九歲七閏。

translations.literal: In three years one leap; six years two leaps; nine years three; eleven years four; fourteen years five; seventeen years six; nineteen years seven.
translations.idiomatic: Leap-month pattern: 1 in 3 years, 2 in 6, 3 in 9, 4 in 11, 5 in 14, 6 in 17, and 7 in 19 years.

sentences.zh: 不盈者數起於天正，算外，則星所見月也。

translations.literal: If not full, count starting from Heaven-correction and exclude the start; this gives the month of stellar visibility.
translations.idiomatic: If not full, count from Heaven alignment (excluding the starting point); that gives the month of stellar visibility.

sentences.zh: 推至日，以中法乘中元餘，盈元法得一，名曰積日，不盈者名曰小餘。

translations.literal: To derive solstice day: multiply middle-rule by origin-midpoint remainder; each full origin-rule gives one, called accumulated days; what does not fill is called small remainder.
translations.idiomatic: To compute solstice day, multiply middle-rule by origin-midpoint remainder. Each complete origin-rule unit gives accumulated days; the leftover is the small remainder.

sentences.zh: 小餘盈二千五百九十七以上，中大。

translations.literal: If small remainder is 2,597 or above, midpoint is long.
translations.idiomatic: If the small remainder is 2,597 or greater, the midpoint interval is long.

sentences.zh: 數除積日如法，算外，則冬至也。

translations.literal: Reduce accumulated days by rule and count excluding start; this is winter solstice.
translations.idiomatic: Reduce accumulated days by rule, count excluding the start, and the result is winter solstice.

sentences.zh: 推朔日，以月法乘月元餘，盈日法得一，名曰積日，餘名曰小餘。

translations.literal: To derive new-moon day: multiply month-rule by month-origin remainder; each full day-rule gives one, called accumulated days; remainder is called small remainder.
translations.idiomatic: To compute the new-moon day, multiply monthly rule by month-origin remainder. Each full day-rule gives accumulated days; what remains is the small remainder.

sentences.zh: 小餘三十八以上，月大。

translations.literal: If small remainder is 38 or above, month is long.
translations.idiomatic: If the small remainder is 38 or above, the month is long.

sentences.zh: 數除積日如法，算外，則星見月朔日也。

translations.literal: Reduce accumulated days by rule and count excluding start; this is the new-moon day of stellar visibility month.
translations.idiomatic: Reduce accumulated days by rule and count excluding the start; this gives the new moon of the star-visibility month.

sentences.zh: 推入中次日度數，以中法乘中餘，以見中法乘其小餘并之，盈見中日法得一，則入中日入次度數也。

translations.literal: To derive day-degree entering midpoint station: multiply middle-rule by midpoint remainder; add visibility-midpoint rule times its small remainder; each full visibility-midpoint day-rule gives one, then this is entering-midpoint day entering-station degree count.
translations.idiomatic: To compute day-degree at entry into midpoint station, multiply middle-rule by midpoint remainder, then add visibility-midpoint-rule times its small remainder. Each full visibility-midpoint day-rule gives one; the result is the entry-day station degree count.

sentences.zh: 中次至日數，次以次初數，算外，則星所見及日所在度數也。

translations.literal: Take midpoint-station day count and station’s initial count, excluding start in counting; this gives both star visibility and solar position degree count.
translations.idiomatic: Using the midpoint-station day count with that station’s initial value, and counting excluding the start, yields both the star-visible and solar position degrees.

sentences.zh: 求夕，在日後十五度。

translations.literal: To seek evening, place it 15 degrees after the sun.
translations.idiomatic: For evening visibility, place the star 15 degrees behind the sun.

sentences.zh: 推入月日數，以月法乘月餘，以見月法乘其小餘并之，盈見月日法得一，則入月日數也。

translations.literal: To compute entered month-day count: multiply month-rule by month remainder, add visibility-month rule times its small remainder; each full visibility-month day-rule gives one, then this is entered month-day count.
translations.idiomatic: To compute the month-day entry count, multiply monthly rule by month remainder and add visibility-month-rule times its small remainder. Each full visibility-month day-rule counts as one; the result is the entry day-count within the month.

sentences.zh: 并之大餘，數除如法，則見日也。

translations.literal: Combine with great remainder, reduce by rule; this gives the visibility day.
translations.idiomatic: Combine that with the great remainder and reduce by rule; the result is the day of visibility.

sentences.zh: 推後見中，加積中於中元餘，加後餘於中餘，盈其法得一，從中元餘，數如法，則見也。

translations.literal: To compute later midpoint visibility: add accumulated midpoint to origin-midpoint remainder; add later remainder to midpoint remainder; each full rule gives one, carry to origin-midpoint remainder, reduce by rule; this gives visibility.
translations.idiomatic: To compute subsequent midpoint visibility, add accumulated midpoint to origin-midpoint remainder and add later remainder to midpoint remainder. Carry each full-rule unit to the origin remainder and reduce by rule; this gives the new visibility point.

sentences.zh: 推後見月，加積月於月元餘，加後月餘於月餘，盈其法得一，從月元餘，除數如法，則後見月也。

translations.literal: To compute later visibility month: add accumulated months to month-origin remainder; add later month remainder to month remainder; each full rule gives one, carry to month-origin remainder, reduce by rule; this gives later visibility month.
translations.idiomatic: For subsequent visibility month, add accumulated months to month-origin remainder and later-month remainder to month remainder. Carry each full-rule unit to month-origin remainder and reduce by rule; the result is the later visibility month.

sentences.zh: 推至日及入中次度數，如上法。

translations.literal: To compute solstice day and entered midpoint-station degree count, use the above method.
translations.idiomatic: Compute solstice day and midpoint-station entry degrees by the same method above.

sentences.zh: 推朔日及入月數，如上法

translations.literal: To compute new-moon day and entered month count, use the above method.
translations.idiomatic: Compute new-moon day and month-entry count by the same method.

sentences.zh: 推晨見加夕，夕見加晨，皆如上法。

translations.literal: For morning visibility adding evening, and evening visibility adding morning, all follow the above method.
translations.idiomatic: Both “morning plus evening” and “evening plus morning” visibility calculations follow the same method above.

sentences.zh: 推五步，置始見以來日數，至所求日，各以其行度數乘之。

translations.literal: To compute five-step motion, set the day count from first visibility to target day, and multiply each by its motion-degree value.
translations.idiomatic: For five-step motion, take days elapsed since first visibility to the target day and multiply by each corresponding motion-degree rate.

sentences.zh: 其星若日有分者，分子乘全為實，分母為法。

translations.literal: If the star or day has a fraction, multiply numerator by whole as dividend, denominator as divisor.
translations.idiomatic: If star or day value has a fraction, use numerator times whole number as dividend and denominator as divisor.

sentences.zh: 其兩有分者，分母分度數乘全，分子從之，令相乘為實，分母相乘為法，實如法得一，名曰積度。

translations.literal: If both have fractions, multiply each denominator and degree value by whole, then follow numerator likewise; make products as dividend and denominators multiplied as divisor. Dividend divided by divisor gives one, called accumulated degrees.
translations.idiomatic: If both terms are fractional, scale degree values by denominators and wholes, then apply numerators; multiply through for the dividend, multiply denominators for the divisor. Division yields the accumulated degrees.

sentences.zh: 數起星初見星宿所在宿度，算外，則星所在宿度也。

translations.literal: Count from the lodge-degree where the star first appeared, excluding the start; this is the star’s current lodge-degree.
translations.idiomatic: Count from the lodge-degree of first appearance, excluding the starting point; this gives the star’s current lodge-degree.

sentences.zh: 歲術推歲所在，置上元以來，外所求年，盈歲數，除去之，不盈者以百四十五乘之，以百四十四為法，如法得一，名曰積次，不盈者名曰次餘。

translations.literal: In year method to derive current year station: set years from upper origin through target year. Remove full year-count units; if not full, multiply by 145 with 144 as divisor. Division gives one, called accumulated stations; remainder is station remainder.
translations.idiomatic: To locate the current year station, count years from upper-origin to the target year. Remove full annual units; for the remainder multiply by 145 and divide by 144. The quotient is accumulated stations, and the leftover is station remainder.

sentences.zh: 積次盈十二，除去之，不盈者名曰定次。

translations.literal: If accumulated stations fill 12, remove it; what does not fill is called fixed station.
translations.idiomatic: If accumulated stations reach 12, reduce by one cycle; the remainder is the fixed station.

sentences.zh: 數從星紀起，算盡之外，則所在次也。

translations.literal: Count from Star Marker; after exhausting count and excluding start, this is the current station.
translations.idiomatic: Count from Star Marker and, excluding the start, the final count gives the current station.

sentences.zh: 欲知太歲，以六十除餘積次，餘不盈者，數從丙子起，算盡之外，則太歲日也。

translations.literal: To know Tai Sui: divide accumulated station remainder by 60; if remainder not full, count from bingzi and excluding start after full count gives Tai Sui day.
translations.idiomatic: To determine Tai Sui, divide station remainder by 60. With the remaining value, count from bingzi; excluding the starting point, the resulting cyclical day is Tai Sui.

sentences.zh: 贏縮。

translations.literal: Expansion and contraction.
translations.idiomatic: This concerns celestial expansion and contraction.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「歲棄其次而旅於明年之次，以害鳥帑，周楚惡之。」

translations.literal: The Tradition says: "When Jupiter abandons its station and drifts into the next year's station, thereby harming birds and granaries, Zhou and Chu regarded it as inauspicious."
translations.idiomatic: As the Tradition says: "If Jupiter leaves its proper station and wanders into the next year's, harming birds and stores, Zhou and Chu took it as an evil sign."

sentences.zh: 五星之盈縮不是過也。

translations.literal: The five planets' expansion and contraction are not errors in excess.
translations.idiomatic: When the five planets expand or contract in their motion, this is not necessarily an overstep.

sentences.zh: 過次者殃大，過舍者災小，不過者亡咎。

translations.literal: Crossing a station brings great misfortune; crossing a lodge brings lesser disaster; not crossing brings no blame.
translations.idiomatic: If a planet overruns a full station, the omen is severe; if it overruns only a lodge, the harm is lighter; if it does not overrun, there is no fault.

sentences.zh: 次度。

translations.literal: Stations and degrees.
translations.idiomatic: The stations and their degree measures.

sentences.zh: 六物者，歲時數日月星辰也。

translations.literal: The six elements are year, season, calculations, sun, moon, and stars.
translations.idiomatic: The six calendrical elements are: years, seasons, reckonings, and the sun, moon, and stars.

sentences.zh: 辰者，日月之會而建所指也。

translations.literal: The "chen" are where sun and moon meet and where the Dipper's handle points.
translations.idiomatic: "Chen" means the junctures of sun and moon, marked by where the Dipper's handle points.

sentences.zh: 九章歲為百七十一歲，而九道小終。

translations.literal: Nine chapter-years equal 171 years, and the nine paths reach a minor completion.
translations.idiomatic: A cycle of nine chapter-years totals 171 years, marking a minor completion of the nine paths.

sentences.zh: 九終千五百三十九歲而大終。

translations.literal: Nine minor completions are 1,539 years and make a major completion.
translations.idiomatic: After nine such minor completions, 1,539 years have passed, producing a major completion.

sentences.zh: 三終而與元終。

translations.literal: After three major completions, it meets the primal completion.
translations.idiomatic: After three major completions, it aligns with the original epoch completion.

sentences.zh: 進退於牽牛之前四度五分。

translations.literal: It advances and retreats 4 du and 5 fen before Ox.
translations.idiomatic: Its forward and backward shift is 4 degrees and 5 parts before the Ox mansion.

sentences.zh: 九會。

translations.literal: Nine convergences.
translations.idiomatic: These are the nine convergences.

sentences.zh: 陽以九終，故日有九道。

translations.literal: Yang ends with nine, therefore the sun has nine paths.
translations.idiomatic: Because yang culminates at nine, the sun has nine paths.

sentences.zh: 陰兼而成之，故月有十九道。

translations.literal: Yin combines to complete it, therefore the moon has nineteen paths.
translations.idiomatic: Yin combines with it to complete the system, so the moon has nineteen paths.

sentences.zh: 陽名成功，故九會而終。

translations.literal: Yang is named successful completion, therefore it ends after nine convergences.
translations.idiomatic: Yang is called accomplished completion; therefore the cycle ends with nine convergences.

sentences.zh: 四營而成易，故四歲中餘一，四章而朔餘一，為篇首，八十一章而終一統。

translations.literal: Four operations complete the Changes, therefore in four years there is one surplus, and in four chapters there is one new-moon surplus; this forms the chapter beginning, and in eighty-one chapters one system is completed.
translations.idiomatic: With four operations the Changes are completed, so every four years there is one excess, and every four chapters there is one new-moon excess. This sets the start of a chapter; after eighty-one chapters, one full system is completed.
