sentences.zh: 癸巳。

translations.literal: Guisi.
translations.idiomatic: Guisi day.

sentences.zh: 庚寅。

translations.literal: Gengyin.
translations.idiomatic: Gengyin day.

sentences.zh: 戊子。

translations.literal: Wuzi.
translations.idiomatic: Wuzi day.

sentences.zh: 丙戌。

translations.literal: Bingxu.
translations.idiomatic: Bingxu day.

sentences.zh: 甲申，孟。

translations.literal: Jiashen, Meng.
translations.idiomatic: Jiashen day, early phase.

sentences.zh: 推章首朔旦冬至日，置大餘三十九，小餘六十一，數除如法，各從其統首起。

translations.literal: To compute the chapter-head new moon and dawn winter solstice day, set greater remainder at 39 and lesser remainder at 61; divide the numbers according to the method, each beginning from the head of its own system.
translations.idiomatic: To calculate the chapter-opening new moon and dawn winter-solstice day, set the greater remainder to 39 and the lesser remainder to 61, divide by the standard rule, and start each from the head of its respective system.

sentences.zh: 求其後章，當加大餘三十九，小餘六十一，各盡其八十一章。

translations.literal: To seek the following chapter, one should add greater remainder 39 and lesser remainder 61, each carried through its full eighty-one chapters.
translations.idiomatic: To obtain the next chapter, add 39 to the greater remainder and 61 to the lesser remainder, running each through the full span of eighty-one chapters.

sentences.zh: 推篇，大餘亦如之，小餘加一。

translations.literal: For computing a pian, the greater remainder is likewise thus; to the lesser remainder add one.
translations.idiomatic: For calculating a pian unit, treat the greater remainder the same way, but add one to the lesser remainder.

sentences.zh: 求周至，加大餘五十九，小餘二十一。

translations.literal: To seek the Zhou culmination, add greater remainder 59 and lesser remainder 21.
translations.idiomatic: To calculate the Zhou terminal point, add 59 to the greater remainder and 21 to the lesser remainder.

sentences.zh: 世經春秋昭公十七年「郯子來朝」，傳曰昭子問少昊氏鳥名何故，對曰：「吾祖也，我知之矣。

translations.literal: The Shijing cites Spring and Autumn, seventeenth year of Duke Zhao, "The viscount of Tan came to court." The commentary says Zhaoxi asked why the Shaohao clan used bird names, and he replied: "It is our ancestor; I know it well."
translations.idiomatic: In the historical canon, under the seventeenth year of Duke Zhao in the Spring and Autumn Annals, it says, "The Lord of Tan came to court." The commentary records that Zhaoxi asked why the Shaohao lineage used bird names, and Tanzi answered, "He is our ancestor; I know this well."

sentences.zh: 昔者，黃帝氏以雲紀，故為雲師而雲名；

translations.literal: In former times, the Yellow Emperor clan kept records by clouds, therefore they made cloud-officials and cloud names;
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity, the Yellow Emperor's house used clouds as its organizing sign, so offices and titles were named for clouds;

sentences.zh: 炎帝氏以火紀，故為火師而火名；

translations.literal: The Flame Emperor clan kept records by fire, therefore they made fire-officials and fire names;
translations.idiomatic: the Flame Emperor's house used fire as its sign, so offices and titles were named for fire;

sentences.zh: 共工氏以水紀，故為水師而水名；

translations.literal: The Gonggong clan kept records by water, therefore they made water-officials and water names;
translations.idiomatic: the Gonggong lineage used water as its sign, so offices and titles were named for water;

sentences.zh: 太昊氏以龍紀，故為龍師而龍名。

translations.literal: The Taihao clan kept records by dragons, therefore they made dragon-officials and dragon names.
translations.idiomatic: the Taihao lineage used dragons as its sign, so offices and titles were named for dragons.

sentences.zh: 我高祖少昊縶之立也，鳳鳥適至，故紀於鳥，為鳥師而鳥名。」

translations.literal: When our high ancestor Shaohao Zhi was established, a phoenix bird happened to arrive; therefore records were made by birds, and they made bird-officials and bird names."
translations.idiomatic: When our fore-ancestor Shaohao Zhi took his place, a phoenix appeared, so the system was marked by birds, with offices and titles named for birds."

sentences.zh: 言郯子據少昊受黃帝，黃帝受炎帝，炎帝受共工，共工受太昊，故先言黃帝，上及太昊。

translations.literal: This says Tanzi traced Shaohao receiving from Yellow Emperor, Yellow Emperor receiving from Flame Emperor, Flame Emperor receiving from Gonggong, Gonggong receiving from Taihao; therefore he first spoke of Yellow Emperor and went upward to Taihao.
translations.idiomatic: Tanzi's argument is that Shaohao inherited from the Yellow Emperor, the Yellow Emperor from the Flame Emperor, the Flame Emperor from Gonggong, and Gonggong from Taihao; so he began with the Yellow Emperor and worked back to Taihao.

sentences.zh: 稽之於易，炮犧、神農、黃帝相繼之世可知。

translations.literal: Examining it against the Changes, the succession of the ages of Paoxi, Shennong, and the Yellow Emperor can be known.
translations.idiomatic: If one checks this against the Book of Changes, the successive eras of Paoxi, Shennong, and the Yellow Emperor become clear.

sentences.zh: 太昊帝易曰：「炮犧氏之王天下也。」

translations.literal: The Taihao chapter in the Changes says: "When the Paoxi clan ruled all under Heaven."
translations.idiomatic: The Taihao section of the Changes says, "When the Paoxi clan ruled the world."

sentences.zh: 言炮犧繼天而王，為百王先，首德始於木，故為帝太昊。

translations.literal: It says Paoxi succeeded Heaven and ruled, being first among the hundred kings; his initial virtue began from wood, therefore he was Emperor Taihao.
translations.idiomatic: This means Paoxi inherited Heaven's mandate and ruled as the first of all later kings; his initiating virtue belonged to Wood, hence the title Emperor Taihao.

sentences.zh: 作罔罟以田漁，取犧牲，故天下號曰炮犧氏。

translations.literal: He made nets and snares for hunting and fishing, and took sacrificial victims; therefore all under Heaven called him Paoxi clan.
translations.idiomatic: He devised nets and fish-traps for hunting and fishing and thereby secured sacrificial offerings, so the world called him the Paoxi lineage.

sentences.zh: 祭典曰：「共工氏伯九域。」

translations.literal: The sacrificial canon says: "The Gonggong clan held hegemonic authority over the nine regions."
translations.idiomatic: The ritual canon states: "The Gonggong house held hegemonic sway over the nine regions."

sentences.zh: 言雖有水德，在火木之間，非其序也。

translations.literal: It says although there was water virtue, it was between fire and wood and not in proper sequence.
translations.idiomatic: That means its Water-phase virtue fell between Fire and Wood and thus did not follow the rightful sequence.

sentences.zh: 任知刑以彊，故伯而不王。

translations.literal: He relied on knowledge and punishments to impose strength, therefore was hegemon and not king.
translations.idiomatic: Because he ruled by coercive intelligence and punishments, he became a hegemon, not a true king.

sentences.zh: 秦以水德，在周、漢木火之間。

translations.literal: Qin, with water virtue, lay between Zhou and Han, which were wood and fire.
translations.idiomatic: Qin's Water virtue likewise stood between Zhou and Han, whose phases were Wood and Fire.

sentences.zh: 周人俣其行序，故易不載。

translations.literal: The Zhou people considered this to violate sequence, therefore the Changes does not record it.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou regarded that as a disruption of proper order, so the Changes does not include it.

sentences.zh: 炎帝易曰：「炮犧氏沒，神農氏作。」

translations.literal: The Flame Emperor chapter in the Changes says: "When Paoxi clan ended, Shennong clan arose."
translations.idiomatic: The Flame Emperor section of the Changes says, "After the Paoxi lineage passed, the Shennong lineage arose."

sentences.zh: 言共工伯而不王，雖有水德，非其序也。

translations.literal: It says Gonggong was hegemon and not king; though having water virtue, it was not in sequence.
translations.idiomatic: It means Gonggong, though possessing Water virtue, was a hegemon rather than king and did not stand in the rightful order.

sentences.zh: 以火承木，故為炎帝。

translations.literal: Using fire to receive wood, therefore he was Flame Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Since Fire succeeded Wood, he was designated the Flame Emperor.

sentences.zh: 教民耕農，故天下號曰神農氏。

translations.literal: He taught the people to plow and farm, therefore all under Heaven called him Shennong clan.
translations.idiomatic: He taught the people tillage and agriculture, and thus the world called him the Shennong lineage.

sentences.zh: 黃帝易曰：「神農氏沒，黃帝氏作。」

translations.literal: The Yellow Emperor chapter in the Changes says: "When Shennong clan ended, Yellow Emperor clan arose."
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Emperor section of the Changes says, "When the Shennong lineage passed, the Yellow Emperor lineage arose."

sentences.zh: 火生土，故為土德。

translations.literal: Fire generates earth, therefore it was earth virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Because Fire generates Earth, his ruling virtue was Earth.

sentences.zh: 與炎帝之後戰於阪泉，遂王天下。

translations.literal: He fought with descendants of Flame Emperor at Banquan, and thereupon ruled all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: He battled the descendants of the Flame Emperor at Banquan and then took kingship over the world.

sentences.zh: 始垂衣裳，有軒冕之服，故天下號曰軒轅氏。

translations.literal: He first let down robes and garments, and had clothing of chariots and crowns; therefore all under Heaven called him Xuanyuan clan.
translations.idiomatic: He instituted formal robes and the attire of canopies and crowns, so people everywhere called him the Xuanyuan lineage.

sentences.zh: 少昊帝考德曰少昊曰清。

translations.literal: The "Examining Virtue" for Emperor Shaohao says: "Shaohao means pure."
translations.idiomatic: In the "Assessment of Virtue," it says of Emperor Shaohao: "Shaohao signifies purity."

sentences.zh: 清者，黃帝之子清陽也，是其子孫名摯立。

translations.literal: "Pure" means Qingyang, son of Yellow Emperor; among his descendants one named Zhi was established.
translations.idiomatic: "Pure" refers to Qingyang, son of the Yellow Emperor; from his descendants, one named Zhi came to the throne.

sentences.zh: 土生金，故為金德，天下號曰金天氏。

translations.literal: Earth generates metal, therefore it was metal virtue; all under Heaven called him Jintian clan.
translations.idiomatic: Because Earth generates Metal, his phase virtue was Metal, and the world called him the Jintian lineage.

sentences.zh: 周俣其樂，故易不載，序於行。

translations.literal: The Zhou regarded his music, therefore the Changes does not record him and only orders him in the sequence.
translations.idiomatic: Because the Zhou classified him by ritual-music tradition, the Changes does not give a separate entry and places him only in sequence.

sentences.zh: 顓頊帝春秋外傳曰，少昊之衰，九黎亂德，顓頊受之，乃命重黎。

translations.literal: The "Outer Tradition" of the Spring and Autumn says for Emperor Zhuanxu: when Shaohao declined and the Nine Li disordered virtue, Zhuanxu received it and then commanded Chong and Li.
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn Outer Commentary says that when Shaohao's line declined and the Nine Li corrupted virtue, Zhuanxu assumed rule and appointed Chong and Li.

sentences.zh: 蒼林昌意之子也。

translations.literal: He was son of Canglin Changyi.
translations.idiomatic: He was the son of Canglin Changyi.

sentences.zh: 金生水，故為水德。

translations.literal: Metal generates water, therefore it was water virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Since Metal generates Water, his ruling virtue was Water.

sentences.zh: 天下號曰高陽氏。

translations.literal: All under Heaven called him Gaoyang clan.
translations.idiomatic: The world called his house the Gaoyang lineage.

sentences.zh: 周俣其樂，故易不載，序於行。

translations.literal: The Zhou regarded his music, therefore the Changes does not record him and only orders him in sequence.
translations.idiomatic: As with others classified by Zhou ritual music, the Changes does not separately record him and places him only in sequence.

sentences.zh: 帝嚳春秋外傳曰，顓頊之所建，帝嚳受之。

translations.literal: For Emperor Ku, the Spring and Autumn Outer Tradition says: what Zhuanxu established, Emperor Ku received.
translations.idiomatic: Regarding Emperor Ku, the Spring and Autumn Outer Commentary says he inherited what Zhuanxu had established.

sentences.zh: 清陽玄囂之孫也。

translations.literal: He was grandson of Qingyang Xuanxiao.
translations.idiomatic: He was a grandson of Qingyang Xuanxiao.

sentences.zh: 水生木，故為木德。

translations.literal: Water generates wood, therefore it was wood virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Because Water generates Wood, his ruling virtue was Wood.

sentences.zh: 天下號曰高辛氏。

translations.literal: All under Heaven called him Gaoxin clan.
translations.idiomatic: People under Heaven called his line the Gaoxin lineage.

sentences.zh: 帝摯繼之，不知世數。

translations.literal: Emperor Zhi succeeded him; number of generations is unknown.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhi succeeded him, but the generational count is unknown.

sentences.zh: 周俣其樂，故易不載。

translations.literal: The Zhou regarded his music, therefore the Changes does not record him.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou treated him under ritual-music tradition, so the Changes does not separately record him.

sentences.zh: 周人禘之。

translations.literal: The Zhou people performed the di-sacrifice to him.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou performed the grand di sacrifice to him.

sentences.zh: 虞帝帝系曰，顓頊生窮蟬，五世而生瞽叟，瞽叟生帝舜，處虞之媯汭，堯嬗以天下。

translations.literal: For Emperor Shun of Yu, the imperial genealogy says: Zhuanxu begot Qiongchan; after five generations was born Gusou; Gusou begot Emperor Shun, who dwelt at Gui-rui of Yu, and Yao ceded the realm to him.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial genealogy states that Zhuanxu begot Qiongchan; five generations later came Gusou, and Gusou fathered Emperor Shun, who lived at Guirui in Yu. Yao then abdicated the world to him.

sentences.zh: 火生土，故為土德。

translations.literal: Fire generates earth, therefore it was earth virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Since Fire generates Earth, his phase virtue was Earth.

sentences.zh: 天下號曰有虞氏。

translations.literal: All under Heaven called him Youyu clan.
translations.idiomatic: The world called his line the Youyu lineage.

sentences.zh: 讓天下於禹，使子商均為諸侯。

translations.literal: He yielded all under Heaven to Yu, and made his son Shangjun a feudal lord.
translations.idiomatic: He ceded the world to Yu and enfeoffed his son Shangjun as a regional lord.

sentences.zh: 即位五十載。

translations.literal: He occupied the throne fifty years.
translations.idiomatic: He reigned for fifty years.

sentences.zh: 伯禹帝系曰，顓頊五世而生鯀，鯀生禹，虞舜嬗以天下。

translations.literal: For Bo Yu, the imperial genealogy says: from Zhuanxu in five generations was born Gun; Gun begot Yu; Shun of Yu ceded all under Heaven to him.
translations.idiomatic: The genealogy for Bo Yu says that five generations after Zhuanxu came Gun, Gun fathered Yu, and Shun of Yu abdicated the world to him.

sentences.zh: 土生金，故為金德。

translations.literal: Earth generates metal, therefore it was metal virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Because Earth generates Metal, his ruling virtue was Metal.

sentences.zh: 天下號曰夏后氏。

translations.literal: All under Heaven called him Xiahou clan.
translations.idiomatic: The world called his house the Xiahou lineage.

sentences.zh: 繼世十七王，四百三十二歲。

translations.literal: Successive generations had seventeen kings, four hundred thirty-two years.
translations.idiomatic: Seventeen kings ruled in succession over 432 years.

sentences.zh: 成湯書經湯誓湯伐夏桀。

translations.literal: For Cheng Tang, the Documents "Tang Oath" says Tang attacked Xia Jie.
translations.idiomatic: For Cheng Tang, the Documents chapter "Tang Oath" records that Tang attacked Xia Jie.

sentences.zh: 金生水，故為水德。

translations.literal: Metal generates water, therefore it was water virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Since Metal generates Water, his phase virtue was Water.

sentences.zh: 天下號曰商，後曰殷。

translations.literal: All under Heaven called it Shang, later called Yin.
translations.idiomatic: The realm called this dynasty Shang, later known as Yin.

sentences.zh: 三統，上元至伐桀之歲，十四萬一千四百八十歲，歲在大火房五度，故傳曰：「大火，閼伯之星也，實紀商人。」

translations.literal: In the three systems, from the upper origin to the year of attacking Jie was 141,480 years; the year was in Dahuo at 5 degrees of Fang. Therefore the tradition says: "Dahuo is the star of Ebo, and truly marks the Shang people."
translations.idiomatic: Under the three-system reckoning, 141,480 years passed from the high origin to the year Jie was attacked; that year lay in Dahuo, at five degrees of Fang. Hence the tradition says: "Dahuo is Ebo's star; it is precisely the marker of the Shang people."

sentences.zh: 後為成湯，方即世崩沒之時，為天子用事十三年矣。

translations.literal: Later, when he became Cheng Tang, at the time he came to end and passed away, he had been acting as Son of Heaven for thirteen years.
translations.idiomatic: When he later became Cheng Tang, by the time of his death he had served in the position of Son of Heaven for thirteen years.

sentences.zh: 商十二月乙丑朔旦冬至，故書序曰：「成湯既沒，太甲元年，使伊尹作伊訓。」

translations.literal: In Shang, twelfth month, yi-chou new moon at dawn winter solstice; therefore the Documents preface says: "After Cheng Tang died, in Taijia first year, he caused Yi Yin to compose Yi Xun."
translations.idiomatic: In Shang reckoning, the winter solstice fell on dawn of the yi-chou new moon in month twelve. Therefore the Documents preface says: "After Cheng Tang died, in Taijia's first year, Yi Yin was made to compose the Yi Xun."

sentences.zh: 伊訓篇曰：「惟太甲元年十有二月乙丑朔，伊尹祀于先王，誕資有牧方明。」

translations.literal: The Yi Xun chapter says: "In Taijia first year, twelfth month, on yi-chou new moon, Yi Yin sacrificed to the former kings; greatly he arranged and aided at Fangming."
translations.idiomatic: The Yi Xun chapter says: "In Taijia year one, on the yi-chou new moon of month twelve, Yi Yin performed sacrifice to the former kings and grandly assisted at the Fangming rite."

sentences.zh: 言雖有成湯、太丁、外丙之服，以冬至越茀祀先王于方明以配上帝，是朔旦冬至之歲也。

translations.literal: It says although there were the reigns of Cheng Tang, Taiding, and Waibing, at winter solstice he crossed and brushed to sacrifice former kings at Fangming to pair with the High Thearch; this was the year of dawn winter-solstice new moon.
translations.idiomatic: This means that although Cheng Tang, Taiding, and Waibing had held rule, the winter-solstice rite still offered to former kings at Fangming in pairing with the High Thearch, and that year was one in which new moon and dawn winter solstice coincided.

sentences.zh: 後九十五歲，商十二月甲申朔旦冬至，亡餘分，是為孟統。

translations.literal: Ninety-five years later, in Shang twelfth month, jia-shen new moon at dawn winter solstice, with no surplus fractions; this was Meng system.
translations.idiomatic: Ninety-five years later, the Shang calendar had a jia-shen new moon at dawn winter solstice in month twelve, with no fractional remainder; this marked the Meng system.

sentences.zh: 自伐桀至武王伐紂，六百二十九歲，故傳曰殷「載祀六百」。

translations.literal: From attacking Jie to King Wu attacking Zhou was six hundred twenty-nine years; therefore the tradition says Yin "carried sacrifices six hundred."
translations.idiomatic: From Tang's attack on Jie to King Wu's attack on Zhou lasted 629 years; hence the tradition says Yin "sustained sacrifice for six hundred years."

sentences.zh: 殷曆曰，當成湯方即世用事十三年，十一月甲子朔旦冬至，終六府首。

translations.literal: The Yin calendar says: when Cheng Tang had just entered service for thirteen years, in eleventh month jia-zi new moon at dawn winter solstice, it ended at the head of sixth fu.
translations.idiomatic: The Yin calendar says that when Cheng Tang had served thirteen years, the eleventh month had a jia-zi new moon at dawn winter solstice, ending at the head of the sixth fu cycle.

sentences.zh: 當周公五年，則為距伐桀四百五十八歲，少百七十一歲，不盈六百二十九。

translations.literal: At Duke of Zhou year five, this was four hundred fifty-eight years from attacking Jie, short by one hundred seventy-one years, not filling six hundred twenty-nine.
translations.idiomatic: By the Duke of Zhou's fifth year, the count from the attack on Jie was 458 years, 171 years short, and thus did not reach 629.

sentences.zh: 又以夏時乙丑為甲子，計其年乃孟統後五章，癸亥朔旦冬至也。

translations.literal: Again taking yi-chou of Xia time as jia-zi, counting its years it was five chapters after Meng system, gui-hai new moon at dawn winter solstice.
translations.idiomatic: If one further treats the Xia-calendar yi-chou as jia-zi, the count falls five chapters after the Meng system, at a gui-hai new moon with dawn winter solstice.

sentences.zh: 以為甲子府首，皆非是。

translations.literal: Taking it as jia-zi fu-head is all not correct.
translations.idiomatic: To treat that as a jia-zi cycle head is entirely incorrect.

sentences.zh: 凡殷世繼嗣三十一王，六百二十九歲。

translations.literal: In all Yin generations of succession there were thirty-one kings and six hundred twenty-nine years.
translations.idiomatic: Altogether, Yin succession comprised thirty-one kings over 629 years.

sentences.zh: 四分，上元至伐桀十三萬二千一百一十三歲，其八十八紀，甲子府首，入伐桀後百二十七歲。

translations.literal: In quarter-division, from upper origin to attacking Jie was 132,113 years; its eighty-eight cycles, jia-zi fu-head, entered one hundred twenty-seven years after attacking Jie.
translations.idiomatic: Under the quarter-division system, 132,113 years elapsed from the high origin to the attack on Jie; its eighty-eight cycles place the jia-zi cycle head 127 years after that attack.

sentences.zh: 春秋曆，周文王四十二年十二月丁丑朔旦冬至，孟統之二會首也。

translations.literal: In Spring and Autumn calendar, in Zhou King Wen forty-second year, twelfth month, ding-chou new moon at dawn winter solstice, this was second convergence head of Meng system.
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn calendar sets the winter solstice at dawn on the ding-chou new moon of month twelve in King Wen of Zhou's forty-second year; this was the second convergence head of the Meng system.

sentences.zh: 後八歲而武王伐紂。

translations.literal: Eight years later King Wu attacked Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Eight years later, King Wu attacked Zhou.

sentences.zh: 武王書經牧誓武王伐商紂。

translations.literal: For King Wu, the Documents "Mu Oath" says King Wu attacked Shang Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: For King Wu, the Documents chapter "Mu Oath" records that King Wu attacked Shang Zhou.

sentences.zh: 水生木，故為木德。

translations.literal: Water generates wood, therefore it was wood virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Since Water generates Wood, his dynastic virtue was Wood.

sentences.zh: 天下號曰周室。

translations.literal: All under Heaven called it Zhou house.
translations.idiomatic: The realm called it the House of Zhou.

sentences.zh: 月二年六月庚午朏，王命作策豐刑。」

translations.literal: In the second year, sixth month, on gengwu crescent moon, the king commanded making a register of Feng punishments."
translations.idiomatic: In year two, in the sixth month on a gengwu crescent day, the king ordered that the punitive statutes at Feng be codified."

sentences.zh: 世家，煬公即位六十年，子幽公宰立。

translations.literal: In the Hereditary House, Duke Yang reigned sixty years, and his son Duke You, named Zai, was established.
translations.idiomatic: The Hereditary House says Duke Yang ruled for sixty years, after which his son Duke You (Zai) took the throne.

sentences.zh: 幽公，世家即位十四年，及微公茀立，泺。

translations.literal: Duke You, in the Hereditary House, reigned fourteen years, and then Duke Wei, named Fu, was established at Luo.
translations.idiomatic: According to the Hereditary House, Duke You reigned fourteen years; then Duke Wei, Fu, was installed, at Luo.

sentences.zh: 微公二十六年正月乙亥朔旦冬至，殷曆以為丙子，距獻公七十六歲。

translations.literal: In Duke Wei's twenty-sixth year, first month, yi-hai new moon at dawn winter solstice; Yin calendar took it as bingzi, seventy-six years from Duke Xian.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Wei's twenty-sixth year, the first month had an yi-hai new moon at dawn winter solstice; the Yin calendar recorded it as bingzi, seventy-six years before Duke Xian.

sentences.zh: 世家，微公即位五十年，子厲公翟立，擢。

translations.literal: In the Hereditary House, Duke Wei reigned fifty years, and his son Duke Li, named Di, was established, succeeding.
translations.idiomatic: The Hereditary House records that Duke Wei ruled fifty years, then his son Duke Li (Di) succeeded.

sentences.zh: 厲公，世家即位三十七年，及獻公具立。

translations.literal: Duke Li, in the Hereditary House, reigned thirty-seven years, then Duke Xian, named Ju, was established.
translations.idiomatic: The Hereditary House says Duke Li reigned thirty-seven years, after which Duke Xian (Ju) took the throne.

sentences.zh: 獻公十五年正月甲寅朔旦冬至，殷曆以為乙卯，距懿公七十六歲。

translations.literal: In Duke Xian's fifteenth year, first month, jiayin new moon at dawn winter solstice; Yin calendar took it as yimao, seventy-six years from Duke Yi.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xian's fifteenth year, the first month had a jiayin new moon at dawn winter solstice; the Yin calendar gave yimao, seventy-six years before Duke Yi.

sentences.zh: 世家，獻公即位五十年，子慎公埶立，撸。

translations.literal: In the Hereditary House, Duke Xian reigned fifty years, and his son Duke Shen, named Zhi, was established.
translations.idiomatic: The Hereditary House states that Duke Xian ruled fifty years, then his son Duke Shen (Zhi) succeeded.

sentences.zh: 慎公，世家即位三十年，及武公敖立。

translations.literal: Duke Shen, in the Hereditary House, reigned thirty years, then Duke Wu, named Ao, was established.
translations.idiomatic: According to the Hereditary House, Duke Shen reigned thirty years and was followed by Duke Wu (Ao).

sentences.zh: 武公，世家即位二年，子懿公被立，戲。

translations.literal: Duke Wu, in the Hereditary House, reigned two years; his son Duke Yi, named Bei, was established.
translations.idiomatic: The Hereditary House records Duke Wu as reigning two years, then his son Duke Yi (Bei) succeeding.

sentences.zh: 懿公九年正月癸巳朔旦冬至，殷曆以為甲午，距惠公七十六歲。

translations.literal: In Duke Yi's ninth year, first month, guisi new moon at dawn winter solstice; Yin calendar took it as jiawu, seventy-six years from Duke Hui.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Yi's ninth year, the first month had a guisi new moon at dawn winter solstice; the Yin calendar marked it as jiawu, seventy-six years before Duke Hui.

sentences.zh: 世家，懿公即位九年，兄子柏御立。

translations.literal: In the Hereditary House, Duke Yi reigned nine years, and his elder brother's son Boyu was established.
translations.idiomatic: The Hereditary House says Duke Yi ruled nine years, then his nephew Boyu was installed.

sentences.zh: 柏御，世家即位十一年，叔父孝公稱立。

translations.literal: Boyu, in the Hereditary House, reigned eleven years; his paternal uncle Duke Xiao, named Cheng, was established.
translations.idiomatic: According to the Hereditary House, Boyu reigned eleven years, then his uncle Duke Xiao (Cheng) came to power.

sentences.zh: 孝公，世家即位二十七年，子惠公皇立。

translations.literal: Duke Xiao, in the Hereditary House, reigned twenty-seven years, and his son Duke Hui, named Huang, was established.
translations.idiomatic: The Hereditary House records Duke Xiao reigning twenty-seven years, followed by his son Duke Hui (Huang).

sentences.zh: 惠公三十八年正月壬申朔旦冬至，殷曆以為癸酉，距釐公七十六歲。

translations.literal: In Duke Hui's thirty-eighth year, first month, renshen new moon at dawn winter solstice; Yin calendar took it as guiyou, seventy-six years from Duke Xi.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Hui's thirty-eighth year, the first month had a renshen new moon at dawn winter solstice; the Yin calendar gave guiyou, seventy-six years before Duke Xi.

sentences.zh: 世家，惠公即位四十六年，子隱公息立。

translations.literal: In the Hereditary House, Duke Hui reigned forty-six years, and his son Duke Yin, named Xi, was established.
translations.idiomatic: The Hereditary House states Duke Hui ruled forty-six years and was succeeded by his son Duke Yin (Xi).

sentences.zh: 凡伯禽至春秋，三百八十六年。

translations.literal: From Bo Qin to the Spring and Autumn era: three hundred eighty-six years in all.
translations.idiomatic: From Bo Qin down to the Spring and Autumn period, the total is 386 years.

sentences.zh: 春秋隱公，春秋即位十一年，及桓公軌立。

translations.literal: Spring and Autumn Duke Yin: in Spring and Autumn he reigned eleven years, then Duke Huan, named Gui, was established.
translations.idiomatic: In the Spring and Autumn record, Duke Yin ruled eleven years, then Duke Huan (Gui) succeeded.

sentences.zh: 此元年上距伐紂四百歲。

translations.literal: This first year is four hundred years above the attack on Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: That first year stood 400 years after the campaign against Zhou.

sentences.zh: 桓公，春秋即位十八年，子莊公同立。

translations.literal: Duke Huan, in Spring and Autumn reigned eighteen years; his son Duke Zhuang, named Tong, was established.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan is recorded as reigning eighteen years, then his son Duke Zhuang (Tong) took the throne.

sentences.zh: 莊公，春秋即位三十二年，子愍公啟方立。

translations.literal: Duke Zhuang, in Spring and Autumn reigned thirty-two years; his son Duke Min, named Qifang, was established.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Zhuang ruled thirty-two years in the annals, followed by his son Duke Min (Qifang).

sentences.zh: 愍公，春秋即位二年，及釐公申立。

translations.literal: Duke Min, in Spring and Autumn reigned two years, then Duke Xi, named Shen, was established.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Min reigned two years, after which Duke Xi (Shen) was installed.

sentences.zh: 釐公五年正月辛亥朔旦冬至，殷曆以為壬子，距成公七十六歲。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi's fifth year, first month, xinhai new moon at dawn winter solstice; Yin calendar took it as renzi, seventy-six years from Duke Cheng.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi's fifth year, a xinhai new moon at dawn winter solstice fell in the first month; the Yin calendar counted it as renzi, seventy-six years before Duke Cheng.

sentences.zh: 是歲距上元十四萬二千五百七十七歲，得孟統五十三章首。

translations.literal: That year was 142,577 years from the upper origin, obtaining the 53rd chapter head of the Meng system.
translations.idiomatic: That year lay 142,577 years from the high origin and corresponded to the fifty-third chapter-head of the Meng system.

sentences.zh: 故傳曰：「五年春王正月辛亥朔，日南至。」

translations.literal: Therefore the Tradition says: "In the fifth year, spring, king's first month, xinhai new moon, the sun reached the southern limit."
translations.idiomatic: Hence the Tradition states: "In year five, spring, first royal month, on a xinhai new moon, the sun reached its southernmost point."

sentences.zh: 「八月甲午，晉侯圍上陽。」

translations.literal: "In the eighth month, on jiawu day, the Marquis of Jin besieged Shangyang."
translations.idiomatic: "In the eighth month, on a jiawu day, the Marquis of Jin laid siege to Shangyang."

sentences.zh: 章謠云：「丙子之辰，龍尾伏辰，袀服振振，取虢之旂。

translations.literal: A chapter chant says: "At the chen of bingzi, Dragon's Tail hides chen; robes neatly arrayed, they take the banner of Guo.
translations.idiomatic: A chapter rhyme says: "At the bingzi time, when Dragon Tail veils the chen, in trim official robes they seize Guo's standard.

sentences.zh: 鶉之賁賁，天策焞焞，火中成軍，虢公其奔。」

translations.literal: The quail-stars are blazing; Heaven's policy burns bright; in mid-Fire the army forms, and the Duke of Guo will flee."
translations.idiomatic: The quail constellations flare; Heaven's mandate burns bright; with Fire at center the host is formed, and the Duke of Guo will run."

sentences.zh: 卜偃曰：「其九月十月之交乎？

translations.literal: Bu Yan said: "Will it be around the turn of ninth and tenth months?
translations.idiomatic: Bu Yan said, "Will it happen around the transition between the ninth and tenth months?

sentences.zh: 丙子旦，日在尾，月在策，鶉火中，必是時也。」

translations.literal: At dawn of bingzi, sun is in Tail, moon in Ce, Quail-Fire is centered; it must be that time."
translations.idiomatic: At bingzi dawn, the sun stands in Tail, the moon in Ce, and Quail Fire is centered - it must be then."

sentences.zh: 冬十二月丙子滅虢。

translations.literal: In winter, twelfth month, on bingzi, Guo was destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: In winter, in the twelfth month on a bingzi day, Guo was extinguished.

sentences.zh: 言曆者以夏時，故周十二月，夏十月也。

translations.literal: Those speaking of the calendar use Xia time; therefore Zhou twelfth month is Xia tenth month.
translations.idiomatic: Calendar discussants are using Xia reckoning, so Zhou's twelfth month corresponds to Xia's tenth month.

sentences.zh: 是歲，歲在大火。

translations.literal: In that year, Jupiter was in Dahuo.
translations.idiomatic: That year, Jupiter stood in Dahuo.

sentences.zh: 故傳曰晉侯使寺人披伐蒲，重耳奔狄。

translations.literal: Therefore the Tradition says the Marquis of Jin sent eunuch Pi to attack Pu, and Chong'er fled to the Di.
translations.idiomatic: So the Tradition says the Marquis of Jin sent the eunuch Pi against Pu, and Chong'er fled to the Di people.

sentences.zh: 董因曰：「君之行，歲在大火。」

translations.literal: Dong Yin said: "When my lord departed, the year-star was in Dahuo."
translations.idiomatic: Dong Yin said, "When you set out, the year-star was in Dahuo."

sentences.zh: 後十二年，釐之十六歲，歲在壽星。

translations.literal: Twelve years later, in Xi's sixteenth year, the year-star was in Shouxing.
translations.idiomatic: Twelve years later, in Duke Xi's sixteenth year, Jupiter was in Shouxing.

sentences.zh: 故傳曰重耳處狄十二年而行，過衛五鹿，乞食於野人，野人舉塊而與之。

translations.literal: Therefore the Tradition says Chong'er stayed among the Di twelve years and then departed, passed Wulu of Wey, begged food from countrymen, and countrymen raised clods and gave them to him.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the Tradition records that Chong'er remained with the Di for twelve years before leaving; passing Wulu in Wey, he begged food from villagers, who handed him a clod of earth.

sentences.zh: 子犯曰：「天賜也，後十二年，必獲此土。

translations.literal: Zifan said: "Heaven gives this; after twelve years, he will surely obtain this land.
translations.idiomatic: Zifan said, "Heaven has granted this omen; after twelve years, he will surely gain this territory.

sentences.zh: 歲復於壽星，必獲諸侯。」

translations.literal: When the year-star returns to Shouxing, he will surely gain the feudal lords."
translations.idiomatic: When Jupiter returns to Shouxing, he will surely win the allegiance of the feudal lords."

sentences.zh: 後八歲，釐之二十四年也，歲在實沈，秦伯納之。

translations.literal: Eight years later, Xi's twenty-fourth year, the year-star was in Shishen, and the Earl of Qin received him.
translations.idiomatic: Eight years later, in Duke Xi's twenty-fourth year, Jupiter was in Shishen, and the Earl of Qin took him in.

sentences.zh: 故傳曰董因云：「君以辰出，而以參入，必獲諸侯。」

translations.literal: Therefore the Tradition says Dong Yin said: "My lord went out with Chen and entered with Shen; he will surely gain the feudal lords."
translations.idiomatic: Thus the Tradition quotes Dong Yin: "You departed under Chen and entered under Shen; you will certainly secure the feudal lords."

sentences.zh: 春秋，釐公即位三十三年，子文公興立。

translations.literal: Spring and Autumn: Duke Xi reigned thirty-three years, and his son Duke Wen, named Xing, was established.
translations.idiomatic: In the Spring and Autumn record, Duke Xi ruled thirty-three years and was succeeded by his son Duke Wen (Xing).

sentences.zh: 文公元年，距辛亥朔旦冬至二十九歲。

translations.literal: Duke Wen first year was twenty-nine years from xinhai new moon at dawn winter solstice.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Wen's first year came twenty-nine years after the xinhai new-moon dawn winter-solstice point.

sentences.zh: 是歲閏餘十三，正小雪，閏當在十一月後，而在三月，故傳曰「非禮也」。

translations.literal: That year had intercalary remainder thirteen; at proper Minor Snow, the intercalary should have been after eleventh month but was in third month; therefore Tradition says "not ritual."
translations.idiomatic: In that year the intercalary remainder was thirteen. The leap month should have followed month eleven at Minor Snow, but it was placed in month three, so the Tradition calls it "contrary to ritual."

sentences.zh: 後五年，閏餘十，是歲亡閏，而置閏。

translations.literal: Five years later, intercalary remainder ten; that year had no leap, yet they placed a leap.
translations.idiomatic: Five years later the intercalary remainder was ten; there should have been no leap month, yet one was inserted.

sentences.zh: 閏，所以正中朔也。

translations.literal: Intercalation is for correcting the central new moon.
translations.idiomatic: The leap month exists to correct the median new-moon cycle.

sentences.zh: 亡閏而置閏，又不告朔，故經曰「閏月不告朔」，言亡此月也。

translations.literal: When there is no leap yet a leap is placed, and again the new moon is not announced, therefore the Canon says "in leap month no announcement of new moon," saying this month was absent.
translations.idiomatic: If a leap month is inserted when none is due, and the new moon is not proclaimed, the Classic therefore says, "In the leap month the new moon was not announced," meaning the proper month was missing.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「不告朔，非禮也。」

translations.literal: The Tradition says: "Not announcing the new moon is not ritual."
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says, "Failing to announce the new moon violates ritual."

sentences.zh: 春秋，文公即位十八年，子宣公倭立。

translations.literal: Spring and Autumn: Duke Wen reigned eighteen years; his son Duke Xuan, named Wo, was established.
translations.idiomatic: In the annals, Duke Wen ruled eighteen years and was succeeded by his son Duke Xuan (Wo).

sentences.zh: 宣公，春秋即位十八年，子成公黑肱立。

translations.literal: Duke Xuan, in Spring and Autumn reigned eighteen years; his son Duke Cheng, named Heigong, was established.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Xuan is recorded as reigning eighteen years, then his son Duke Cheng (Heigong) took the throne.

sentences.zh: 成公十二年正月庚寅朔旦冬至，殷曆以為辛卯，距定公七年七十六歲。

translations.literal: In Duke Cheng's twelfth year, first month, gengyin new moon at dawn winter solstice; Yin calendar took it as xinmao, seventy-six years from Duke Ding year seven.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Cheng's twelfth year, the first month had a gengyin new moon at dawn winter solstice; the Yin calendar rendered xinmao, seventy-six years before Duke Ding's seventh year.

sentences.zh: 春秋，定公即位十五年，子哀公將立。

translations.literal: Spring and Autumn: Duke Ding reigned fifteen years; his son Duke Ai, named Jiang, was established.
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn record says Duke Ding ruled fifteen years, then his son Duke Ai (Jiang) succeeded.

sentences.zh: 哀公十二年冬十二月流火，非建戌之月也。

translations.literal: In Duke Ai's twelfth year, winter twelfth month, flowing-fire; this was not a jianxu month.
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ai's twelfth year, in winter month twelve, "flowing fire" appeared, which was not a month of the jianxu position.

sentences.zh: 是月也螽，故傳曰：「火伏而後蟄者畢，今火猶西流，司曆過也。」

translations.literal: In that month there were locusts; therefore Tradition says: "Only after Fire is hidden do hibernating insects all settle. Now Fire is still flowing westward; this is an error of the calendar officials."
translations.idiomatic: That same month saw swarms of locusts, so the Tradition says: "Only after Fire has sunk do the hibernating insects fully settle. Now Fire is still moving west - the calendrical office has erred."

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰：「七月流火。」

translations.literal: The Odes says: "In the seventh month, Fire flows."
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says, "In the seventh month, Fire drifts west."

sentences.zh: 春秋，哀公即位二十七年。

translations.literal: Spring and Autumn: Duke Ai reigned twenty-seven years.
translations.idiomatic: In the Spring and Autumn accounting, Duke Ai reigned twenty-seven years.

sentences.zh: 自春秋盡哀十四年，凡二百四十二年。

translations.literal: From the beginning of Spring and Autumn to Duke Ai year fourteen, in all two hundred forty-two years.
translations.idiomatic: From the start of the Spring and Autumn era to Duke Ai year fourteen totals 242 years.

sentences.zh: 惠帝，著紀即位七年。

translations.literal: Emperor Hui: in the Annals, reigned seven years.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Hui is recorded in the annals as reigning seven years.

sentences.zh: 高帝，著紀即位八年。

translations.literal: Emperor Gao: in the Annals, reigned eight years.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gao is recorded as reigning eight years.

sentences.zh: 文帝，前十六年，後七年，著紀即位二十三年。

translations.literal: Emperor Wen: former sixteen years, latter seven years; in the Annals, reigned twenty-three years.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen had sixteen earlier years and seven later years; his total reign in the annals is twenty-three years.

sentences.zh: 景帝，前七年，中六年，後三年，著紀即位十六年。

translations.literal: Emperor Jing: former seven years, middle six years, latter three years; in the Annals, reigned sixteen years.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing's reign is divided into former seven, middle six, and latter three years - sixteen years in all.

sentences.zh: 武帝建元、元光、元朔各六年。

translations.literal: Under Emperor Wu, Jianyuan, Yuanguang, and Yuanshuo were each six years.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wu, the Jianyuan, Yuanguang, and Yuanshuo eras each lasted six years.

sentences.zh: 元朔六年十一月甲申朔旦冬至，殷曆以為乙酉，距初元七十六歲。

translations.literal: In Yuanshuo sixth year, eleventh month, jiashen new moon at dawn winter solstice; Yin calendar took it as yiyou, seventy-six years from Chuyuan.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshuo year six, month eleven had a jiashen new moon at dawn winter solstice; the Yin calendar counted it as yiyou, seventy-six years before Chuyuan.

sentences.zh: 元狩、元鼎、元封各六年。

translations.literal: Yuanshou, Yuanding, and Yuanfeng were each six years.
translations.idiomatic: The Yuanshou, Yuanding, and Yuanfeng eras each spanned six years.

sentences.zh: 漢曆太初元年，距上元十四萬三千一百二十七歲。

translations.literal: In Han calendar Taichu first year, it was 143,127 years from upper origin.
translations.idiomatic: Han Taichu year one fell 143,127 years after the high origin.

sentences.zh: 前十一月甲子朔旦冬至，歲在星紀婺女六度，故漢志曰歲名困敦，正月歲星出婺女。

translations.literal: In previous eleventh month, jiazi new moon at dawn winter solstice, year-star was at Xingji, Wunu six degrees; therefore Han Treatise says year-name Kundun, in first month Jupiter appeared in Wunu.
translations.idiomatic: In the preceding eleventh month, a jiazi new moon coincided with dawn winter solstice, with Jupiter at Xingji, six degrees of Wunu. Hence the Han Treatise names the year Kundun and says Jupiter emerged in Wunu in month one.

sentences.zh: 太初、天漢、太始、征和各四年，後二年，著紀即位五十四年。

translations.literal: Taichu, Tianhan, Taishi, and Zhenghe each were four years; plus latter two years, in the Annals he reigned fifty-four years.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu, Tianhan, Taishi, and Zhenghe each lasted four years; adding the final two years gives a fifty-four-year reign in the annals.

sentences.zh: 昭帝始元、元鳳各六年，元平一年，著紀即位十三年。

translations.literal: Under Emperor Zhao, Shiyuan and Yuanfeng each six years, Yuanping one year; in the Annals reigned thirteen years.
translations.idiomatic: For Emperor Zhao, Shiyuan and Yuanfeng were six years each, with one year of Yuanping, totaling thirteen years in the annals.

sentences.zh: 宣帝本始、地節、元康、神爵、五鳳、甘露各四年，黃龍一年，著紀即位二十五年。

translations.literal: Under Emperor Xuan, Benshi, Dijie, Yuankang, Shenjue, Wufeng, and Ganlu each four years, Huanglong one year; in the Annals reigned twenty-five years.
translations.idiomatic: For Emperor Xuan, Benshi, Dijie, Yuankang, Shenjue, Wufeng, and Ganlu ran four years each, with one year of Huanglong, for a twenty-five-year reign.

sentences.zh: 元帝初元二年十一月癸亥朔旦冬至，殷曆以為甲子，以為紀首。

translations.literal: In Emperor Yuan Chuyuan second year, eleventh month, guihai new moon at dawn winter solstice; Yin calendar took it as jiazi and treated it as cycle head.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Yuan's Chuyuan year two, month eleven had a guihai new moon at dawn winter solstice; the Yin calendar mapped it to jiazi and took it as a cycle start.

sentences.zh: 是歲也，十月日食，非合辰之會，不得為紀首。

translations.literal: In that year, there was a solar eclipse in tenth month; it was not a conjunction meeting and could not be cycle head.
translations.idiomatic: Yet that year had a tenth-month solar eclipse, not at the proper conjunction, so it could not serve as a cycle head.

sentences.zh: 距建武七十六歲。

translations.literal: It was seventy-six years from Jianwu.
translations.idiomatic: This stood seventy-six years from Jianwu.

sentences.zh: 初元、永光、建昭各五年，竟寧一年，著紀即位十六年。

translations.literal: Chuyuan, Yongguang, and Jianzhao each five years, Jingning one year; in the Annals reigned sixteen years.
translations.idiomatic: Chuyuan, Yongguang, and Jianzhao each lasted five years, plus one year of Jingning, for sixteen years in total.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始、河平、陽朔、鴻嘉、永始、元延各四年，綏和二年，著紀即位二十六年。

translations.literal: Under Emperor Cheng, Jianshi, Heping, Yangshuo, Hongjia, Yongshi, and Yuanyan each four years, Suihe two years; in the Annals reigned twenty-six years.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Cheng, Jianshi, Heping, Yangshuo, Hongjia, Yongshi, and Yuanyan were each four years, with two years of Suihe, making twenty-six years.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平四年，元壽二年，著紀即位六年。

translations.literal: Under Emperor Ai, Jianping four years, Yuanshou two years; in the Annals reigned six years.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ai's reign comprised four years of Jianping and two of Yuanshou, totaling six years.

sentences.zh: 平帝，著紀即位元始五年，以宣帝玄孫嬰為嗣，謂之孺子。

translations.literal: Emperor Ping, in the Annals reigned Yuanshi five years; made Ying, great-great-grandson of Emperor Xuan, successor, called the Ruzi.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ping is recorded as reigning five Yuanshi years; he designated Ying, a great-great-grandson of Emperor Xuan, as heir, known as the Child Heir.

sentences.zh: 孺子，著紀新都侯王莽居攝三年，王莽居攝，盜襲帝位，竊號曰新室。

translations.literal: Ruzi: in the Annals, Marquis of Xindu Wang Mang acted as regent three years; Wang Mang regented, stole and seized imperial position, usurping title and calling it the New House.
translations.idiomatic: For the Child Heir period, the annals record three years of regency by Wang Mang, Marquis of Xindu. Wang Mang then usurped the imperial throne and proclaimed the "New" dynasty.

sentences.zh: 始建國五年，天鳳六年，地皇三年，著紀盜位十四年。

translations.literal: Shijianguo five years, Tianfeng six years, Dihuang three years; in the Annals, usurped position fourteen years.
translations.idiomatic: The eras Shijianguo (5), Tianfeng (6), and Dihuang (3) together make fourteen years of usurpation in the annals.

sentences.zh: 更始帝，著紀以漢宗室滅王莽，即位二年。

translations.literal: Emperor Gengshi: in the Annals, as Han imperial clansman destroyed Wang Mang, and reigned two years.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gengshi is recorded as a Han clansman who overthrew Wang Mang and reigned two years.

sentences.zh: 赤眉賊立宗室劉盆子，滅更始帝。

translations.literal: The Red Eyebrow rebels established imperial clansman Liu Penzi and destroyed Emperor Gengshi.
translations.idiomatic: The Red Eyebrow rebels installed Liu Penzi of the imperial clan and brought down Emperor Gengshi.

sentences.zh: 自漢元年訖更始二年，凡二百三十歲。

translations.literal: From Han first year to end of Gengshi second year, in all two hundred thirty years.
translations.idiomatic: From Han year one through Gengshi year two, the total span is 230 years.

sentences.zh: 光武皇帝，著紀以景帝後高祖九世孫受命中興復漢，改元曰建武，歲在鶉尾之張度。

translations.literal: Emperor Guangwu, in the Annals: as ninth-generation grandson of Gaozu from Emperor Jing line, he received mandate, revitalized and restored Han, changed era to Jianwu, with year-star in Zhang degree of Chunwei.
translations.idiomatic: The annals state that Emperor Guangwu, a ninth-generation descendant of Gaozu through Emperor Jing's branch, received Heaven's mandate, revived Han, and proclaimed the Jianwu era, with Jupiter in the Zhang degree of Chunwei.

sentences.zh: 建武三十一年，中元二年，即位三十三年。

translations.literal: Jianwu thirty-one years, Zhongyuan two years, reigned thirty-three years.
translations.idiomatic: He ruled for thirty-three years in total: thirty-one of Jianwu and two of Zhongyuan.
